text:
&T modern Thai grill -The restaurant is beautifully decorated with a nice atmosphere. It seems they put a lot of effort into it and the space is well-organized. Adding a smoke extractor above the stove would make dining more comfortable.
-We ordered several dishes  and the prices weren't expensive. Overall  the taste was just average. The meats  especially the beef and lamb  weren't cut very well  and the marinades and sauces could be improved significantly.
-The beef fried rice with pickled egg was good; the pickled egg was delicious  but the rice itself was just okay. It felt a bit similar to Pepper Lunch. Selling the pickled egg separately as a dip would probably be popular.
-The fried pork cheek was rather average  a little salty and fatty. The Tom Yum Goong (spicy shrimp soup) had plenty of ingredients  and the broth was thick and flavorful (I thought it would be easier to eat if it were milder). However  the shrimp was slightly mushy.
-The service was average. The staff didn't check on the tables very often. The food didn't take long to arrive  but an order was missed (and we had to follow up)  causing an extra half hour's wait.
-Overall  I think the restaurant has a good foundation  both in terms of decor and menu concept. If they improve the taste and service a little  it could be much better. ^__^ &T modern Thai grill Great restaurant  great service  delicious food.
🔥 Experience grilled skewers from a different perspective.
AndT Modern Thai Grill — a newly opened restaurant in the Samyan area.
Specializing in lamb & rotating skewers  aiming to make Thai people “open their minds and try” it. &T modern Thai grill  &T modern Thai grill 📌 AndT Modern Thai Grill  Samyan (Rotating Skewer BBQ)

If you're talking about the hottest BBQ restaurant in the Samyan-Banthad Thong area  AndT (แอนด์ที) is definitely on the list. This restaurant breaks the mold of traditional skewer BBQ  transforming it into a cool and fun dining experience.
Their grill rotates 360 degrees  ensuring the meat cooks evenly and gets a beautiful char without you having to manually flip it. It also significantly reduces smoke while you eat. The ingredients on the skewers are elevated with special marinades  and there's a wide variety of dipping sauces to choose from  ranging from spicy Jaew to fusion-style sauces. Overall  the taste is excellent. Typical prices for this area include a 10% service charge and 7% VAT.

Location 📌 1491 Soi Chulalongkorn 11  Wang Mai Subdistrict  Pathum Wan District  Bangkok 10330
Hours ⏰ 11:30 AM - 10:00 PM
Price per person 💵 200++ THB/person
Parking 🚗 Parking available &T modern Thai grill The skewers menu has a moderate selection  but there are many other diverse dishes. The quality of each skewer is excellent.
+ Tools such as heat-resistant pads or forks are provided for removing food from the skewers.
+ There are three types of dipping sauces: chili powder and salt  mala  and jaew.
+ The pomelo salad and pani puri are delicious; you can choose how many pieces you want.
The citrus granita is sweet and refreshing  with a unique citrus flavor and a hint of bitterness.
+ The stir-fried morning glory has slightly tough stems  but the flavor is good.

28Ventotto The Italian food here is authentically Italian  but the prices are quite high. The coffee is good  with a fragrant aroma and smooth taste. The restaurant is air-conditioned and there is parking available. 28Ventotto Since it's a restaurant not far from home  and we've seen it many times  we finally had the chance to take our family there.
We made a reservation for dinner with our family on the 2nd of this month. There were over 10 of us  and we were given two separate tables  which the staff explained was to make it easier to move around  which was fine with us.

So  one table was for the adults  and the other was for the grandchildren. Everyone ordered their own dishes.
We ordered many things  including salad  spaghetti with dried chilies and carbonara  nachos  spare ribs  baked vegetables with cheese  halibut  etc.
The grandchildren also ordered pizza and several other items. After ordering  the dishes started to come out gradually.

At first  it was strange that the staff started serving the grandchildren's table first  even though the adults' table had finished ordering some time ago. But since we're all family  we didn't mind.

Overall  the service and food coming out of the kitchen were steadily increasing  but some dishes were served slowly. Our two tables exchanged glances several times  asking how many items were missing  and the elderly members of our group started getting tired and sleepy  becoming irritable while waiting.
We waited for quite a while... Finally  a staff member came out and informed us that the fish & chips couldn't be served because the fryer was malfunctioning  and offered to serve a salmon dish instead  free of charge. Everyone looked at each other in confusion and nodded in acknowledgment.
At that point  the fish dish we had ordered a long time ago still hadn't been served  so we asked how long it would take. The restaurant said that all the dishes would be ready within 10 minutes  so we decided to wait.

During this time  a female owner  who we weren't sure was also the chef  came out and politely apologized to both tables.

A little later  a staff member went to another table  informed them about the missing dish  and said they would serve salmon instead of fish & chips  along with an extra pizza for each table as an additional apology.

P.S. When we arrived  we saw a large table seated together already there  and other tables were gradually filling up the restaurant. We're not sure why the food was stuck and taking so long to come out that evening.

Personally  the sincerity shown by the owner and staff in explaining  apologizing  and taking responsibility showed genuine intent. We don't think any other restaurant would accept and choose to take action as sincerely as they did that day. We also think there must have been some kind of problem in the kitchen causing this issue with the food service line  which we can understand. And they didn't hold a grudge in the end.
They just tried to entertain the elderly person  make them understand  and get them home first.

Regarding the taste of the food  I personally think the restaurant put a lot of effort into it and did a good job with many dishes. I liked many of the dishes. If I have the opportunity  I might stop by again. 28Ventotto The staff were lovely  helpful  and very polite. 28Ventotto Truly delicious. Great food and coffee all in one place. 28Ventotto The food is delicious  perfect for a family meal. The restaurant is beautiful with many photo opportunities.

restaurant name: 4K Café Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel
text:
4K Café Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel  4K Café Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel  4K Café Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel 😍 Hello and welcome  everyone! 📅 Happy December! The month of happiness! 🎊 The month of parties  smiles  and fun! 😂 And a variety of chaotic year-end activities!

🎊 Today  we have a mini-party from Wongnai to review. I just joined the 2019 New Year's Eve party  and this is my first year! 📅 The event was held this past Saturday  December 21  2019.

🚶 The restaurant we visited today is X2 Cafe (Fork Vibe) 🏨 in the X2 Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit hotel  located behind Lotus On Nut.

🏨 X2 Vibe Bangkok is a newly opened hotel in 2017. 😍 Their slogan is "Stay  Chill  Enjoy" 😂

❄ The decor: The restaurant is decorated in a mostly white and turquoise theme. With a visually pleasing concept 👀 and inviting seating  there's both a bar area and indoor and outdoor seating.

📖 Menu :: We offer Thai  European  and fusion dishes combining both Thai and European cuisines for you to try.

🚗 Access :: Accessible via both Sukhumvit Soi 52 (BTS Exit 4 🚄) and Sukhumvit Soi 50 (a 5-minute walk down the alley).🚶

🚗 Parking :: Parking available within the hotel.

⛳ Location :: Located on the ground floor of the X2 Vibe Bangkok Hotel

🕒 Opening Hours :: 6:30 AM to 12:00 AM

📅 Schedule
6:00 PM to 6:30 PM :: Registration
6:30 PM to 7:30 PM :: Buffet
7:30 PM to 8:30 PM :: Opening ceremony  lucky draw  and photo ops.

🚶 Upon entering the restaurant  we were greeted warmly by the staff. We then proceeded to the registration table. We were given glasses  eye masks  and small  cute  circular plates. I'm not sure what they're called  but we each received one set. The glasses we received ☕ had our names written on the side  the usernames we used to register. It was so pretty and adorable! I loved it!

😋 Let's take a look at the food:: The food was neatly prepared. It was a line buffet where everyone could refill as much as they wanted. There was a wide selection of food  including cocktails  wine  and soft drinks.

What's on the Line Buffet menu?
** Appetizers:: Potato chips  peanuts  peas  and soybean halves served in beautiful wine glasses. Enjoyable snacks.**** Mushroom soup with truffle oil served with crispy bread for 150 baht:: I can tell you that this wasn't as rich as I thought it would be  because it came with crispy bread 🍞  which went really well together. The mushroom soup was rich and the bread was crispy and cut into cute cubes.
** Grilled Chicken :: Grilled chicken served with jaew sauce. 🐔 The chicken was fragrant and delicious  the meat was tender  and the jaew sauce was good  not too spicy. 😋 The flavor was good.
** Stir-fried Curry Powder :: This dish was also delicious. The flavor was rich with curry powder. 😋 It was fragrant and sweet.
** Kale and Crispy Pork :: The kale was very fresh. 👌 Stir-fried to perfection. 🐷 The crispy pork was very crispy and tender. ♨ It was delicious when eaten with hot rice.
** Salmon Spicy Salad :: This dish we ate twice. The flavor was very spicy. 😋 There was a little wasabi sprinkled on the edges. 🐠 The salmon was fresh and very sweet. I really liked this dish.
** Four Cheese Pizza :: Baked fresh 🍕 The crust was very crispy  the cheese wasn't too gooey. 😋 The flavor was rich and delicious. I enjoyed several pieces.
** Crispy Chicken and Lemongrass Salad :: This dish is for those who love the aroma of lemongrass. The crispy chicken is delicious and flavorful  not too spicy. It's another perfect snack.
** Buffalo Wings & Gorgonzola Sauce :: Tender and delicious chicken 🐔 topped with a special sauce that's a bit sour but not too spicy. The sauce is incredibly juicy and generous.

Desserts 😋
** Lod Chong in Coconut Milk :: Lod Chong is served in a wine glass decorated with beautiful banana leaves and ice to scoop into. I thought the Lod Chong was a bit too firm 😋  not soft  but the coconut milk was good 👌  quite tasty.
** Pudding :: The pudding is incredibly dense and smooth. It's great with sliced ​​fruit 😍
** Fruit Tart :: The tart itself wasn't impressive  but the overall presentation is beautiful 😋 and very appetizing. The fruit is fresh and clean.

Drinks ♨
** Cocktails :: We have a full menu.
** Wine :: White and red wine 🍷 We also have a full menu.
** Soft Drinks :: Water  soft drinks (Coke  Orange Juice  Fanta). For those who don't drink alcohol 4K Café Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel This is the breakfast area for Cross Vibe Bangkok hotel. The equivalent of $15 for all you can graze. The food is not that fantastic. This is not worth it for an outsider. 4K Café Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel

501 Home Cook Delicious! Looking forward to the next new dish. 501 Home Cook Very nice thai kitchen  recommend 501 Home Cook Hidden gems in Bantadtong
When I say hidden  I mean really hidden—I had a hard time finding the restaurant at first. Get your google map ready!

I was so impressed to learn that the chef started as a passionate home cook before opening this place.

They’re only open on Saturdays and Sundays  and reservations are required.

Creamy mushroom soup with espuma foam & garlic bread – Absolutely delicious  rich yet airy. A great start with a warm welcome.

Summer salad – Light  fresh  and cooling. Watermelon was perfectly match for Bangkok's hot day.

Sashimi with ponzu sauce – I wished for more acidity and brightness here. Personally  I’d love a sharper punch—maybe passionfruit or mango would elevate it. Still  not a bad dish at all.

Duck breast (instead of beef tongue) – Since my group doesn’t eat beef  the chef kindly swapped in duck. Generous portions  with perfectly crispy skin  rendered fat  and tender meat. A highlight!

Garden salad – Simple and refreshing.

Crispy pork belly – Wow. One of the best I’ve ever had! The grill chili dip (Nam Prik Noom) was an amazing pairing  and the Chinese-style sauce with coriander and sesame oil worked beautifully too.

Pan-fried salmon – Great sear  still super moist inside. Just a touch more salt would have balanced it better. The spicy avocado and creamy mustard sauces complemented it nicely.

Japanese cold noodle – A surprisingly light and satisfying way to end the savory courses.

Watermelon & strawberry granita with whipped cream cheese – great dish. You can ask for refill!

Overall  a true hidden gems! Really enjoy the night here. Highly recommended! 501 Home Cook Great moment with great vibe and awesome meal served in course. Dry aged beef steak  and Crispy pork are must. Small party is suitable. 501 Home Cook Delicious! Pim and her husband were incredibly gracious hosts serving an incredible meal that was deliciously surprising each coarse. Eat all of it of course  but yes  the Beef Tongue (my first)  Crispy Pork Belly  Dry Aged Beef Steak  and Granita were fantastic.

restaurant name: ALATi at Siam Kempinski Hotel Bangkok
text:
ALATi at Siam Kempinski Hotel Bangkok I ordered a 1kg grilled steak today  and it was absolutely delicious.

The waitress  Kaolin  was incredibly friendly; her food and wine recommendations were fantastic  and the service was excellent. ALATi at Siam Kempinski Hotel Bangkok The seafood on ice was fresh and delicious  especially the lobster 🦞. The staff provided excellent service. ALATi at Siam Kempinski Hotel Bangkok This is not an occasional issue but a regular practice at the restaurant of the Kempinski hotel.

A large table in the very center of the dining room is always marked as “Reserved” and kept for so-called VIP guests. When asked  the restaurant manager explains that this table is reserved specifically for VIPs  stating that they are “very sensitive” (her exact words). However  despite this explanation  these guests are not seated in the private VIP rooms  but are instead expected to sit at a regular table in the main dining area.
This is not a special event or private function  just a supposed dining reservation. Yet most of the time the guests do not show up at all  while the table remains empty from lunch until closing  and other guests are refused seating.
To emphasize exclusivity  the adjacent table is removed so no one can sit nearby  and a large fresh flower arrangement is placed on this table  while the rest of the restaurant has only small dried flowers.
At the same time  all other guests are required to reserve a specific table for a specific time  and their reservation would normally be released if they do not arrive. Holding a prime table all day without confirmed arrival creates a clear sense of unequal treatment.
What is most disappointing is not the reservation itself  but the philosophy behind it. This approach openly supports a hierarchy among guests. Some are granted space  flexibility and visual privilege «just in case»  while others are expected to comply strictly with rules and availability.

This hardly feels like the standard of a large  premium international hotel and restaurant brand. It feels outdated  almost primitive and  above all  deeply disrespectful to the majority of guests who are quietly reminded that the restaurant is not designed for them in the same way. The atmosphere is cozy but elegant  and the food has a lovely Mediterranean taste with fresh  quality ingredients. The dishes are simple  well-made  and very satisfying. It’s a great place to relax  enjoy good food  and spend time with friends or family. I’d be happy to come back again. ALATi at Siam Kempinski Hotel Bangkok We tried Sunday Brunch in January 2025. I re-visit again in December 2025. The menu was updated and much better. Main course are prepared individually but the rest are on buffet lines. The tempura rice bowl featured large pieces of shrimp with plump textures  a crispy but not oily batter  and a perfectly balanced sweet and savory sauce. It paired wonderfully with the hot rice.

The restaurant is located in a mall  making it easy to get to. The atmosphere is clean and organized  the staff are attentive  and the food comes out relatively quickly. There might be a short wait during peak hours  but overall  the quality and taste are worth it.

It's perfect for those who want a truly satisfying and filling Japanese meal without feeling overwhelmed by the taste. Tempura lovers will definitely enjoy it! Akimitsu Tendon Central World Absolutely five stars. I am japanese  visiting Bangkok and this was my favorite meal. The soft shell crab and especially the egg tempura. I keep thinking about this place I am going today again for lunch. Service is excellent  I love the pickled vegetables which may seem strange to others but for us japanese we love it.  It is so difficult to have crispy tempura that isn't greasy.  This place is SO good. It's hard for tempura to be bad  so
while it's tasty  the oil is often not fully drained 
and you can sometimes taste the oil when you bite into the batter.
More oil doesn't necessarily mean better flavor

Anyway  if you're looking for tempura at Central World

Amara Bangkok Hotel Staycation ruime kamers  geen gezellige lobby. Zwembad is niet groot  maar we hebben er toch veel plezier in beleefd. Geen authentieke Thaise vibes  maar voor 2 nachten toch eens tof. Het ontbijt is zeer ruim. In de straat zijn er veel massage salons. Ook veel ongedierte te zien in de straat. Lijkt me geen goeie locatie om de tempels te bezoeken. Amara Bangkok Hotel Staycation 1. When we came to this hotel  the first thing that I noticed was the drinks and snacks that they sell at the lobby. On the other side of it was starbucks. For a small lobby (not their fault)  they could have use the space to put sofas for their guests while waiting for their grab or check in. There are tables and chairs but those are for the guests who will order their drinks from the bar. You can sit there also but you cannot bring outside drinks. Like  this is the lobby of the hotel. What if I have an outside drink and i just want to chill there before my check in? This is the first hotel that I have been too that immediately sells food and drinks in the lobby. Kinda done in poor taste.
2. The swimming pool area is also small. There are like 5-6 lounge chairs for guests. Again  like the lobby  for a small place ( not their fault) they could have chosen to not put a bar near the pool. Just use the space for lounging chairs. Guests can just order from the restaurant downstairs if they want a drink and the staff can bring it up. This is how tou maxime the space for the comfort of your guest and not use every little space to earn profit. I know times are hard  but treat your patrons well also.
3. Oftentimes I see that the management would say that the pool is crowded due to peak season. But knowing that they have almost 200 rooms  they should be prepared for these seasons and make sure that the amenities that they post on the website to attract guests are proportionate to the number of guest. I mean  they cant always blame it to the peak season  this has been a constant concern so it should be addressed already.
4. The cleaning staff do not come everyday. Like for the 15 days that we stayed here  2-3 time they did not clean eventhough we pressed the make-up room button. Or sometimes they would come 4:30 in the afternoon when we come back from our tour already. If they see you still have tissue  they will not give you the supply for that day (the remaining tissue is not enough to last for another day). They also do not give fresh towels sometimes if they feel that you will still use the used ones. So we often called the lobby to give us new towels. These are little stuff but you will feel how they try to make ways to scrimp and short change their guests.
5. The tv is also not a smart tv which is so unsual for a 5-star hotel. I mean 3-star hotels already upgraded to smart tv by this time. We just used our laptop and bought a cable and attached it to the tv to watch movies and netflix. This one makes the feel of the hotel not on a 5-star level.

All in all  the hotel is not what we expect from a 5-star hotel. It is more of a 3-star but super expensive. We suggests that you find other hotels nearby. There is a lot of them anyways in the area. Amara Bangkok Hotel Staycation It was a great stay at the Amara hotel.
Special thanks to Reggap at the concierge service. He didn’t give up getting me into the golf club.
Will definitely come back again. Amara Bangkok Hotel Staycation Very good  well located. This is the hotel I've stayed at in Bangkok for the past 10 years. P

A must list: Grilled river prawn  Grilled cheese scallop  Salmon spicy salad  and wagyu skewer! Anchor Premium Buffet I'd like to offer some advice to the owner:
The most important thing for a restaurant like this that makes customers want to return is the food.

I noticed a lack of courage to excel in any particular flavor; it's like they're trying to make every dish mediocre.
The scallops used butter that was excessively greasy and sickening.
The dishes offered were inconsistent in flavor; there was no signature dish that made it feel like a standout item. Nothing was amazing; it felt like people just ate to fill their stomachs and be done with it  because it's a buffet.

Compared to other restaurants  the price is justified. The only good thing about the restaurant is the view. Despite the fact that there should be more people enjoying their meal  I observed that many tables didn't have that. I apologize if this review offends anyone  but visiting on Valentine's Day made the experience even more disappointing. Thankfully  I didn't take the Valentine's Day special which charged double the price  as it wasn't much different except for the drinks and the availability of a reserved table. text:
Ancient Tea House Asiatique the Riverfront  Ancient Tea House Asiatique the Riverfront  Ancient Tea House Asiatique the Riverfront This is a high-end restaurant.
The staff are attentive and considerate.
I stopped in for tea without a reservation  and the atmosphere was very comfortable. Ancient Tea House Asiatique the Riverfront ✨✨✨An oasis of calm offering an excellent choice of Chinese food and outstanding service. 🌟🌟🌟 in the middle of Bangkok’s animated  crowded and noisy attraction Center “Asiatique” we were delighted to be seated in a green Terrace with privacy and calm. The option “all you can eat - pay 1 get 2” is an excellent offer. All you can eat is something we generally avoid as it comes with horrible food buffet for mass… this is the contrary and therefore we recommend highly: you order all your plates one by one. They come in small portions so you get a chance to really try a maximum of variety. It was delicious  creative  well presented  partly surprising… there is enough effort to reach the higher end of Chinese cuisine. The very friendly and impeccable service made our romantic dinner an unforgettable moment. My personal recommendation is the clear soup. It is a hidden jewel of simplicity and savoury delight  a true relief in the most overwhelming bombardment of tastes you get all around. Reading other reviews I certainly would never spend New Year’s eve or other big holidays at the Asiatiaque… 👉 choose a normal weekday  Lean back and enjoy 🤩 🙏💐😋 Ancient Tea House Asiatique the Riverfront This review is solely about the parking lot. The management is terrible. I came with two cars and parked for 9 hours  spending 3-4 thousand baht. There was no one at the information counter. When I asked about parking fees  I went to ask three different security guards  and each gave a different answer. In the end  my friends parked for free  but I paid the full amount. The bill was meaningless and worthless. Anyone bringing a car should ask about the parking fees beforehand.

restaurant name: Annyeong Grill 안녕 그릴 Railway Station (Chiangmai)
text:
Annyeong Grill 안녕 그릴 Railway Station (Chiangmai) This BBQ restaurant near Chiang Mai train station was amazing when I went at full price. When they had a promotion for 118 baht  I joined in! 🧡

The food selection isn't huge  but I chose pork  mainly pork belly  although they also have shrimp  squid  and side dishes. It was great value for me because everything was delicious (too bad they didn't have gochujang!). ☺️😋

Two hours was enough time to eat. They have free small ice cream cones so you can try many flavors.

The staff provided good service.

Drinks aren't included  but they have refillable green tea.

Plenty of parking. Reservations are recommended. 🥗🥩🍖🍨 Annyeong Grill 안녕 그릴 Railway Station (Chiangmai) There are many varieties of beef  489 baht per person. The downside is that you'll smell of oil and smoke after eating. Annyeong Grill 안녕 그릴 Railway Station (Chiangmai)  Annyeong Grill 안녕 그릴 Railway Station (Chiangmai) Here's an honest review:
Overall  the food is good  the pork is good  the side dishes are good  and it's delicious.
At a price of 300 to 400+ baht  I think it's reasonable  but it doesn't include drinks for the buffet. However  if you come alone  there's an extra 100 baht charge. If you get the pork package plus refillable drinks and the solo seating fee  it will be around 500+ baht. I'm not sure if there's VAT added  but I think it's a bit expensive per person.
Honestly  I've wanted to eat here for a long time but never had the time. Today  I tried it with a special promotion. The restaurant said that if you come alone  there's an extra 100 baht charge  but today there was a promotion for 118 baht off  so I could eat alone without the extra charge. Today  I only paid 358.17 baht. If you come at the regular price without the promotion  I wouldn't recommend it at all. Some dishes are very large portions and need to be shared; they're not suitable for eating alone at all. Actually  many restaurants are quite welcoming to solo diners and don't charge extra.
Regarding the service  there was a mistake in the order. They served kimchi soup and fried rice that I didn't order  but I only returned the fried rice. I hesitated to order because the menu said "large size " but the service was good. They refilled our water frequently without us having to ask  which was great.
Also  the round tables by the window were extremely hot. Even under the air conditioning  the air didn't reach them  and the sun shone directly in. Sitting there between 4 and 5 pm was unbearably hot from the sun.
Please consider this.
***I'm confused about how a solo customer's meal is more expensive. Solo customers eat the same amount and pay the same price. In fact  solo customers often eat less than four people. This excuse is completely unconvincing.*** Annyeong Grill 안녕 그릴 Railway Station (Chiangmai)

Anya Authentic Thai Cuisine ⭐️⭐️☆☆☆ (2/5)

We had a reservation for Valentine’s dinner at Anya Restaurant and chose the sharing menu for two through Hungry Hub. Unfortunately  the experience was very disappointing due to poor service and food quality.

We arrived at 7:30 pm  and at that time there was only one table occupied. We looked at the menu and placed our order shortly after. However  between then and 8:20 pm  four other groups arrived and were served 2–3 dishes each  while we received nothing.

The only thing we were served was one iced Thai tea  which was very diluted and tasted more like milk than tea — almost like old milk — with barely any tea flavor.

At 8:22 pm  our first dish finally arrived  and it was cold. The tofu dish that followed was good  but unfortunately the Pad Thai with prawns was unacceptable. The prawns smelled very fishy  and both the prawns and the noodles tasted like there was gasoline in them. There was no proper flavor at all.

Because of the poor quality  we could not eat the food and ended up walking out without finishing anything  including the iced tea. We didn’t even wait for our chicken tom yum soup to come out.

We were especially disappointed because we had visited in 2023 and were very impressed  which is why we decided to come back for Valentine’s Day. Sadly  this visit did not meet our expectations at all.

Based on this experience  we would not recommend this place and would advise others to avoid it until major improvements are made.

We hope management will take this feedback seriously and improve both service and food quality. Anya Authentic Thai Cuisine The food and service were excellent  highly recommended! Arno's Naradhiwas Rajanagarindra Soi 20 We live nearby and Arno’s has always been our first choice when we want to dine out. In the past  the service was generally average but acceptable. However  tonight’s experience was very disappointing.

An older staff member made a mistake with our order. A dish was brought to our table that we clearly did not order. Both of us were confused because it was something we would never order — we are actually allergic to it. When we politely explained that we hadn’t ordered that dish  instead of apologizing or checking again  the staff member rolled her eyes multiple times  removed the menu abruptly  and did not offer a single word of apology.

In addition  when I ordered my pasta  I specifically asked whether it was olive oil–based. She confirmed that it was. However  when the dish arrived  it was clearly tomato-based. This showed a lack of basic product knowledge  which is very disappointing for a restaurant at this level.

Everyone can make mistakes  and we completely understand that. What was upsetting was not just the wrong order  but the attitude  lack of professionalism  and lack of basic menu knowledge.

As regular customers who live nearby and frequently choose this restaurant  we expected better service standards. Sadly  today’s experience has left us very disappointed. I came based on reviews on TikTok  but the reality was completely different. Nothing was prepared. The wait for food was ridiculously long. We chose the buffet  almost 1 000 baht per person for 3 hours  but the food took forever to arrive  and the kitchen system was terrible. There were hours-long gaps between orders  and we kept asking for food. When they finally came back  they said  "Only 5 minutes left! Order quickly! Order a lot!" The food from the previous round hadn't arrived yet  and the last dish we ordered was incredibly burnt. The staff looked confused about what was happening. My friends and I (5-6 people) just left. In short  one time is enough. Before following reviews anywhere  check the comments on Google Maps first – that's the most accurate information. restaurant name: Atrium Restaurant The Landmark Bangkok Hotel
text:
Atrium Restaurant The Landmark Bangkok Hotel  Atrium Restaurant The Landmark Bangkok Hotel We had a 2-for-1 promotion on February 7th  2026. The brunch cost approximately 3 500 THB for two people.
The price included drinks: tea  coffee  water  and fruit smoothies (I recommend the orange juice  it's delicious).

1. Steak: A little overcooked. I recommend informing the chef of your preferred level of doneness. The taste was delicious.
2. Lamb: Not gamey  but a bit too fatty.
3. Foie Gras: The first dish was served in a thin  perfectly cooked slice. It paired well with the bread and berry sauce  cutting through the richness. This dish was delicious. However  the next serving seemed to be thicker and larger  resulting in a less flavorful and richer texture.
4. Truffle Soup: Served with a delicious croissant. I really liked it (but it's still not as good as Copper's).
5. The restaurant's selling point: Grilled river prawns. The prawns were quite large  but not fresh; the meat was mushy and not firm. My boyfriend and I ordered them 3-4 times and still didn't find any with firm meat. However  the seafood dipping sauce was good.
6. Steamed Crab with Roe: For us  it was just okay; the crabs were too small.
7. Large  fresh oysters  delicious  served with fried shallots  acacia leaves  and chili paste – a perfect combination.
8. Sashimi – too bad they didn't have scallops.
9. Thinly sliced ​​beef dish  I can't remember the name  but it was near the sashimi station  highly recommended  very delicious.
10. Papaya salad was very sweet  like syrup.
11. Pickled crab with roe was just okay  the pickling liquid wasn't impressive  too sweet.
12. Roast duck was terrible  very tough  like eating rubber.
13. Crispy pork belly was very crispy but too salty.
14. Mango sticky rice was not sweet at all  bland.
The menu is quite extensive and varied. If you're paying full price  I wouldn't recommend it  but with the promotion  it's about 1 500 baht per person  still acceptable. Atrium Restaurant The Landmark Bangkok Hotel Why have we not eaten here before ? Can't believe we've stayed at the Landmark many times but the Atrium has always passed us by.
We took advantage of the 50% off voucher so basically it was 'pay for one and the other lines for free'.
Absolutely impossible to complain and the food selection   there's something for everyone here.  If you're a seafood lover then you're going to be in heaven.
Meat lovers.... just WOW.
for me....Foie Gras and grilled shrimp done me perfectly well.  Add some Wagyu and Japanese beef and I left a very happy diner.
Probably one of the best buffets I've experienced in 64 trips to Thailand.... and here's a big tip for anyone wanting to try it.
Apparently it's even better on the weekends with food upgrades. I'm not sure what that involves though and how they can actually improve on what they're already doing... will try it next time and report back. Atrium Restaurant The Landmark Bangkok Hotel  Atrium Restaurant The Landmark Bangkok Hotel An incredible experience with attentive service from start to finish! We thoroughly enjoyed ourselves! Nothing like the all-you-can-eat buffets in Europe (France in particular). text:
Audrey Restaurant - A Chatrium Collection (Khao Yai)  Audrey Restaurant - A Chatrium Collection (Khao Yai) Fresh and frozen seafood  chicken parts.
Original shop in Warin Chamrap district  open since 1976  certified formaldehyde-free. Audrey Restaurant - A Chatrium Collection (Khao Yai) Lacol dome is perfect for its visual appeal  but the food quality and service lag behind.

We reserved the dome seating and it was absolutely stunning  with a beautifully curated setup that looks amazing in photos. There was a minimum spend of 3000 baht for the dome  but totally worth it. The dinner tea tree dessert alone was around 1200baht  so it was not that difficult to reach the minimum spend. The dinner tea tree dessert presentation was a real highlight  especially perfect when paired with the dome. We had seen similar concepts elsewhere  but this is the biggest and most visually impressive one  making it perfect for photos.

Unfortunately  the actual desserts did not matches the presentation and were rather ordinary in taste. The main courses were also quite mediocre  which was disappointing given its higher prices. The restaurant was very quiet during our visit  with only a few tables occupied and spaced far apart. It was difficult to get the attention of the staff. We ordered sparkling water and also requested for warm water  but despite asking for the warm water three separate times  it never arrived.

This place is great for photos and some fun  but not somewhere for a quality dining experience. restaurant name: Baan Suan Rim Khlong Maha Sawat
text: Baan Suan Rim Khlong Maha Sawat 離海灘走路要10分鐘以上，外出交通需要摩托車或是計程車，附設的餐廳是附近最美味的 Baan Suan Rim Khlong Maha Sawat  Baan Suan Rim Khlong Maha Sawat We had a really lovely two night stay here. The rooms are spacious and clean with everything you need  there is a lovely quiet pool and there are lovely flowers and ponds around. We had a fantastic breakfast too! Baan Suan Rim Khlong Maha Sawat We had two perfect nights here; the environment and cleanliness were excellent. I'd like to thank the lady at reception who noticed my companion wasn't feeling well and prepared electrolyte water. We were very grateful and touched. In fact  every staff member here is incredibly friendly  and I hope to see you again sometime. Baan Suan Rim Khlong Maha Sawat The rooms are great and especially for the price point. Clean  tidy and spacious. Short walk across the road and through the school to the beach (Relax Bay) - 5 mins max. Very quiet in the evening but a short walk to lots of restaurants (10-20 minute). Some of the best bars are on Relax Bay. Staff can't to enough and the pool and cafe are great value and very tasty. A highlight was the mulberry smoothie with the berries grown around 100 ft from the restaurant. Make sure you have one! 😋

Baan Suriyasai @ One Bangkok Google Maps says to go to Exit 3  but Exit 1 is more convenient. It's not in the main tower  but in The Stories building. I visited here because I wanted to enjoy a gourmet Thai meal. The main branch in Surawong is very famous  but it didn't fit my itinerary  so I went to the branch  One Bangkok  first. Even though I visited on a Saturday lunchtime  the mall itself wasn't as crowded as it is on weekends in Seoul or in Chit Lom or Asoke  so I was able to enjoy my meal leisurely. The interior was also very classy. The atmosphere seemed perfect for women and couples on dates. The good lighting made for beautiful photos. The ingredients  whether vegetables or meat  were incredibly fresh  making the meal enjoyable. The Hungry Hub deal was very economical  so we ended up eating a two-person set that was filling. While the set is for one or two people  it's a lot for one person. Baan Suriyasai @ One Bangkok I'm surprised I didn't try this place sooner. Maybe it's because One Bangkok has so many great restaurants. And once I tried it  I loved it! The traditional Thai food was incredibly delicious and beautifully presented. Before entering  I thought it would be more expensive  but considering the ingredients  the ambiance  the service  and its central location with views of the surrounding area  the price felt very reasonable – even cheaper than I expected. Especially yesterday  New Year's Eve with the countdown party  even though the area outside was lively and noisy  the restaurant was peaceful and comfortable  perfect for enjoying a meal with family and friends.

Another outstanding thing was the staff; they were all incredibly lovely and provided excellent service. As for the food  I expected it to be of high standard for traditional Thai cuisine in a luxury restaurant  but when I actually tasted it  I could feel the care put into everything – from menu planning and preparation to the beautiful and unique presentation.

In short  it exceeded my expectations. I was very impressed. Next time  I'll try the Afternoon Tea! We actually ate a much wider variety of dishes than what I managed to photograph.😅💕 Baan Suriyasai @ One Bangkok This is definitely not just a typical shopping mall restaurant  it was truly one of the best meals we had during visit to Bangkok. The restaurant is beautifully decorated and the food presentation is stunning. A special thank you to Ms. Biu  who was incredibly attentive and helpful and made the experience even better. Highly recommend! Baan Suriyasai @ One Bangkok  Baan Suriyasai @ One Bangkok I'm a tourist who visits Bangkok five or six times a year to play golf.

I stay at the Conrad Bangkok or The Sukhothai Bangkok  and I've tried all the delicious Thai restaurants nearby.

After a long deliberation  I ended up at Baan Surai Sai  which has been getting a lot of reviews lately. I was hoping to visit the beautiful restaurant  but there were only four of us  and it was difficult to call a Grab  so we decided to walk to One Bangkok.

One Bangkok is so big that it's not easy to find  but if you follow the map or photos  you'll be there in no time.

I booked through HungryHub and got a discount of about 40%. The waitress spoke English well and was very friendly and explained each dish to us.
The drinks list  especially the cocktails  were excellent.
The Wagyu beef and the crab shrimp (Choo Choo Curry) were delicious.

Some dishes are a bit spicy  but it wasn't too spicy for a Korean.
The portions are generous enough that you don't need to eat much rice.

You can eat your fill at the incredibly hot One Bangkok  with giant giant prawns  Wagyu beef  and even crab.

I visited the French Spa for the first time on the second floor of the Maison Hotel  across from the Doubletree Hotel  which is visible from my hotel.

I love oil and scrub treatments  and I chose a two-hour Thai massage with a friend. I booked through the Gowabi app and got a great discount.

The woman at the counter spoke English well and took feedback after each treatment. The masseuses were easy to communicate with.
They offered tea and fruit upon arrival and departure.
It wasn't a stuffy massage parlor  but a comfortable space reminiscent of a yoga or meditation space.
I would definitely return  and it's conveniently located near Langsuan and Shatton. If you're driving  take the elevator to the lobby first  then switch to the lobby elevator. The elevators are quick and don't take long to arrive.
-The dim sum buffet includes one main course  one soup  and one dessert.
-The restaurant opens at 11:30 AM. You have to wait until the restaurant opens before you can take the elevator to the 59th floor. Upon arrival  you'll be seated in another room for about 10+ minutes.
-The staff is friendly  polite  and efficient.
-The first course of food arrives quickly  but subsequent orders may take longer.
-The mocktails are decent.
-My favorite dim sum dishes include: Abalone and Caviar Shrimp Dumplings (very fragrant)  Steamed Scallops with Garlic  Steamed Crab Legs  Steamed Sea Bass  Taro Fritters with Truffle Chicken Filling  and Fried Purple Sweet Potato.
-For the main course  the Stir-fried Beef with Black Pepper is tender and not gamey.
-The Fish Maw Soup is very salty.
-For dessert  the Black Sesame Glutinous Rice Balls in Ginger Syrup are delicious  and the glutinous rice balls are acceptable.
-The Egg Tart was not hot  the pastry was mushy  and the taste was not to my liking. Bai Yun Banyan Tree Bangkok The night view is amazing  and although it's a little pricey  there's a wide variety of dim sum options  so I'm very satisfied! Mile was also very kind  and I'd definitely like to come again. restaurant name: Bangkok Heightz The Continent Hotel Bangkok
text: Bangkok Heightz The Continent Hotel Bangkok good view   nice buffet breakfast. service is great. especially staff called beer. come and visit here！ Bangkok Heightz The Continent Hotel Bangkok  Bangkok Heightz The Continent Hotel Bangkok beautiful view  good food and great service by Mind. 👏🏻👏🏻 👏🏻 👍🏻 👍🏻 👍🏻 Bangkok Heightz The Continent Hotel Bangkok Dining on the 39th floor at Bangkok Heightz feels less like a rooftop stop and more like a carefully staged culinary experience above the city. The value here is not only the view: the real strength lies in the quality of the ingredients  precise preparation  and thoughtful plating. The kitchen delivers a flavor of Siam with western notes  balancing fragrant herbs and subtle spice with clean  structured techniques. Textures are handled with care  from crisp  lightly charred edges to tender  juicy interiors  and the sweet  spicy  and acidic elements are calibrated so each bite feels composed rather than loud. Aromas of lemongrass and citrus rise gently from the plate  and the presentation shows attention to detail without overcomplicating the dish. Service is attentive and well paced  with staff explaining components clearly and maintaining smooth timing between courses. The atmosphere is refined yet relaxed  allowing the skyline to frame the evening without overshadowing the food. Book a table around sunset for the best light and reserve in advance  especially on weekends  to secure a spot near the edge. Bangkok Heightz The Continent Hotel Bangkok Beer welcomed us warmly

restaurant name: Bangkok Sky Buffet (76/78th floor) at Baiyoke Sky Hotel
text:
Bangkok Sky Buffet (76/78th floor) at Baiyoke Sky Hotel  Bangkok Sky Buffet (76/78th floor) at Baiyoke Sky Hotel The food was indeed quite ordinary. The ticket I booked on Trip.com cost 1000 NTD  but the main reason to go was to see the 84th-floor observation deck. The entire observation deck rotates  offering panoramic views of Bangkok. After seeing it  I felt the ticket was well worth it. Bangkok Sky Buffet (76/78th floor) at Baiyoke Sky Hotel  Bangkok Sky Buffet (76/78th floor) at Baiyoke Sky Hotel The buffet was quite poor for what one expects when visiting a place like this.

I've been to other buffets in my life  and the variety and quality of the food at this one left much to be desired. It's true that some dishes were okay  like the meat or prepared pasta  but nothing special. Overall  the food was very basic for my taste.

The only thing worthwhile is the view  although the views from the 78th floor  where the buffet is located  are difficult to appreciate because the windows are tinted. I don't know if they're like that or if they're just dirty. At least they give you the option of going up to the 84th floor  where there's a revolving platform that allows you to enjoy breathtaking views of Bangkok. I went at night  and it was spectacular.

If you're looking for the experience of eating in a place like this  it's worth it. But as for the food itself  I think there are much better restaurants in Bangkok at better prices. Bangkok Sky Buffet (76/78th floor) at Baiyoke Sky Hotel The main attraction is being able to go up to the 84th floor  which revolves  offering a fantastic view.

The buffet is very outdated  the furniture and its condition are awful  the food is average  especially the desserts. Some of the main dishes are okay  but it's all very old-fashioned; it feels like you're in an old food court.

Staff too busy on their phones to bring drinks
At least it wasn’t crazy expensive Bar Yang Who knows? It's so rare to find such an affordable restaurant in Singapore's Zundu district—one with a river view  great atmosphere  and perfect for a date!

You sit down  light the fire  and they stir-fry the clams right there. The aroma explodes in the air as soon as the charcoal fire is lit. No need for further explanation; you're already captivated by the fragrance! 😌

The clams are fresh. Each one is plump  firm  and juicy. They absorb all the savory flavor of the broth after being cooked.

The broth is slowly simmered over charcoal  with just the right amount of saltiness. One sip  and you're instantly warmed up.

Add a touch of Shaoxing wine  and the aroma deepens  making it even more delicious with each sip.

Sliced ​​meat  meatballs  glass noodles  and vegetables are added one after another. The savory flavor of the broth slowly seeps into the ingredients  becoming increasingly fragrant as it cooks. As you eat  the pace of conversation naturally slows down.

The self-service condiment station allows you to customize your own condiments.

At night  it transforms into a Nanyang-style bar. Order a drink 🍹  feel the gentle river breeze  and enjoy the steaming hot pot.

The atmosphere is just right—not noisy  not awkward. Bar Yang  Bar Yang The lala soup base is rich and flavorful  and the clams are plump and sweet. There's a good variety of condiments  and the service is excellent. The restaurant is located by the river  with a chill atmosphere  perfect for relaxing and chatting with friends. Bar Yang

restaurant name: Basil at Sheraton Grande Sukhumvit A Luxury Collection Hotel
text:
Basil at Sheraton Grande Sukhumvit A Luxury Collection Hotel I thought it was an authentic Thai restaurant  so I was a bit skeptical. I was worried that some of the dishes might not suit me because of the strong spices. But everyone around me recommended it  and it was so delicious that I decided to visit.

In conclusion  it's the most delicious and perfect meal I've had while traveling in Bangkok. There's nothing difficult about the menu. Every Korean will love the spiciness  the right amount of fish sauce  and the savory flavor. The stir-fried meat is tender and perfectly flavorful  with no gamey odor. I reserved a Hungry Herb Party Pack  and although it's not a small portion (for 2-3 people)  my older partner  who has poor digestion  ate everything and even added dessert. The ice cream was also incredibly delicious. The mango and coconut flesh were so delicious that I decided to bring my family back to Bangkok right after my meal. 😋

And the reason I'm giving such a long review is because all the staff are incredibly friendly and cheerful. They're always willing to give you more! Since it was our last day  I spent all my cash and couldn't leave a tip  so I'm leaving a long message of praise. 🥺❣️ Basil at Sheraton Grande Sukhumvit A Luxury Collection Hotel Asok is a place where you can enjoy authentic Thai food with your parents in a luxurious setting. While the prices on the menu are a bit pricey  they're reasonable for the hotel's class. I'm one of those people who struggles with making choices  so I made a reservation for the Hungry Hub Party Pack for three. I was able to get almost half price on the most expensive items from Group ABC. Draft beer was even more expensive  though. Marriott Bonvoy members also get a discount. I received a 10% discount on beer. Upon arrival  I was treated like royalty. The service from the entrance was excellent. If you want Thai food worthy of a five-star hotel  this is a place I highly recommend  and I would definitely visit again. Perhaps because there are many Korean guests  it's quite Korean-friendly. I don't think you need to go far to places like Sathorn  Lumpini  or the Chao Phraya River just to eat Thai food. Even though my accommodation was a few stops further from Asok Station  I felt it was worth the time. Basil at Sheraton Grande Sukhumvit A Luxury Collection Hotel  Basil at Sheraton Grande Sukhumvit A Luxury Collection Hotel I booked through Hungry Hub 
thinking it would be a simple four-course meal: appetizer  soup  main course (choose one of four)  and dessert.
I asked the staff  and they said they'd provide everything  not just one main course. It was so great.

I got about a 10% discount for booking through Hungry Hub.
I just remembered that you can earn Marriott points on your food and drink bill. Hahaha
They showed me to my room. It wasn't a closed room  so it wasn't stuffy  but it was quiet and free of distracting noises.
It wasn't on the menu  but they provided a welcome drink.

Since it was a hotel  they kindly laid out a napkin for me. ^^
One of the staff members was in charge. She spoke English fluently and was very friendly. She explained everything in detail for me  a middle-aged person with a curious appetite. She also threw in some humor here and there.
The appetizers came first. They were beautifully presented. Haha. Maybe there are a lot of Koreans at the hotel  so there were Korean instructions.
Thailand has perilla leaves. Haha. You can put various ingredients in the betel leaf wrap and eat it. I didn't eat much  so I ate three by myself.

Fried tiger prawns wrapped in egg noodles and grilled beef brisket served with tamarind sauce.

My little palate loved it.

A clear tom yum soup came out  and if you look closely  the sticks piercing the grilled scallops aren't plastic. Interesting.

This soup is made with spicy coconut milk.

What the heck  it wasn't shared  but served individually. It was so clean and tidy.

We started with half and half rice.

It wasn't morning glory  which Koreans love  but chayote. Stir-fried with shiitake mushrooms.

Fried sea bass in sweet sour sauce. It was so convenient because you don't have to dip it in it.

Duck breast with red curry and green onion.

Soft  chewy soft-shell crab.

I praised the chayote  saying it was delicious  so the kind ace employee I mentioned earlier told the chef 
and he brought it out himself and explained it to me. My junior girlfriend who was with me even tipped the chef and waiter. LOL
(Actually  I was so curious about this ingredient that I Googled it  but there wasn't much that came up.)
.
They served mango sticky rice and warm tea.
It was a truly delicious  formal  and moving meal  with a detailed explanation.

After sending the junior couple off  my friend and I called a Grab to go do something naughty. LOL
We called a Grab at the main entrance  but it wasn't there.
The employee looked at his phone  and after talking  he suddenly told us to follow him. He walked about 300 meters 
down one floor  and then another  and finally to the street to find the Grab.
I was so touched. It turned out the Grab was waiting at the back street corner  not the main entrance. Basil at Sheraton Grande Sukhumvit A Luxury Collection Hotel I haven’t been back for a long while.  It’s been closed down.  So glad it’s reopen  one of my favorite Thai restaurant.  It’s so convenient  right on BTS station.  The most important is quality of food.  First come with complimentary dish.  The second complimentary dish is amazing  prawn and crab meat raped inside rice needle  in a bit spicy coconut cream sauce  perfect combination of presentation and taste! We ordered beef cheek  soft  shell crab and prawn.  They are all ver yummy!

Basilico at Conrad Singapore Orchard Visited for the weekend brunch buffet. The service was disappointing for a 5-star hotel. Staff at the entrance weren't particularly welcoming and didn't provide any information when seating us. Food-wise  the pizza and pasta were mediocre  but they did have a great salad and cheese selection. I won't be returning :( Basilico at Conrad Singapore Orchard  Basilico at Conrad Singapore Orchard Visited Basilico with my family to celebrate our wedding anniversary. Food was great  esp. Italian cuisines - pasta  pasta  lasagna  steak - and selection of cheeses are incomparable. Seamless service from Jenny and Raja made our dinner more pleasant and memorable. I'll definitely visit this place again. Basilico at Conrad Singapore Orchard Basilico at Conrad Singapore Tanglin — excellent food and impeccable service  truly memorable as always.
A special thank you to manager Raphael  and to Raji  David  and the entire team for their professionalism and warm care. Basilico at Conrad Singapore Orchard The staff were very friendly. Had an enjoyable dinner  as well as a slide of birthday cake to celebrate. And sang us a birthday song 🎂 🎶    The staff was attentive  making sure that we are comfortable  and seek feedback. Not our first time here and will be back soon.

Thank you. Beauty in The Pot No service except when you stopped yr chopstick..they hurried you to clear table Beauty in The Pot  Beauty in The Pot Frankly find this place rather overrated. I’m not comparing to other hotpot places because I seldom eat hotpot but I think in general  it’s expensive and nothing too special. I do appreciate that they use decent ingredients and that the meats are fresh. Service is pretty good and the service staff are attentive and polite. The only qualm I have is that I wish that more of them spoke English and not assume that just because we speak Chinese just because we look Chinese. I had difficulty understanding them because I don’t speak Chinese. I get that it’s a Chinese restaurant but we’re still in Singapore where English is predominantly used and spoken. And whenever I come here with non-Chinese friends  we sometimes have difficulty communicating with the staff. Beauty in The Pot  Beauty in The Pot We try this hot pot buffet at Nex outlets. Soups are tasty 😋. Before we go  we do it reserved. So that no need to wait. Vegetable 🌽 & meats are fresh. A little expensive than other buffets but worth it. Staffs are attentive and friendly.

Beef Express Muangthong Nonthaburi The taste was great  very delicious. I drove all the way from Bang Na to eat at the Muang Thong branch because the Bang Na branch had closed. The staff provided good service  were friendly  and efficient. My only complaint is that the steak I ordered medium was quite rare; ordering it almost well-done would probably have been better. But overall  I'm still impressed. The Dogger steak was delicious; those who like sweet flavors would probably enjoy it. The soy sauce fried beef was also delicious. Everything was delicious! I'm happy with my meal after driving so far. Beef Express Muangthong Nonthaburi Delicious food  affordable prices  convenient parking  and excellent service. Highly recommend! Beef Express Muangthong Nonthaburi The deep-fried beef was chewy and arrived at our table first. Tables that arrived about 15 minutes later  also ordering grilled beef tongue  received theirs much later and had to ask for it. The sea bass was fresh  the fried sweet potato fries were delicious  and the soup was flavorful. Beef Express Muangthong Nonthaburi I was so excited to come here because I saw the reviews because the food looked beautiful and delicious. But after taking my first bite  I promised myself I would never come back.

P.S.
1. Food prices do not include 7% VAT and 5% service charge.
2. Beef Bolognese with Steak Toppings: 349 baht
3. Pesto Pasta with Steak Toppings: 289 baht Beef Express Muangthong Nonthaburi Every dish was absolutely delicious. The flavors were well executed  beautifully presented  and truly exceeded expectations. A very pleasant surprise.

Beef Express Yothinpattana 3 I bought a package through Hungry Hub  valid for 90 minutes.
I made a reservation for 5:00 PM and parked at 4:37 PM.
I probably got seated around 4:45 PM. When I finished eating at 5:50 PM  the staff told me I only had 10 minutes left and that I had a reservation for 4:30 PM. This isn't very acceptable. I initially thought this restaurant would give a 4-star rating  but after this incident  I'm giving it a 1. Beef Express Yothinpattana 3 The restaurant is small  and the storefront looks dark  almost like it's closed.
Considering the price compared to the taste and quantity  the food isn't very good value. The pictures/social media photos make it look big and appetizing  but the actual portions are quite small for the price.
This branch has very few tables and few staff. After sitting down  you have to go and get the menu yourself because there's only one server. The taste is average  nothing special.
The kitchen is open  so the smell of food clings to you.
If you want the buffet  you must book in advance through Hungry Hub.
There is parking available in the complex. Beef Express Yothinpattana 3 The fried beef was about 130g  and the portion was significantly less than expected.
(Other reviews mentioned larger pieces; this one was practically the size of an egg yolk.) The beef was also quite tough.

The striploin steak was listed as about 250g  which was contradictory; even a pineapple and corn kernels were bigger than that.

The bill included two extra items that we didn't order.
Quite disappointed. Beef Express Yothinpattana 3 Delicious food  great service. It's an all-you-can-eat buffet  and the food quality is excellent – ​​they don't skimp on portions. The price is good  including appetizers  drinks  and desserts. Each dish is as large as a regular à la carte item  so be careful not to order too much or you won't be able to finish it all! Beef Express Yothinpattana 3

Beer Collection  Beer Collection The service was not good. They will walk by and ignore you unless you are Thai. Beer Collection The OG of bottle craft beer collection around Phrom Phong. They have a huge collection of craft beer on the menu but I suspect they have everything available.

The bottle craft beer are reasonably priced as well as the food menu  although not extensive.

One negative note is that there are a lot of mosquitoes inside the shop. It's quite unbelievable that within a fully air conditioned beer place  there are so many mosquitoes.

The shop is desperately in need of a maintenance as well. The floor has tapes all over to cover the defects and there's a small pothole upon entering the shop from the back door so be careful not to fall down. Beer Collection ร้านคราฟเบียร์เจ้าเก่าอยู่คู่ K village พร้อมพงษ์มานานหลายปีมาก ที่จอดรถมีเหลือเฟือ มีโปรคราฟเบียร์ 2 แถม 1 เฉพาะ on tap อาหารชอบขาหมูเยอรมัน 399 บาท ชิ้นใหญ่คุ้มราคาเนื้อหนานุ่ม จุ่มซอสแซ่บอร่อย ลาบทอดก็ใช้ได้ Beer Collection This restaurant offers a wide variety of beers from many countries  with prices ranging from 1xx to 3xx baht. Beer lovers should definitely come  but we don't drink. 😅

Even if you don't drink beer  their food selection is very diverse. Some dishes are delicious  others are just okay.

Some of our favorite dishes include the BBQ pork ribs  German pork knuckle  and Japanese crab roe spaghetti.

Dishes we didn't like include the T-Bone Kurobuta and the Lobster bisque cream soup.

The atmosphere is nice  with both indoor and outdoor seating. We chose outdoor because the indoor area was reserved by a group and we were worried about noise. It was a bit hot outside  but the restaurant had plenty of fans.

There's also a live singer  but their performance wasn't up to par. 😅

The service was good  although there were some moments of confusion and we had to ask for our order a few times.

Beer Pradith Very friendly staff and good music  Craft beers is very tasty!! Must try! Beer Pradith Beer Pradith is a great spot for craft beer lovers. The first thing you notice is the wall of taps. There are so many options that it feels like you could come back multiple times and still have new beers to try. It is the kind of place where you can take your time  explore different styles  and actually enjoy the tasting experience.

The atmosphere is relaxed and easygoing  perfect for hanging out with friends or having a casual night out. A small but lovely touch is the free popcorn  which makes the whole place feel even more welcoming.

If you enjoy craft beer and want a place with a big selection on tap  this is definitely worth visiting. Beer Pradith Staff in bar very good   friendly and kind and very polite to customers  comfortable place  very good beer even we ordered a lot of beer this is the first place in Thailand that we visited the draft beer is delicious and the best  and the service is good ❤️ Beer Pradith  Beer Pradith Good beer  delicious food  perfect for chilling out after work. 👍🏻👍🏻

Begin Again The restaurant is beautiful  with a classic Christmas theme decoration. The service is excellent  the food is good  and the prices are reasonable. It's perfect for a date or a meal with friends. The owner is lovely and helped arrange photo spots  providing props and decorating beautifully with bows. Highly recommend this place for anyone looking for a place to eat and have drinks. Begin Again Booking a sharing set through Hungry Hub was great value. I went at lunchtime and there was nobody there  but there was only one staff member handling both service and food preparation  so the food took a while to arrive. The food tasted good and the portions were just right. The restaurant isn't large but has nice decor and plenty of parking. They don't add any extra charges for credit card payments. Begin Again Various choice of coffee and mixed drinks. Crepe is good. Staff are very nice and helpful. Beautiful interior and building. Begin Again  Begin Again Lovely and quiet atmosphere of the cafe located near the airport in Bangkok.

Food and drinks were exceptional. Invited my friend who I haven't seen for 5 and half years. Last time we met was in London UK before COVID and we had a wonderful discussion on what's going on in the world  our travel plans  and hopefully to meet up again  in Korea  or anywhere in the world!

Behind Bars A pleasant find for families to chill and dine in a neighbour town. 4-piece live band is a bonus. Food is a bit expensive but taken into account the entire experience  no complaints. Behind Bars  Behind Bars Went there last night... Standard has dropped drastically. Saw new faces and all the kitchens staff has changed. Steak price went up but the quality dropped and the steak became smaller. The satay also has dropped in standard. The prawns are not fresh but it seems more like frostened prawns. Behind Bars Good place to chill relax and listen to some live music. Mostly self service but pple are friendly. Behind Bars My group ordered buffalo wings  chicken tikka pizza  prosciutto pizza  carbonara and satay with some drinks. I think the carbonara and satays were rather delicious. The carbonara sauce was thick and creamy  and the spaghetti did not feel mushy even after sitting there for a long time. The satays were very savoury and had more meat compared to what I would find elsewhere. I was a little disappointed that their satays did not come with ketupat  but I guess it differs from place to place.

restaurant name: Bella Sera at Sofitel Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel
text:
Bella Sera at Sofitel Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel  Bella Sera at Sofitel Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel Food was truly authentic Italian food.  Diavola pizza took me back to living in Tuscany.   Aey  Jiaw  Kaew  and Natt were very attentive and knowledgeable about Italian Cuisine.  Highly recommended. Bella Sera at Sofitel Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel  Bella Sera at Sofitel Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel Amazing place.
Great food
Good ambiance and wonderful team.
Merci to Jian  Acy  Natty and Tus.
I truly recommend Bella Sera at Sofitel Bangkok Sukhumvit Hotel What a disappointment. We ordered two spaghetti dishes  one of which was Bolognese  but there was barely 30 or 40 grams of Bolognese sauce. I was served a stale  dry chocolate fondant with low-quality vanilla ice cream. By constantly reducing the quantity  the quality and freshness suffer  even with an Explorer card and a 30% discount  the bill came to nearly 3000 baht. I'm convinced that by presenting the Explorer card  the portions are reduced in the kitchen. Everything in the Sofitel hotel and restaurant is cut corners  with prices constantly rising. 80 euros for dinner with the Explorer card for two  only to eat elsewhere afterward! Former Platinum or Diamond guests like myself can see the difference. For me  after 15 years of loyalty to the Sofitel and the Accor group  it's definitely over today. Benares Modern Indian Cuisine Bangkok  Benares Modern Indian Cuisine Bangkok Visited on a Friday evening - it’s a 15 min walk from the Soi Cowboy area and is a more up market offering than the Indian Street kitchen next door. Benjarong Hotel Bangkok Staycation I stay here very often and am extremely impressed. The accommodation is clean  the beds are large  and the bathrooms are spotless. The staff provides excellent service from the moment you arrive  especially the staff member named Tawanchai  who explained everything in detail  spoke politely  and escorted me precisely to my room. They offer a shuttle service to the MRT and BTS  have a lounge area  and the food is delicious. I just realized that staying in a hotel feels like being princessed – this is the place! I will definitely stay here again. I rate it as number one in the Ladprao area. Benjarong Hotel Bangkok Staycation This is a 3 star hotel.  Room was ok  but shower was faulty. Technician came but did not fix  just said to remove filter to help water run down  which didn't fix issue.
Many items missing from room  almost 2 star setup.
Breakfast was ok  but simply with limited choices.  Been to many four star hotels  this was not 4 star.
Location  not so great if you want to get to central Bangkok. Benjarong Hotel Bangkok Staycation The accommodation was amazing! I loved it. We stayed there because we were going to party with friends in the city at night  and our plan was to take the MRT train. It was very convenient; the hotel is very close to Lat Phrao MRT station. The front desk service was excellent. The next morning  we had to leave the hotel early and checked out around 9 am  but the staff were still very welcoming and attentive. I would definitely stay there again if I have the chance. The room was spotless  and the bed was incredibly comfortable.

P.S. Even though our room faced the street  we didn't hear much traffic noise and slept soundly.😅 Benjarong Hotel Bangkok Staycation Bugs found in my luggage after leaving this hotel. First time and the last time staying at this place. Truly disgusting. Benjarong Hotel Bangkok Staycation I was very disappointed with my stay due to a serious odor issue in the room.
There was a strong and unpleasant smell coming from the bathroom drainage system  which made the room very uncomfortable.

The hotel attempted to fix the problem by placing a drain cover  but this caused the water to not drain properly  so I had to remove the cover every time I used the bathroom. Unfortunately  even with the drain covered  the bad smell was not resolved at all.

I was offered a room change  but sadly  the new room had the same strong sewage smell as well. This issue significantly affected my stay and comfort.

Although the location is good and convenient  the odor problem is a major issue that should not be ignored.
For this reason  I do not recommend staying at this hotel until the drainage problem is properly fixed.

Big Bite Burger BKK I went to Big Bite Burger  Bar & Grill on the 1st floor of Courtyard by Marriott Bangkok Suvarnabhumi Airport.
The atmosphere is a warm  friendly 90s American home style. 🍔

The new menu items exceeded expectations  and the ingredients were high quality.
The flavors were rich and truly homemade.
My favorites were the Bubba Gump Shrimp Burger and the Chicken Parmigiana – the cheesy goodness was amazing from the first bite!
For meat lovers  I recommend the Mixed Grill.
And the Loaded Chili Cheese Fries were addictive too.

If you're staying near Suvarnabhumi Airport or at this hotel 
I highly recommend trying it. Burger lovers will definitely love it! ❤️ Big Bite Burger BKK Not only was the hamburger worthy of its name  but the onion rings and spinach soup were also excellent. The portion in the photo was more than enough for my husband and me. Big Bite Burger BKK Loved everything about our visit to the Big Bite Burger Bar & Grill which is like an upscale diner experience.

The atmosphere here is casual  fun  and welcoming  it was perfect for us while we were staying at Courtyard by Marriott Bangkok.

Their menu is seriously packed. Juicy burgers are the stars here  cooked to perfection and loaded with flavour  we were pleasantly surprised about the flavor of their plant based burger we ordered and the fish burger was also just as delicious but there’s plenty more to enjoy here with their crispy sides  hearty mains  fresh salads  and I can’t leave out their creamy milkshakes that hit the spot. Generous portions  satisfying  and thoughtfully prepared.

What truly makes Big Bite special  though  is the staff. Friendly  attentive  and genuinely warm  they go out of their way to make you feel welcome. Big Bite Burger Bar & Grill has Great food  great people  and an easygoing vibe.  I highly recommend! Big Bite Burger BKK  Big Bite Burger BKK Good burgers

Bodhi Terrace at Anantara Chiang Mai Resort  Bodhi Terrace at Anantara Chiang Mai Resort We went here on our last night in Chiang Mai  deciding to go somewhere nice for a fine dining type experience. The reviews looked good  so we booked.

The evening started reasonably well. We ordered drinks  although my wife commented that her cocktail was bland. That can happen  so we moved on and ordered starters. The Larb Muang pork was greasy and lacked any spice. The soft shell crab was acceptable.

While I was still eating the pork  the waitress removed the condiments from the table. When I mentioned this  she replied  “You want more nuts.” I explained that I also wanted the onion  chilli  and lime  but they had been taken away mid-meal. She repeated that I must want more nuts. Her English was limited  and she reacted quickly without fully understanding the feedback.

For the mains  my wife ordered the ka prao  which arrived literally cold on the plate. My river prawn with red curry sauce was tepid and consisted mostly of shell with only a spoonful of meat.

The waitress did not understand or engage with any of the feedback we tried to give  so I asked for the head waiter. We explained that we were leaving and were happy to pay for the starters and drinks only. He brought the bill  which still included the service charge. When we queried this  another manager said they could not remove it. After some back and forth  they eventually applied a 10% discount to the food instead.

Overall  the food was below average  some staff were not suited to this price point  and the entire experience was disappointing. For a Saturday night  the restaurant was noticeably empty  which in hindsight was telling. In a foodie capital like Chiang Mai  this was a genuinely poor dining experience.

We visited on a Friday evening and ordered Paneer Butter Masala  two garlic naans  and one veg pizza. It took 45 minutes just to receive the naan and curry. The waiter even brought an extra naan  claiming it comes with the curry. I mentioned that if we had been informed earlier  we would have ordered only one. He simply said “oh” and added that nothing could be done because the other naan was already being prepared.

The curry looked like it was cooked and poured straight into the bowl with zero presentation. It was also too salty  and I informed the server.

The worst part was the pizza  which arrived one hour after ordering—and even after such a long wait  it was disappointing. The base was hard and didn’t taste fresh.

All of this happened even though the restaurant was almost empty  with only 3–4 tables occupied.

Looking for the perfect place to unwind after work? This rooftop delivers stunning panoramic city views—especially at sunset  when the golden hour feels truly magical. The open-air breeze makes it far more relaxing than a typical indoor bar.

🍺 Drinks: A paradise for craft beer lovers  with an impressive selection on tap and by the bottle from around the world.
🍔 Food & Service: Tasty international bar bites that pair perfectly with the drinks  plus attentive  knowledgeable staff and quick service—even when busy.

We wanted to know which seating area to choose. There were empty tables inside  but the staff said they were reserved  which we understood. We asked if we could move to another area if a table became available  as our current table was too close to the speakers. However  the staff said it wasn't possible because reservations were made by phone  and we noticed some people arriving between 8 and 10 PM. They insisted reservations were the only way to secure a specific seating area. I don't blame the staff  as they were following instructions from the manager. Honestly  the food is delicious  but the restaurant's customer management is poor. They prioritize walk-in customers who have reserved (and often arrive late) over those who do. In short  if you want a specific seating area  call ahead and reserve a table  no matter how late you arrive. Bull Market Noodle Bar This Japanese-Western fusion steakhouse & noodle bar seeks to offer a taste of luxury at reasonable prices. While I didn’t manage to try their beef this time  I was quite entertained by their extensive range of unhinged menu names & descriptions.

We had their Fairlady Z Kara-age ($9)  well battered crunchy chicken that’s crunchy outside and juicy inside  paired with smokey sweet-savoury aioli sauce. It’s a pretty decent snack that’s not too oily to accompany the mains/drinks.

(v) Tartufata ($25):
our top pick !! This was a handmade truffle-based Tagliatelle all'uovo pasta with a distinct flavour of creamy umami mushroom (generous amounts of shimeiji mushrooms)  & cheeses: Fior di latte & Parmigiano  though addition of meat would have justified the price tag/elevated the dish.

Loved the thin kurobuta pork + pre-sliced cheesy pizza slices with baked egg within the 12” White Hawaiian Pizza ($25)  & the juicy roasted pineapple chunks that were not too acidic in taste !! It’s fermented for 48h  hand stretched  & then stone-baked & wood-fired  creating an enjoyably crispy exterior that’s soft on the inside  with iconic “leopard spots” charred bits (which I personally didn’t enjoy for health reasons :”)).

Blue Hour ($8):
Mocktail made with green apple  ume plum  white pepper  lemon  butterflypea — reminded us of a Fruit Plus candy in soda drink form !!

Passion Night ($8):
passionfruit-forward soda drink — made with ginger ale  raspberries  passionfruit  fresh lemon.

Drinks came in decent sized glasses  & not too sweet. personally  passion night > blue hour  though both wouldn’t be my first choice of drinks haha (there’s alcohol)

ps: FREE scoop of Mõvenpick ice cream (vanilla  strawberry  or chocolate) if you leave them a Google review !!

text:

Just have a look from internet. By The Pool Restaurant & Pool Bar Decided to visit this place when we needed an afternoon respite from a busy day. Easily accessible by elevator. You can sit at the bar and stare at the booze bottles or enjoy a table under the lattice shaded area or even chillax at the half booth circular area and watch the blue waters of the pool as well as the fresh green palm fronds.
This visit we had a cold Tiger beer and a frozen margarita.  Both just fine. The truffle fries were some of the freshest you'll find. Yum! Good poolside food on a hot day.
Staff was smiling  hard working and seemed to understand exactly how to make guests feel not only welcomed but pampered.
Example: A mynah bird flew in  wanting some of our food. It made a mess spot on a seating area and within less than a minute a smiling staff member swooped in just as fast to tidy up. All this done with a sense of purpose and care. You don't find that every day. We've done our share of mess cleaning too and we know it can get old. However  being here was being welcomed. Thank you  Pan Pacific Poolside. Well done.
