CHANAN Japanese Cuisine (Samut Sakhon)  CHANAN Japanese Cuisine (Samut Sakhon) Delicious  fresh  and clean. The staff provided good service. I ate alone  and the portion size was just right. Recommended! CHANAN Japanese Cuisine (Samut Sakhon) Based on a TikTok video  the food looked amazing and delicious. It wasn't too far from home  so we decided to try it. However  after trying it  my family and I agreed that it didn't quite live up to the pictures.

But the restaurant's decor is absolutely stunning. The atmosphere is fantastic  and the décor is better than many upscale restaurants. We loved it!

As for the food:

👍🏻 The most delicious dish  absolutely mind-blowing  was the spicy beef salad. For us  it was excellent; the beef wasn't gamey  but the dressing could be a little less salty.
Another dish that was standard and very tasty was the crunchy rolls.

👎🏻 What needs serious improvement is the curry rice. The pork was very tough  the rice was hard and clumpy  and the sauce was completely unappetizing. It was really bad. Also  the green tea was served at different times. The first glass was extremely strong and full of green tea powder  while the second glass was normal. We understand it might be due to the bottom of the glass  but we've never encountered this at other restaurants where the color was consistent even after multiple refills. 🥲

Another issue we think could be improved is the door. Someone had to open and close it constantly. We think staff should only be busy with this task within the restaurant. Don't worry about the door. I understand that opening it forcefully will cause it to hit the concrete block in front of the shop. I recommend switching to a sliding door; the current one is extremely impractical. The seating is also problematic; some sections offer full back support  while others only provide half-back support  which is uncomfortable and causes pain. 🥲

Overall  there were both delicious and terrible experiences. For us  the price is expensive  but I understand they want a premium feel. However  I think we'll go back to restaurants with similar prices and premium quality  where we can order more food and they're closer to home. CHANAN Japanese Cuisine (Samut Sakhon) The atmosphere in the restaurant is nice  clean  and gives a Japanese feel.
The service is excellent  and the food tastes great. The fish is incredibly fresh. Overall  everything was excellent.

@ QSNCC (Queen Sirikit National Convention Center)

An Isan restaurant that brings the spiciness of Isan to the heart of Queen Sirikit National Convention Center.
The atmosphere is beautifully decorated in a contemporary style  simple yet stylish.
Comfortable and uncrowded  perfect for lunch between meetings 
or a warm and inviting dinner after work.

Recommended dish: "Grilled Chicken " fragrant with charcoal  crispy skin and tender meat.
Served with jaew sauce and hot sticky rice.
Also available: Somtam (Thai spicy salad)  Larb (Thai minced pork with chili and ginger)  Crispy Pork with Chili and Ginger  and Mixed Mushroom Curry.
The flavors are full of authentic Isan flavors  but perfectly balanced.

If you're visiting QSNCC and looking for spicy  yet easy-to-eat Thai food  we recommend stopping by here — you won't be disappointed.

Canale House  Canale House  Canale House It was an absolutely terrible experience. We went in with our child and sat in the air-conditioned room. After a while  no one came to attend to us  and there was no menu. We called out to a young woman who was pressing buttons on something and asked for a menu. She looked at us but didn't hand one. My partner finished parking and came in  and we still hadn't received a menu. So he went and got one himself. When we tried to call for a staff member to order  no one paid attention. A male staff member was serving customers at the table behind us. My partner and I called out to him 3-4 times  and he definitely heard us because we were right behind us. After he finished serving  he just walked past our table without saying a word.

The service was incredibly bad  the worst I've ever experienced. My partner said we couldn't take it anymore and wanted to leave.

However  I would like to suggest that the restaurant improve its staff training.
The day I ate there  it was around 10 PM. I misread the date  thinking the restaurant closed at midnight  but it actually closes at 11 PM. I was a little surprised when I found out  but I understood and was okay with it.
Nevertheless  the staff repeatedly emphasized that the restaurant closes at 11 PM and suggested I go to another restaurant  even though I insisted that I was still okay and intended to eat at this restaurant. This made the service experience a bit awkward.

Might be because some menus are only for Christmas but it was unsatisfactory in the taste department. The french toast for instance was very mediocre  the edge was very thick and doesn’t taste like French toast at all. We ate 4 bites and stopped.
The service is kinda weird as well. Nowhere near the standard of Cadence. And again with a foreign staff who doesn’t bother to learn Thai. Very common in fine dinning scene here but please respect our country and at least make sure your staff speak our language because not every customer is comfortable with English (my parents for example).
Over all very disappointing.
I heard regular menus are good but I would not want to go back. Caper by Dan Bark I visited with high expectations because of the great reviews.

1. Chicken Liver Mousse Baguette
The bread was really tasty  but the chicken liver mousse had a strong chicken flavor. I'm not sure if it would pair well with the honey truffle sauce on top of the chicken liver mousse.

2. Beef Tartare
As soon as the food arrived  I thought there was too much sauce for the meat. As expected  it was a bit overpowering and a bit salty. The sauce itself was delicious.

3. Wagyu Beef Dip
It tasted like a mix of Korean galbi bulgogi seasoning  Dijon mustard  and Thai jew sauce.

4. Seafood Bisque Pasta
The instructions said the sauce would be a bit bitter because it's made with lobster shells. It wasn't overly bitter  and it blended well  so I enjoyed it. However  some people may not like it.

5. Duck Breast Searing
The meat alone had a slightly gamey flavor. It was fine with the marinade and red cabbage.

The bread  mocktails  and cocktails were absolutely delicious  and the service was excellent. However  perhaps I was expecting too much  but the food was a bit disappointing. Caper by Dan Bark It's simply delicious. It's perfect for the Korean palate. The portions aren't huge  so I recommend ordering several. If you go on the weekend  the French toast is also delicious. And if you use an app like Hungry Love  you can get discounts  so take advantage of it... You'll probably find yourself happily ordering more if you buy it there. Caper by Dan Bark

Casa Pasta Had lunch here during Valentines Day. The wait time was acceptably long but moving. Pizza and pasta were made fresh and tasted amazingly well for the price. Portions were great too. Highly recommend! Casa Pasta Food is great but that is all that deserves praise in this place. We got the prosciutto pizza and the sausage and Gorgonzola pizza. Pizza dough was light and crispy  toppings were generous. Taste was overall delicious so no complaints.

The service  or lack thereof  leaves a lot to be desired. You have two female hosts who look like they would rather be anywhere else  no smiles  no welcomes. You can be standing there without them even acknowledging your presence. Before they even get you a table they tell you the last order is at a certain time and they close half an hour later. I understand it’s the eve of a public holiday and there are certain timings  but to not even be welcomed and then basically told when you need to leave is off putting. Your management needs to put in more effort when it comes to training of your staff  specifically your hosts.

Menu is not explained and not even updated. You have to order from a QR code  which is fine  but the physical menu does not correspond with the digital menu  and you’re not told about it until asked. I don’t understand why the restaurant is still using an old outdated menu and are alright with it being shown to patrons. Perhaps you can look into that as well  or at least train your staff to helpfully inform before hand the items that aren’t available anymore.

We were told twice before being seated that last orders were at 230pm  however the last order was being taken at 215pm. Again  understand that it’s the eve of a public holiday and you’re operating on shorter hours  but at least standardise your practices if you do implement them.

It’s unsurprising other reviews here also mention the abysmal service. The food is good but I don’t think I’ll be returning. Kudos to the chefs though  it was a great tasting lunch. restaurant name: Cheat Day by Modish The PARQ Rama 4
text:
Cheat Day by Modish The PARQ Rama 4 The restaurant is located on the 1st floor of The Parq building  next to the eyewear shop. It's easy to find.

The menu shows the calorie count for each item.
Lunch is served from 10:30 AM to 1:30 PM. Set menus are available  including your choice of drink (bael fruit juice  hibiscus juice  or mineral water) and a salad bar. The set costs 189 baht  which is excellent value and very generous in portion size – enough to fill you up completely. It also comes with grilled vegetables.

The Caesar salad has fresh greens  tender chicken  croutons  and fried bacon. The dressing isn't too oily.
It seems very healthy.

The food arrived very quickly  even though it was lunchtime and there were many customers.

The staff seemed a bit stern and didn't repeat the order  but the food was served correctly. Cheat Day by Modish The PARQ Rama 4 Good food! Tried their bibimbap and sea bass with grans and both were delicious. Not as bland as the photos might suggest.

Cheeky Signature’s (Bee Hoon) As promised  I came back to Cheeky Signature this afternoon for lunch. This time I ordered their Meatball with minced pork with flat noodles (mee pok)  dried version which comes with their signature soup in a separate bowl. The pork meatballs are chewy  with bits of mushroom to give them quite a unique taste. The flat noodles are not the common yellow mee pok variant  but rather seems to be made from wheat (aka soba-like).The savoury soup is excellent - seems to blend with dried noodles very well and this time I even asked for re-fill after I finished the meal! Once again  the owners are very hospitable and on this occassion  I was offered 2 different sauces to go with the meatballs and noodles - chilli with chopped garlic in soya sauce and a green lemonly tasting corinder sauce. Will not hesitate to bring the wifely along the next time! Cheeky Signature’s (Bee Hoon) Another hidden jem in Sim Lim Square. Homey home cooked food kind of food. Warms the belly and heart. I had the braised pork with pork lard rice (rare to find pork lard rice in SG)  and my partner had the fish slice and mince meat soup. Soup is packed with flavour. Fish slice is fresh  braised pork is really soft and almost melt in the mouth kind. Delicious and value for money.

The downside is that the store location is away from the usual popular food corridor on the second floor. They are located on the following corridor. I only knew about this stall when I decided to explore further down. Do give them a try.

Looking forward to try the other dishes on next visit. Cheeky Signature’s (Bee Hoon) Usually you would expect either the soup or the noodle small  I was surprised by the size of both. Value for money! Cheeky Signature’s (Bee Hoon)  Cheeky Signature’s (Bee Hoon) Fully enjoyed the meal. Finished the soup and rice with ever feeling they were too oily/salty.

- The service was excellent.
- The restaurant was quiet and peaceful.
- The spaghetti with shrimp in tomato sauce was good  tasty  and had good noodles and lots of shrimp.
- The bread on the pork steak was terrible. The bread quality was really poor  but what was worse was the use of margarine. This resulted in a lower-quality meal than I expected. (stir-fried chili and garlic) dishes; they'd probably make them well.

The service from the staff was excellent. The entrance door was broken and the automatic sliding door didn't work  so they quickly helped open it for both entering and exiting. It would have been better if the door was working properly  as it's the first impression for everyone entering.

The menu is beautiful  in good condition  and the illustrations are interesting and well-organized. However  what I really didn't like was the idea of ​​a restaurant offering two menus at different prices  claiming they were from different suppliers. This left customers like me feeling unsure of the brand's message. Especially since they've been giving me a menu from Chef Thanom for quite some time  and then sending me a separate menu from It's Ezy. The question I have is  how can customers be sure they're getting products from a better supplier at a higher price? If they're going to do this  they should separate the restaurants like they do in multiple locations. It's more comfortable.

An additional point: should chefs or food preparers wear gloves when handling customers' food? Please consider whether using bare hands is appropriate  or whether they should use utensils. I'm not sure if the bread I'm handling is cooked.

The two dishes I ordered:

The portions of the stir-fried clams with butter seem to have decreased. The buttery aroma was reduced. The taste  in my opinion  wasn't as impressive as before. But the garlic bread was still okay.

Salmon Risotto: I was very disappointed with my expectations of Italian food from this restaurant. The risotto lacked any cheese or creaminess. If it did  it wasn't even 10%  or it was the restaurant's special recipe. It could be described as dry rice soup  which would have been almost right if the rice were softer. Okay  the rice was just right  not overcooked or undercooked  but the pepper was too much  giving it a crunchy texture. The salmon was  I think  small pieces to garnish the dish  making it look appetizing. However  I felt that if it had been a larger fillet  it would have been more impressive.

Both the clams and the risotto weren't hot at all  barely warm. I don't understand. It might just be a personal preference.

Drinks: No comments.

I ordered the pumpkin soup from the "It's EZY" menu. It was good  not too sweet. Considering the price  it was okay  no complaints.

I'd like to try Chef Thanom's to compare. I'll consider which brand I'll choose next time I return  as the price is about double. And given my less-than-satisfactory experience  I'd like the restaurant to maintain its established standards. I've lost a lot of confidence  considering the price I paid.

P.S. I sent feedback back through the shop's contact channels  but they read it and haven't responded. I'll really have to think hard about using their services again.

Edit: The shop contacted me to say they've acknowledged my feedback.
They've summarized it and will present it to management for improved service. I'll have time to go back and inspect it again. China Palace Restaurant (Chong Nonsi) We came for a New Year's lunch.
Dim sum: 5 baskets  get 1 free (the free item depends on the restaurant  you can't choose).
Spend 3500 baht (excluding Peking duck) and get a Peking duck promotion: from 1200 baht down to 600 baht  plus one free dish made with duck meat.
Cash payment gets a 10% discount.

- Comfortable and well-organized seating. Very convenient access via BTS Chong Nonsi station.
- The restaurant doesn't have parking. You can park at the Trinity building next door for free for 1 hour.
- Free appetizer: pickled mustard greens salad (1 bowl for 4 people). Additional bowls are 40 baht each.
- Dim sum: The taste is okay. Some items are made fresh  some are frozen and reheated  not very fresh. We ordered 10 baskets and got 2 free.
There's a variety of dipping sauces to choose from.
- Peking duck: Delicious. The duck meat in the wrap is also delicious  especially with the fresh lettuce.
- Crispy noodle stir-fry with pork: Generous portion of noodles  served with the sauce and toppings separately. The portion size wasn't quite balanced with the noodles.
- The jellyfish salad was delicious.
- The marinated chicken wasn't very sweet.
Overall  the price is quite high compared to the quality.
Suitable for groups and easily accessible by BTS. China Palace Restaurant (Chong Nonsi) We had Peking duck here.
It was served in a unique way.
First  we ate all the filleted  crispy  grilled duck skin with wraps and vegetables.
Then we were asked how the meat should be served.
The English spoken was very difficult to understand.
So we opted for sweet and sour. We didn't like it.
It was very sweet  which completely destroyed the duck's flavor. Such a shame.

restaurant name: China Rose at Maruay Garden Hotel
text:
China Rose at Maruay Garden Hotel I tried the lunch buffet for 950 baht per person. They currently have a 4-for-3 promotion until December 31  2024.

For me  the fried food was delicious  the desserts were delicious  and the dim sum was both good and average  but they used good ingredients. Some things I didn't like were the chicken legs (tough  not tender) and the goose legs (tough  not tender  and had a fishy smell).

Recommendations: Crab dumplings  har gow  shrimp noodle rolls  cream-filled buns (not lava-filled  the lava-filled ones are a bit fishy)  kale in oyster sauce  fried shrimp dumplings  fried tofu skin  ginkgo nuts in fresh milk  and cantaloupe sago.

The Peking duck was average  as the duck was thick. However  the other side dishes were good.

The most impressive thing was the service. China Rose at Maruay Garden Hotel Went for the second time on October 12  2014 at 11:30 AM.

It was starting to get crowded  and the service was good. However  some of the highlight dishes were incredibly slow. For example  the Peking duck had much tougher skin than the first time. The fried tofu arrived just as I was about to be full.

Thankfully  the restaurant is located in a hotel  so there were no additional charges and I could return the food.

Went on August 31  2014 at 11:30 AM. There weren't many people.

Every dish was delicious  and the service was fast. They didn't hold back on the Peking duck. China Rose at Maruay Garden Hotel This dim sum buffet has a huge variety of food. The overall quality was great for us  exceeding expectations for the price. Everything was delicious  from the dim sum to other Chinese dishes like jellyfish in oyster sauce  stir-fried vegetables  noodles in gravy  and the must-try Peking duck.
There are also three soup options  all delicious. We especially liked the fish maw soup and the pork bone soup.
Overall  it was impressive and better than many other places we've been to. Great value for money.
See the pictures  there's so much food!
They currently have a promotion: buy 5  pay for 4. China Rose at Maruay Garden Hotel Dim sum buffet. I came for the 950 baht per person buffet. There were 5 of us  but 4 paid.
The dim sum was delicious. The jellyfish salad was amazing. The jellyfish and sesame oil were incredibly fragrant. The bamboo pith soup was also delicious. Peking duck was also a highlight. The restaurant is on the second floor and open daily for lunch.
We just sat at a table and ordered. The service was as good as a hotel. The staff were very polite. I recommend the fried tofu with chili and pickled peppers. Crispy on the outside  soft on the inside. China Rose at Maruay Garden Hotel

Chinda Hotpot Porto Chino I've seen this restaurant since it wasn't crowded  then they hired reviewers  and now people are queuing for 3-4 hours. The mala craze is clearly declining. If the restaurant doesn't improve  it's going to fail. There was only one other person eating there. It's no surprise it's not as popular anymore.
1. The dipping sauce has a unique and delicious flavor  but they don't seem to understand Thai people's preference for large portions and a net price  not small charges. It shows the owner hasn't calculated the break-even point.

2. The staff aren't Thai; let's call them employees. They just do their job  going through the motions without showing any effort or enthusiasm for service.

3. There are many small  unclear points. For example:
1. The menu pictures show white plates  but the pictures on each table show a black bottom. The pictures should show a plain white plate  not a black object as a backdrop.
2. The pictures on the tables don't show the orange sticks  but there are orange sticks on the conveyor belt. How much are they?
3. The tofu skin on skewers... I know they're 10฿ each  but the shape keeps changing. 1. It's served in sheets. What's strange is  if tofu skin on a stick is 10฿ and you get 4 for 40฿  then they sell 4 pieces of tofu skin on a white plate for 50฿. It's very confusing. Who would buy tofu skin on a plate like that?
4. I saw a sign that said "Payment via scan only." After scanning  the employee takes a screenshot of the receipt as proof.
This shows the owner doesn't trust employees to handle cash. The owner checks the money through the account to match the screenshot from the employee.
And I also saw a sign that said if an employee clocks in and then goes for a walk or smokes  100฿ will be deducted from their pay.
This shows that their mind is only focused on numbers  money  and business. They don't think about work relationships  helping each other  or relaxation. It's no wonder that other branches have been failing one after another.
In today's business  taste is secondary. Service and customer care are paramount.
Personally  I won't go back because the price and service are unreasonable. For the same price  I could go to Tee Noi or Mama Chiang for more filling food and better service. If the business owner doesn't understand the habits of both Thai and foreign customers  it's hard to survive. Chinda Hotpot Porto Chino The food is delicious and very fresh. Chinda Hotpot Udomsuk 58  Chinda Hotpot Udomsuk 58 I recommend anyone coming to this restaurant to always ask for the price before getting ripped off with extra charges. I've been confused by their pricing three times now. Their customer service is practically non-existent.
1. The first time  we ate the buffet. We asked how much it was  and the staff said 299 baht  not including refills. Refills were 39 baht. We asked if we could skip the drinks since it was just a buffet  and they said yes. We thought  "Wow  that's fairer than other places " because we usually don't drink water. And when it came time to pay  they really didn't include drinks.
2. The second time  we ate right away. When it came time to pay  we said we didn't drink water  so they shouldn't charge for it. Again  the staff said no  it had to include drinks. We paid because we thought the first time was wrong.
3. Lately  we've been ordering à la carte instead because my boyfriend eats less but wants shaved ice. We asked if ordering refillable drinks included ice cream and shaved ice  and the staff said yes. After that  we always ordered à la carte + refills  and we've even gotten two free pots of soup and are about to get a third.
BUT!!! This last time  the staff charged us for the ice cream separately! We argued that our regular bill included refills of water  but the employee  with an innocent look  insisted it was 29 baht per scoop. We were confused  wondering why this wasn't mentioned before. We thought it had always been the same. She said it had always been like this. So what about the past? We paid anyway. When we left  we forgot to stamp our loyalty card  so my girlfriend went to get it. She asked why there was an extra charge for ice cream. The employee told her she got 2 scoops for 29 baht  which is the usual price. But she had just told me it was 29 baht per scoop! I told her the truth: I ate 3 scoops and my girlfriend ate one shaved ice dessert  and they charged me 87 baht! So what about the 2 scoops for 29 baht my girlfriend got? Doesn't that mean it should have been 58 baht? The employee said that the buffet + refill option includes free ice cream  but the à la carte + refill option doesn't. They didn't inform us of this before we ate. As regular customers  we thought it was always the same.

Can I report this to the Department of Internal Trade?
(Regarding the Price Control Act B.E. 2542  Section 28)

Can I report this to the Consumer Protection Board? The price doesn't match what was stated/the standard isn't clear. Changing the terms and conditions without notification???

restaurant name: Chon Som at Courtyard by Marriott Suvarnabhumi Airport
text: Chon Som at Courtyard by Marriott Suvarnabhumi Airport I had a buffet dinner on Christmas Day. Although the spread and choice of selection fell short of my expectation  the price  taste and quality of the food somehow made up for it. The lack of grilled giant river prawns or lobsters gave me the opportunity to explore the desserts which I normally skip when I take buffet. Surprisingly  the pastries  Chendol and ice-cream were good. The choice of sashimi cuts were good. The baked salmon was also one of my favourites.  The wonderful service and the soothing atmosphere somewhat elevated the overall dining experience. One thing to note  however  is the location of this place  which is rather far from the city center and the road in front of the Hotel is jammed during peak hours. I came for a Saturday afternoon and there were many empty tables.
But as soon as I sat down  the restaurant was full.
The staff taking orders were attentive and provided complete details 
including menu details and promotions.
The food didn't take long to arrive; it started arriving shortly after ordering.

During our visit  there was a Friday-Saturday promotion for 1 250 baht per person. It was a buy 4 get 3 free offer  making it 937 baht per person.

The Sunday promotion  however  did not include river prawns  lamb ribs  or grouper and was 650 baht per person.
Personally  I think it was very good value. It's a buffet I'll definitely be back for.

Cielo Sky Bar & Restaurant Rooftop Don’t come here on weekends or during special events  or if you don’t come at all and find another place even better  very expensive with poor service!!

We came for dinner during valentine and placed our order at 10:00 PM. The food arrived 1 hour and 30 minutes later!! When we asked about the delay  the explanation was that they were busy because of Valentine’s Day.
Well  with respect  that’s not the customer’s problem.

What made it even more confusing was that many tables were still empty  so it didn’t look fully booked.

Some of the staff were also not very friendly. There was no real apology for the long wait and no attempt to offer anything to make up for it. Only after I clearly showed that I was annoyed did the food finally arrive  with a quick “sorry.”

This was actually my third visit. The previous times were fine  probably because they were on weekdays. Unfortunately  this experience completely changed my mind - it will be my last.

If you can’t handle the number of guests  then why accept the bookings?

I chose not to argue at the restaurant because I didn’t want to create a scene  but the experience was disappointing and far below expectations. Cielo Sky Bar & Restaurant Rooftop AWFUL FOOD.

Booked for Valentine’s Day dinner  paid almost 14 000 baht for 2 people as a set meal.

Live music was good  service was good…. The food was genuinely some of the worst food  if not the number 1 worst food I’ve ever had at a restaurant.

Bland  un-fresh  strange dish choices  everything from start to finish was a 2/3 out of 10 in terms of food.

Shame as the view and service was nice. Won’t be coming back! The drinks were really good and natural tasting. The food I wasnt bad but not what I would  expect in such an amazing place  but don’t let that put you off  this place is expecting yous any a romantic experience. Coriander Leaf @ Singapore Polo Club This place has got it all; great service staff  attentive and knowledgeable; great food that is customizable; and the ambience of the restaurant in an open polo field!!!
Before the food descriptions  just want to highlight they are very versatile and honest about what they can do. I'm vegetarian and lactose-intolerant  and they have fully impressed me!
I was introduced to this place by a relative of mine  and I'm so glad that I explored it. We went in a party of 4 with the following request; no strong chilli nor spicy  no milk  and we are all vegetarians.
They came up with this incredible menu:
1 mock meat tandori; 1 jack fruit curry; 1 vegetable dumpling; 1 falalfal; 1 stirfried Dou Miao; 1 grilled eggplant; and 1 fried okra in batter!!! Yeah  it is that incredible  I'm serious. And this is NOT a vegetarian restaurant!
The food is to die-for:
-Jackfruit in curry paste  this is phenomenal! The fruit is firm and the curry paste is full bodied and aromatic.
-Steamed dumpling in light chilli paste  the chilli is very light  but the dumpling is soft and juicy.
-Dou Miao is soft and fresh  simply fried with garlic. Beautiful!
-Falalfal  this is phenomenal because it is slightly moist and the frangrance is awsome
-The okra in batter is nice chewy not tough and laced with a sweet sauce
-The eggplant is lightly grilled or fried with garlic  slightly charred which gives a nice aroma
I can go on  but I think you get it now  this is an awesome place to have a slow dinner and talk.
The ambience is great  as we were seated facing the polo field and the night was slightly breezy. So you get mild lighting and great food. All you need to do is to eat slowly!!!
Make a reservation now. Thank me later. Coriander Leaf @ Singapore Polo Club  Coriander Leaf @ Singapore Polo Club Really amazing food and service. Garlic naan shiok as hell. Coriander rice is mindblowing. The cumin lamb was A++++. Our server (1 Feb  7pm)  Chinese girl with brown hair and spectacles  was really attentive and helpful with lots of initiative. Great work! Coriander Leaf @ Singapore Polo Club It felt great to be back and the menu had been updated! But hey  the signature beef rendang and asam pedas were still there  and they were absolutely delicious! The food was fresh and really high quality. Make sure to say hi to James - he makes the whole dining experience awesome! restaurant name: Crab Wang Imperial Dining At Hotel Boss
text:
Crab Wang Imperial Dining At Hotel Boss Finally found a custard crab near a restaurant (usually served with chili and black pepper).

Thanks to Meituan (surprisingly  you can even buy Meituan coupons in Singapore – this meal was about 68% off) – paid in RMB with my phone  and paid the difference by card.

Meituan's navigation function is also great.

I might not have explained my order clearly (the bill said not too wet).

Compared to previous meals  the batter coating was too thick  and the crab meat near the edges and legs was too dry.

Probably fried for too long or reduced for too long.

However  the crab meat was very fresh  and the total price after the Meituan discount was very reasonable ❤️

Since I was dining alone  I couldn't order any other dishes  but I was very satisfied to be able to eat such a large crab ☺️ Crab Wang Imperial Dining At Hotel Boss Staff are nice and quick to correct the wrong order they dine  They even made the wrong menu for free. I appreciate that. Crab Wang Imperial Dining At Hotel Boss We visited on last Wednesday night. Currently they are running 1 crab at $25  2 crabs at $45 & 3 crabs at $60. Decided to come here to try out their Black Pepper Crab  didn't really put much expectation as we purely have cravings to eat Black Pepper Crab. Turn out it exceeds our expectations. The crab meat was fresh  easily off the shell  and the Black Pepper gravy was flavourful  with genrous amount of onion to perfect the sauce. We requested to have more gravy for our Black Pepper crab (wet version) instead of drier version and they have done it perfectly for us  although the sauce can be a little spicier to have that kick.

Nonetheless  overall it was really a pleasant visit. We ordered 3 crabs and 1 fried rice with pork cutlet. Didn't expect the the crab was quite meaty  despite they mention it's 400g  to us it's more like 500g above. It really worth the value!

Our bill was about $80+. 10% GST but no service charge 👍🏻

Not to forget the lady staff attended to us was very friendly and nice  and was very attentive as well. Helping us to put on the disposable apron and gloves. And the ambience was really comfortable too!

Highly recommended 💯 Crab Wang Imperial Dining At Hotel Boss We tried the famous Singaporean dish  chili crab. The ingredients were fresh  the portions generous and full of flavor. The atmosphere was pleasant and the staff very friendly. A truly great place to remember! Crab Wang Imperial Dining At Hotel Boss Dishes were good but the prawns were undercooked. The entire property is of the highest standard  and the rooms are beautiful  stylish  and perfectly maintained. But what makes this place truly special are the PEOPLE. Watching the sun go down from the property was one of the most magical parts of our stay. Cross Pratamnak Staycation (Pattaya) The staff member named James provided excellent service. Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Staycation  Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Staycation Das Hotel liegt wirklich hervorragend. So gut  dass man sich fast fragt  ob man überhaupt noch ein Taxi braucht – einmal stolpern und man steht mitten im Geschehen. Dafür gibt es ehrlich Pluspunkte.

Das Personal ist ausgesprochen freundlich. Egal zu welcher Tageszeit  man wird mit einem Lächeln begrüßt – vermutlich auch dann noch  wenn man nachts um drei beschließt  sich ein Glas Wasser zu organisieren.

Das Frühstück ist richtig gut. Große Auswahl  alles frisch und lecker. Hier wird man ernsthaft in Versuchung geführt  mehr zu essen als gesellschaftlich akzeptabel ist. Allein dafür lohnt sich der Aufenthalt schon fast.

Das Zimmer war gut und absolut in Ordnung. Sauber  ordentlich  alles da  was man braucht. Kein unnötiger Schnickschnack – angenehm.

Jetzt zum Fitnessraum: Er überzeugt durch sein minimalistisches Konzept. Wer schon immer der Meinung war  dass weniger mehr ist  wird hier philosophisch abgeholt. Wenige Gewichte  überschaubare Geräteauswahl – man wird kreativ! Kniebeugen mit Koffer? Warum nicht. Es fühlt sich ein bisschen an wie „Fitnessraum – Compact Edition“.

Der Pool ist ebenfalls ein Highlight – allerdings eher im Sinne von „Wie viele Menschen passen realistisch in eine größere Badewanne?“. Für die Anzahl der Gäste ist er… nennen wir es intim. Wenn man mit drei anderen Personen gleichzeitig drin ist  kennt man sich spätestens nach fünf Minuten beim Vornamen. Schwimmen ist schwierig  aber man kann hervorragend stehen und sich fragen  ob das jetzt Aquafitness oder ein Gesellschaftsspiel ist.

Und dann die Massage. Ein Erlebnis für sich. Die Masseurin arbeitet mit einer Intensität  bei der man kurz überlegt  ob sie heimlich eine Ausbildung im Knochenrichten absolviert hat. Ich war mir zeitweise nicht sicher  ob ich gerade entspannt werde oder auf meine strukturelle Belastbarkeit getestet werde. Tiefengewebe? Definitiv. Sehr tief. Sehr sehr tief.
Der Preis ist dabei genauso kräftig wie der Druck – da spürt man die Wirkung nicht nur im Rücken  sondern auch im Geldbeutel.

Fazit: Tolle Lage  super Frühstück  freundliches Personal – kombiniert mit einem Fitnessraum im Minimalformat  einem Pool im Badewannen-Design und einer Massage  die man so schnell nicht vergisst. Unterhaltung inklusive. Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Staycation Cream was very kind at check in  explained everything well. Great start to our trip 💕

We had a massage at the spa  was so relaxing! Koi really kind and really an amazing masseuse 🤗 Cross Vibe Bangkok Sukhumvit Staycation Noi did my massage and it was brilliant!

I really enjoyed it and will return 😃

restaurant name: Crossroads Buffet at Singapore Marriott Tang Plaza Hotel
text:
Crossroads Buffet at Singapore Marriott Tang Plaza Hotel A massive disappointment  especially if you’ve experienced the high standards of a Las Vegas buffet. Compared to the variety and quality you get in Vegas  the spread here felt incredibly limited and uninspired. The selection was underwhelming for the price. Definitely not worth the trip  save your money for a better dining experience. Crossroads Buffet at Singapore Marriott Tang Plaza Hotel The staffs are very fast and handling the empty plates. I like to highlight one of the Waiter  John for being so nice and attentive to our needs. The other staffs are very good too.

Ok let's comment on the food. In today's competitive buffet market  the food serve here feel like 5 years ago.

Same old style  same old food. Which i am perfectly fine with it BUT the lamb is horrible - hard and not chewable. The crab has a strong mud taste. Using slipper lobster instead of bigger lobster here? The cheese platter with 3 cheese only? Your breakfast cheese has a better selection than that! Rojak for buffet? Your cakes uses same "template" and style to do? All the same style and no creative in it?

I am sorry to be so mean here but as a Platinum Elite member of marriot for 8 years  I have ate at many Marriot buffet  this us perhaps one of the most disappointing one. Crossroads Buffet at Singapore Marriott Tang Plaza Hotel Marriott Crossroads buffet is one of the most well-known buffets in Singapore and easily accessible. My only complaint is for this price there many things missing. For example  there are no oysters  no sashimi  and they don’t even have simple vanilla or chocolate ice cream. Compare compared to other buffets that have improved a lot  Marriott really needs to work harder to improve their menu. Crossroads Buffet at Singapore Marriott Tang Plaza Hotel Food is acceptable but too expensive for the price. Table too small for 4 as it is meant comfortably for 2. restaurant name: Crystal Grill 82nd floor at Baiyoke Sky Hotel
text:
Crystal Grill 82nd floor at Baiyoke Sky Hotel We visited between 11:00 AM and 1:00 PM on a Sunday in February 2026. It wasn't crowded; there were about 5 large families sitting comfortably spread out.
We had reserved parking  and the security guard directed us with our license plates.

The staff were attentive.

The food was freshly prepared  except for the fried items which weren't very crispy.

The shabu-shabu broth was rich and flavorful.
The raw salmon wasn't the sashimi grade you'd find at other Japanese restaurants; it was a bit chewy.
The truffle mushroom soup was fragrant and rich.

There was also a Joker-themed staff member making fancy balloons for the children. Crystal Grill 82nd floor at Baiyoke Sky Hotel Buffet Price: 1550฿ (Dinner)

🦐Premium Menu: Focuses on premium seafood and steaks  such as grilled river prawns (large)  scallops  Wagyu beef steak  lamb steak  sushi  and international dishes including Chinese (Peking duck  roasted suckling pig)  and Japanese.

🗼City View: The restaurant's high glass windows offer stunning views of Bangkok.

🌃Free Access to the Observation Deck: This price includes access to the observation deck on the 77th floor (observation tower) and the rotating rooftop on the 84th floor. Crystal Grill 82nd floor at Baiyoke Sky Hotel It tastes good. Crystal Grill 82nd floor at Baiyoke Sky Hotel We went there during Christmas.
4 people  7 000 baht.

Overall 9/10
The food selection might not be that diverse  but the quality was excellent  especially the grilled prawns and the grill section.
My husband ate 20 prawns  and my grandfather ate 4 plates of Lamb Chops (the lamb was exceptionally well-prepared). We were incredibly full! 🥹
Everyone got to choose one drink from the rooftop bar – unlimited choices – which was great value.

The atmosphere and view were just okay. If you're looking for ambiance  this might not be the best choice. The distance between tables is quite narrow (in some areas)  making it difficult to get up to get food.

Other than that  everything was perfect. We would definitely come back again if we wanted to eat there. Crystal Grill 82nd floor at Baiyoke Sky Hotel Not worth it at all 
The selection was very very limited  unfortunately I would not recommend it.

Dino Mookrata Rama 3 Dino Moo Kata is one of my favorite restaurants  a place I've been frequenting since before it had air conditioning. Now it's bigger and more comfortable  but the taste remains consistently excellent. The dipping sauce is fantastic – perfectly balanced and you can even customize it yourself. It pairs perfectly with the well-marinated  tender  and delicious pork; you barely need the sauce! The ingredients are high-quality  making it suitable for both serious Moo Kata lovers and those looking to relax  as they've now added a spicy salad menu.

There's seating both outside and inside  and you can order individual dishes or sets  catering to your style. It's perfect for groups of friends to enjoy a long  relaxed meal in a friendly atmosphere.

A minor drawback is limited parking; you'll likely have to find parking on the street near the restaurant. Overall  it's worth it – the prices are friendly  and the taste lives up to my expectations since its inception.

Dolcetto at Conrad Singapore Orchard I made a last-minute call to order a cherry cake for collection the next day between 3pm to 5pm  and I arrived at around 4pm. The cake itself deserves five stars-well balanced in taste and thoroughly enjoyable. Unfortunately  the service experience fell short.

I informed the staff member that my card needed to be inserted into the machine for payment and handed it to her properly  holding it with my thumb and index finger. I did not toss or slide the card toward her. However  she took my card between her index and middle fingers and  after the transaction  placed it on the folder instead of returning it directly to me.

This gesture felt discourteous and inconsistent with the service standards one would expect from a five-star resort. While the product was excellent  the service did not reflect the same level of professionalism. Dolcetto at Conrad Singapore Orchard Surprisingly good Italian fare at this small grocer located on the ground floor of the hotel  the seafood stew was one of the best I have tried and every ingredient was fresh Dolcetto at Conrad Singapore Orchard Great pastries and sandwiches  also serves Illy Cold Brew  a rare find in Singapore. Dolcetto at Conrad Singapore Orchard The coffee and cakes were very good  though the cakes were a little on the sweeter side  sweet enough that I wished they had toned it down just a notch. Still  it’s a comfortable spot for catching up with friends or enjoying some quiet solo time.

The cake-ordering experience  however  was an adventure. Despite no one being at the counter before me  what should have been a quick order turned into a spectator sport. After taking down my order  the staff suddenly became extremely interested in serving every male customer who walked in after me. I basically watched a whole queue form  order  and pay before I got to pay for my own cake.

To add to the fun  the staff seemed permanently glued to their devices  only noticing customers after a polite eternity of waiting. And the cherry on top? No smiles  no greetings  unless you screamed a dramatic "Buongiorno!" like you were auditioning for an Italian opera. Strangely  it worked wonders for the men who came in after me. Dolcetto at Conrad Singapore Orchard Excellent coffee and super friendly staff

text: Dream Premium Korean BBQ Buffet Ruea-bin - 드림 코리안 비비큐  Dream Premium Korean BBQ Buffet Ruea-bin - 드림 코리안 비비큐 I recently ate at the Wat Sriwaninoy branch. The restaurant was very dirty  but I didn't mind because I was planning to go there based on positive reviews on Facebook. However  I was very disappointed. The appetizers were very dry and there were only a few options. The pork seemed like leftovers  chilled for ages  with only 4-5 pieces of each item. text:

If you’re a seafood lover like me  you need to check out the "Seafood Vibes" buffet at Dusit Gourmet  dusitD2 Samyan. It’s a once-a-month dinner event—only on the first Wednesday of every month  and the next one is coming up on June 4th. I had the chance to experience it and honestly… I’m still thinking about it!

✨ Highlights of the night ✨
▪ A massive seafood spread 🦀 – prawns  mantis shrimp  crabs  and more  all super fresh and perfectly paired with a spicy Thai seafood sauce
▪ Oyster lovers rejoice! 🐚 They had oysters  mussels  clams  scallops  blood cockles  razor clams—you name it
▪ Thai favorites 🌶️ – the oyster salad and som tum were fresh  bold  and packed with flavor
▪ Japanese & Korean corner 🍣 – sushi  sashimi  soy-marinated crabs & shrimp. Absolutely addictive
▪ Fusion dishes 🥩 – grilled meats  steaks  pasta. A great mix if you want something beyond seafood
▪ Dessert heaven 🍰 – cakes  ice cream  and a full bakery spread
▪ Drinks were plentiful  and the live band made the whole evening even more enjoyable 🎶🍸

💖 If you're looking for a premium international buffet with a great vibe  quality food  and relaxing music  I highly recommend Seafood Vibes at Dusit Gourmet  dusitD2 Samyan. restaurant name: Earw Thai Suki Boran Seacon Square Srinagarindra
text:
Earw Thai Suki Boran Seacon Square Srinagarindra This is my favorite traditional sukiyaki restaurant.

The sukiyaki is served with a traditional sauce  a side of tender marinated meat  and a flavorful broth that's not bland – I personally love it! It's delicious.

The restaurant also does other dishes well. We ordered steamed sea bass with soy sauce; the fish was tender  delicious  and fresh.

The Peking duck was on sale for just a few hundred baht – and it tasted great!
It's just as good as the famous restaurants. Earw Thai Suki Boran Seacon Square Srinagarindra The service at this branch is excellent and very friendly.
Even without the buffet  the food is delicious and great value.
Especially the crispy-skinned duck served with pancakes and vegetables – it's seriously delicious  on par with hotel ducks. The stir-fried duck meat is also fantastic; it's plentiful  tender  and not chewy – truly impressive.
The sukiyaki is also excellent  a guaranteed highlight.
I highly recommend trying the duck at this restaurant! Earw Thai Suki Boran Seacon Square Srinagarindra The food was delicious and flavorful. The sukiyaki dipping sauce was amazing. The duck was tender. Highly recommended. Earw Thai Suki Boran Seacon Square Srinagarindra Everything was delicious. Nong Lily provided excellent service. restaurant name: Edge at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore
text:
Edge at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore Had an awesome CNY buffet dinner at Edge las Saturday. Impressive food variety especially at the dessert station. Loved the durian desserts most.
It would be excellent if the staff can be more smiley. Edge at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore Never thought I'll leave a bad review here but most my collegues felt the same too . Of all the years we celebrated company dinners   this has got to be the worst . One example ... I was at the spagetti counter when I saw a couple of cooked ones ready to be taken .... i do not see numbers on the plates or anything/notice that indicated that they were waiting someone to collect . Last I know this is a buffet resturant ... I approached the aunty cook behind the section & asked if I could have 1 . Shockingly all I get was . " Cannot   you need to order   these are customers " Then she turned & continued her loud conversations with her cooks like she owns the place ... well maybe she does as she looks a fierce senior . But most of us are there to celebrate CNY . No matter how senior you are   she could have been taught to respond with kinder words or even directed me on the ordering process . I left that counter wondering why I asked for a spagetti .... what had happened to this resturant after all these years of high service standard . This ordering process is poor & will cause embarrassment to diners if its not done right by those cooks that are untrained in handling customers . My collegues also felt the same . We will consider somewhere else in 2027 if you think your restaurant is indispensable . If you charged a premier price   at least have some basic trainning for these cooks behind the counter ... they may often feel they have a certain authority over customers and forgot we are merely there to celebrate & have a good time . I really wish they do something about these lousy invisible ordering process . 2 plates of spagetti .... " these are for customers" LOL ... I am not trying to steal the spagetti la   just hoping you teach me.how to get one . Such pity all good food reviews damaged by some untrained staff . This should not be a norm in my opinion   at least not anywhere that provide buffet services . Edge at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore We were served by auntie Ivy who is super nice and friendly! She’s so hardworking and inspiring:) love the variety & freshness of foods. To be honest  this may be one of the buffet that I went which I don’t have anything I don’t like! Shoutout to the fast replenishment of foods  great place for gathering! Edge at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore Great food.. Edge at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore I had weekday dinner during CNY period and the bill came up to 120 bucks per pax after 15% discount with UOB card. It was an utter waste of money. Ingredients used were inferior for a buffet at this pricepoint and cooking was awful. The laksa too sweet  suckling pig too salty with no usual sweet sauce to balance out  briyani rice too oily  baked oysters too salty  calamari and chicken dishes were of the frozen variety  fried nian gao had putrid reused oil smell  sashimi was dried out from being prepared way ahead. Things got so bad I was picking on cucumber slices n salad greens so as not to be disappointed. Even the coffee and tea inclusive of the buffet was watered down compared to other similar buffets. Service was spotty. With the exception of one friendly waitress  the rest tried to avoid eye contact (and hence service). Tables and chairs were very worn out. Only saving grace was the dessert section especially if one is a chocolate lover though ice cream was way too sweet. I would say to AVOID and take your money and tummy elsewhere. Their  pre Covid hey days are long gone. Special thanks to the chef for the fantastic dishes  and to the lovely owner for the warm service and explanations. Element At Amara Bangkok I am staying at the Amara Hotel with my partner. We wanted to dine in house. The atmosphere was nice  but the AC was set so low that even as an European we were freezing.

My main dish was served ~25min after my partners. When it arrived  she was already done eating. The supposedly self cooked (chef recommended) noodles  seemed suspiciously like super-market ware and came severely undercooked. The tiger prawns were not cleaned of feces  but in fact had a big pile of it. Disgusting. The sauce was tasty  but it couldn't save the otherwise catastrophic meal. To top it all  there was a small catapillar on the salat.

If I had to guess  I would assume that they couldnt fulfil the order  but instead of telling us that it was unavailable  they sent an employee to grab noodles at the 7/11 and rushed the whole meal  which would explain the delay in the meal  the undercooked 7/11-style noodles  as well as the improper cleaning of prawns and salat.

The only saving grace for the restaurant are the friendly waiters. Either way  wewill definitely not eat dinner here again during our stay. Note that at the time we arrived in the restaurant there was only one other couple  so it was definitely not rush-hour for the kitchen. Know we know why. Elements, Inspired by Ciel Bleu at The Okura Prestige Bangkok  Elements, Inspired by Ciel Bleu at The Okura Prestige Bangkok I ordered the 7-course menu  and one of the “courses” was simply a small  standard loaf of bread. It’s difficult to understand how this qualifies as a course in a tasting menu.

I felt compelled to leave a review mainly because of the main course: the Wagyu A5 striploin. What I was served was almost entirely fat  with barely any meat. This is not the level of quality one would expect from a Michelin-rated establishment. Nonetheless 
when I raised the issue with the staff  they offered to prepare a replacement. However  by that point it was already late  and as the dishes take a long time to prepare  I declined.

Regarding the service  it was mediocre. The first staff member did not explain anything (although  the second did). The ambience was also disappointing. If you dine as a pair  you are seated near the window  but there is no view as it is  blocked by shrubs and trees.

To be fair  some of the other dishes were delicious  and the dessert was enjoyable. Unfortunately  these positives were not enough to make up for the overall shortcomings  especially considering the price and the restaurant’s reputation.

FLNT I had a reservation booked the day before and when I arrived it was very quiet for a Sunday afternoon. However  the table that was reserved for me didn’t seem to be fully prepped before my arrival… no napkins  no glasses. We had our full high tea session without any of the servers noticing 😕 I’ve tried the get the attention of the servers a few times but they don’t seem to be attentive at all. Like I said  it wasn’t busy  there were only 3-4 tables filled at the time  so this poor service is unjustifiable.

When our high tea arrived  the server  with a very blank expression  attempted to introduce each savouries and sweets to us but only introduced 3 of them  stopped and left 😂 I mean… I don’t mind if it wasn’t introduced in the first place… I’ve seen the menu so I know exactly what we’re having  but witnessing such half-assed service was quite amusing. We left the restaurant feeling very dissatisfied. This was meant to be a nice treat for us as we don’t go to these posh places very often. We didn’t need to be treated exceptionally  just expecting the bare minimum customer service in F&B. FLNT We held our wedding at 1-Altico  so returning for Valentine’s Day carried meaningful expectations. When a venue positions itself at a premium price point  consistency  professionalism  and polished execution are not optional — they are baseline expectations.

Unfortunately  the experience fell noticeably short.

Service standards appeared inconsistent and  at times  poorly coordinated. Several team members seemed insufficiently trained and uncertain of their responsibilities. More concerning was the dynamic observed between senior staff and part-time employees. Instructions were delivered in a manner that appeared unnecessarily harsh  and at one point  part-timers were made to stand outside the restroom area in what looked like a public reprimand. Witnessing this created discomfort and detracted from the overall dining atmosphere.

Internal management culture inevitably reflects outwardly. When staff appear tense  unsupported  or inadequately prepared  guests feel it. The ambience that evening felt strained and disorganised — far from the refined experience one would expect at this price level.

The venue itself has strong potential  and our wedding experience was the reason we returned. However  the operational standards and leadership oversight on this occasion did not align with the brand’s positioning. For a premium establishment  professionalism and team culture must match the pricing.

Disappointing  especially given the expectations attached to the venue. FLNT We definitely enjoyed our stay at Atico Lounge. We didn’t expect to be seated with the view of the Marina Bay Sands  and that made the experience a lot better. We loved the food and how they were presented. What stood out to us was how Rana and the other girl provided service. We were also surprised when we were given complimentary cake slice in celebration of our anniversary. We’ll surely be back with our friends. FLNT Food service and view are all amazing!! Seafood ramen is so umami and the tiger prawns and scallops are so fresh. Sashimi is thick and tender. Five Crossing Eatery & Winery  Five Crossing Eatery & Winery  Five Crossing Eatery & Winery **Five Crossing** is an Italian gem that offers a memorable dining experience. The restaurant is known for its wood oven pizza  which delivers a perfect blend of crispy and chewy crusts topped with fresh  flavorful ingredients. Each bite is a delightful reminder of why traditional methods still reign supreme in pizza making.

The tiramisu is another highlight at Five Crossing. This classic Italian dessert is executed with finesse  featuring layers of delicate ladyfingers soaked in coffee  creamy mascarpone  and a dusting of cocoa that leaves you craving more.

For those seeking heartier fare  the pork chop is a must-try. Cooked to perfection  it's juicy  tender  and seasoned just right  offering a satisfying depth of flavor. The salmon is equally impressive  showcasing the chef's skill in preparing seafood with a light  flaky texture and a deliciously seasoned crust.

restaurant name: Food Exchange Restaurant at Novotel Bangkok Sukhumvit 20
text:
Food Exchange Restaurant at Novotel Bangkok Sukhumvit 20 2026.1
12:00~15:00 Only lunch was consumed using Eatigo.
Overall  it was okay  but the price exceeded my expectations.
A very filling meal was available for around 400 THB.
They had freshly cooked pasta and pancakes (if you don't know how to make them  you can ask the chef to make them for you). There were approximately ten dishes: stir-fried dishes  red curry  seafood curry  cold dishes  fried food  Japanese sushi  sweet white shrimp and mussels  pizza  juice  Coke  Sprite  etc. (soft drinks are not charged separately)  mashed potatoes  fruit  Thai desserts  etc. You can eat until 3 PM  and they don't rush customers; you can stay until about 3:30 PM. It's a very good value restaurant  but the environment needs improvement. There were quite a few flies and small insects  and you had to keep shooing them away while eating. I hope the restaurant can improve this. Food Exchange Restaurant at Novotel Bangkok Sukhumvit 20 Great salads  avocado or pomelo  yummy wagyu beef  outstanding wine sauce  BBQ sauce  tomato sauce...

Fu Lin Bar & Kitchen (Clarke Quay) Last night my wife and I stumbled on this restaurant  after passing it over earlier for not having any recent reviews  but from the look and sounds from the live music we gave it a chance. One immediate benefit we hadn't noticed was their happy hour extended to 9pm  when the majority of places end at 8pm ( in general  we have quickly learnt that  happy hours are usually from midday to 8pm) So we took full advantage and ordered their house wine option of 2 large glasses for 22SGD  one red and one white. both were very nice. We ordered the Premium beef in a spicy broth with rice and the Red snapper with potatoes and veg. We were warned that the beef was very spicy and was that ok? I'm glad we agreed to calm it down  as the amount of chillies (which were easy to remove  once confirmed they were fire hot) were still too much for my taste. However once these were removed it was delicious and I finished it.

The live music was very good indeed and really set a perfect background for the evening. The staff were very attentive and friendly.

GingerBowl Cafe I was truly amazed! The broth of this noodle soup was incredibly rich and flavorful  with a perfect balance of sour and spicy. One sip and you could taste the complex layers of spices – it was just as good as any trendy noodle shop. The noodles were chewy and springy  soaking up the broth beautifully. Topped with large chunks of fresh shrimp  squid  and crispy fried pork skin  every bite was incredibly satisfying. The best part was the lime slice; squeezing it in instantly brightened the entire bowl of noodles  making it refreshing and appetizing. A small dish of dry noodles was also served on the side  with a rich and flavorful sauce – it was delicious even when mixed in. The best part? No queue! Being able to sit down and eat comfortably in the crowded night market was pure bliss. The prices were also very reasonable  less than 200 baht per person  yet the taste was just as good. If you don't want to be stuck in the crowds at Jeh O Chula  this is definitely the best budget alternative. Highly recommended for anyone craving authentic Mama noodles! GingerBowl Cafe We ordered stir-fried chicken noodles  chicken wonton noodles  and braised fish maw in red sauce. Every dish was delicious  especially the stir-fried chicken noodles – not too greasy  not burnt  and perfectly seasoned. GingerBowl Cafe Stopped by for some dessert. If you've never been here before  the place is like a rabbit warren  but the staff are always friendly and attentive and will find you wherever you end up! Would be rude not to take some of their signature desserts home with you if you don't have room to eat any while you're there. They taste as good as they look. Ginpao Phochana Delicious
Clean
Attentive service
No complaints Ginpao Phochana This Chinese restaurant in Lotus mall has a quiet atmosphere and a steady stream of customers.
The food tasted great and was very much to my liking. The sweet and sour prawns were perfectly balanced  with fresh  large prawns coated in batter before stir-frying. The salt and pepper fried squid was a bit salty  perfect as an appetizer  and the squid wasn't chewy at all – excellent! The fish and cabbage soup was slightly spicy  with a nice touch of sourness from the cabbage  making the broth easy to drink. The most surprising dish was the Jinbao spiced fried lamb ribs – it was amazing! There was no gamey smell at all  the batter was thin and crispy  and the lamb meat was incredibly tender. No dipping sauce was needed  but they did provide some chili powder (probably mixed with spices) which added a nice aroma and flavor.
The Szechuan soup was spicy; I requested it extra spicy  and they made it similar to fish maw soup  which was good for cutting through the richness.

The staff were friendly  helpful  and provided good service.

Parking is available at the Phichaiyathi Building  which has ample space. The restaurant stamps your parking ticket for 2 hours of free parking. Ginpao Phochana The food is generally of high quality; the Sichuan cuisine is spicy and satisfying with generous portions  although some dishes are a bit salty and need to be eaten with rice. The service is courteous and tea is frequently refilled. The environment is clean and has a Chinese ambiance  and parking is convenient. Prices are affordable  ranging from 300-800 baht per person  suitable for both lunch and dinner  and ideal for large groups. Ginpao Phochana  Ginpao Phochana Everything is delicious. The staff provides excellent service. The shop is located in Lotus Yaowarat.

Today  I'm taking you to a spicy restaurant with a very clear name: "Eat Chili" 🌶️ If you love spicy food  you'll definitely love it.

The dishes we ordered included stir-fried Gnetum leaves with eggs  which was so good with hot rice – I couldn't stop eating!

Stir-fried lotus stems with shrimp  mildly sweet and spicy  with fresh  bouncy shrimp in every bite.

Finally  grilled fatty beef with dipping sauce – so good! The highlight was the fried rice with grilled pork neck – so delicious!

The atmosphere is relaxed  with simple wooden tables  a homey feel  but they're very generous with the curry spices. If you like strong Thai flavors  you have to come here.

restaurant name: Giorgio's Riverside Royal Orchid Sheraton Hotel
text:
Giorgio's Riverside Royal Orchid Sheraton Hotel Had clams pasta (overcooked and tasteless) and a pizza (gummy). I'm Italian and quite picky about Italian food but plenty of restaurants in BKK do way better.
Prices are clearly high as we are in a 5 star hotel but funnily I found on the menu a white wine that I regularly buy from a local retailer. It's s great Lugana and you should try it.
I normally buy one bottle from the retailer at around 600 bht incl. tax and delivery. A (small) glass at Giorgio's was 360 bht plus tax and service charge ( if memory serves 22%). Do your maths 🙂
Finally hard to get a riverside table if one does not book long in advance Giorgio's Riverside Royal Orchid Sheraton Hotel  Giorgio's Riverside Royal Orchid Sheraton Hotel The shop is beautiful  the atmosphere is great  the staff provides good service  and the food and desserts taste delicious. Giorgio's Riverside Royal Orchid Sheraton Hotel January 1  2026
I wanted to enjoy a relaxing dinner to wrap up my Bangkok trip  so I decided to go to this restaurant at the Royal Orchid Sheraton  where I was staying.

I ordered a four-course outdoor romantic set with sparkling wine via Hungry Hub for about ¥12 000.

The atmosphere was riverside  but it felt more casual than romantic. The staff was busy setting up the table.

However  after two delicious dishes arrived  nothing else arrived  which seemed strange.

It turns out they had forgotten about it. 🥹
After I ordered the third dish  it didn't arrive until 40 minutes later. I was there for two hours. I got bitten by a lot of mosquitoes. 💦
As an apology  I received a glass of sparkling wine each.

The main dish 
ROASTED BEEF TENDERLOIN ROSSINI  was excellent  as were the squid linguine and cold smoked black salmon crostino.

The food was delicious  but the service was disappointing.
I'll come again  so please improve it. Giorgio's Riverside Royal Orchid Sheraton Hotel The staff provide excellent service  speak politely  and are very professional.
The food comes out quickly  and just the view alone is worth it.
The prices are good value for money  and you can take photos in every corner. text:
Goji Kitchen Grill & Bar at Pattaya Marriott Resort and Spa (Pattaya) Amazing! I came without any expectations. My favorite dish  which everyone probably overlooks  is the "Chicken Stew with Young Tamarind Leaves." It was incredibly delicious! Goji Kitchen Grill & Bar is located in the Bangkok Marriott Marquis Queen's Park.
Given the similarity in name to the restaurant  I had high expectations for the size of the venue and the variety of food.

As you can see from the photo  it's spacious  has a wide selection of food  and is of a very satisfying quality.

They also serve some Japanese dishes  such as miso soup  and there were a few Japanese customers  which gave me an idea of ​​how popular it is.

There was so much food that I couldn't finish it all  so if I had the opportunity to stay here again  I would like to try a variety of dishes. Goji Kitchen Grill & Bar at Pattaya Marriott Resort and Spa (Pattaya)  Goji Kitchen Grill & Bar at Pattaya Marriott Resort and Spa (Pattaya) I ate there on December 26th and only just had time to review it.
It was a Friday evening meal  booked through Hungry Hub for 1488 THB per person. There was a "buy 4  pay for 3" promotion  which was great value for a rare celebration.

The atmosphere was good  well-decorated  and clean.
Not too crowded  so no need to compete for tables.
The staff were friendly  though there was a slight surprise. I asked for directions to the restroom to wash my hands after just cracking open crab  but they told me to wash them in the hot kitchen  which made me feel a little awkward.

The food selection was extensive  with many dishes I liked.
The dessert selection was also excellent – ​​a dessert lover's paradise.
More details in the photo captions.

Things I'd like to see added/improved:
- Blue crab can be ordered grilled or steamed.
- More roasted rice and lime are available to make your own dipping sauce.
- Beef noodle soup with spicy broth.
- The curry should be made with peeled crab meat.
- The grilled chicken should be kept warm.

Overall  it was a great meal. I'll definitely come back if I have the chance.

text:

Golden Peony At Conrad Singapore Marina Bay Ordering five dishes  getting three wrong—the Sheraton should probably consider terminating its contract with this restaurant. Golden Peony At Conrad Singapore Marina Bay Thanks Christopher for introducing golden peony to us me and my family were very happy with the amazing food  especially the fish and beef were amazing. Golden Peony At Conrad Singapore Marina Bay We chose Golden Peony for our post-ROM lunch with our immediate family  so we came in with high expectations  especially given the price point. Unfortunately  the experience was rather disappointing.

The restaurant was almost empty when we arrived around 1:40pm  yet the service felt oddly rushed the moment we all arrived  as though the staff were eager for us to finish so they could go on their break. It didn’t feel like a relaxed  celebratory meal  which was what we had hoped for on such a special occasion.

Service was also not proactive even though we were the only remaining table at that time. Tea was not refilled unless we repeatedly reminded the staff  and overall attentiveness was lacking. Even during the yusheng preparation  usually a festive and engaging moment  the staff member handling it appeared unenthusiastic  which made the experience feel transactional rather than celebratory.

For a restaurant of this reputation and cost  we expected a much higher level of hospitality and guest awareness. It’s a pity  because we specifically selected this venue to mark an important milestone with family. Sadly  the service experience did not match the occasion or expectations. Golden Peony At Conrad Singapore Marina Bay A pleasant dinner out with the ladies. Food was good and Vanessa was v attentive. There’s a 20% off with Takashimaya card. Great deal overall!! Grok 1970  Grok 1970  Grok 1970 Thai food here is delicious and flavorful; I loved every dish! (Although several menu items have been discontinued.) Grok 1970 A truly memorable dining experience  every dish is beautifully balanced  deeply flavorful  and thoughtfully presented. Gui Ratchayo Fantastic experience 🙏🏼🍀 Gui Ratchayo

The meat was tender throughout  the beef tongue was excellent  the seafood was fresh (shrimp  scallops)  and the lamb was superb.

You can order for 1 hour and 30 minutes and eat for 2 hours.
Love it! Gyu-Ya Yakiniku Rama 3 The staff at the Rama III branch are lovely and provide great service. I've eaten here many times. You can make reservations  especially for larger groups  as you can book a large room. The quality is good  the food arrives quickly  and they are responsive. It's great value for money. Gyu-Ya Yakiniku Rama 3 The food is good  and the 399 baht promotion is considered good value. Gyu-Ya Yakiniku Rama 3 I went on Sunday  November 23  2015. I usually eat at King Kong  but now many branches have closed  leaving only a few far away. So I switched to this restaurant. Personally  I think it's a good substitute. This restaurant has sushi and sashimi for 899+ (962 net). You can use the "Half-Half" discount  which is 200 baht per person per privilege. You have to pay with the "Half-Half" discount based on the total amount per person. The actual amount is 762+ the 200 baht "Half-Half" discount. Anyone who doesn't have the privilege will miss out.
- The food is good. The beef is good. The pork and bacon are good. Most of the food is good.
