restaurant name: Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side)
text:
Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side) I don’t know why I didn’t finish the meal; the sauce was taken away before I could finish eating it. The quality of the meat was truly excellent  so I’ll definitely go back there again. However  the service was a bit lacking. Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side) The food was delicious  and the staff member  P'Note  was very nice and provided excellent service. He spoke politely. 😊 Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side) 1. The female staff member (Note) provided excellent service. She was very helpful  served politely  and the food was delicious. Meeting such a lovely staff member made the experience even better! 100/100 Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side)  Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side) Gyu-Ya Yakiniku at True Digital Park delivers solid yakiniku vibes right from the start 🔥🥩😎 The place is clean  modern  and feels comfortable whether you’re here for a casual meal or a proper meat feast. The food quality is genuinely good — fresh cuts  nicely marinated meats  and premium items that look great the moment they hit the grill. Service is also on point  staff are attentive  polite  and quick to help when you need anything 👍✨

Grilling the meat is satisfying  and most items cook well and taste great 😋🔥 The beef comes out juicy  flavors are well-balanced  and overall it feels worth the price. But here’s the big issue — the grill heat 😅🥲 The stove wasn’t hot enough  which became very obvious when grilling river prawns. They took way too long to cook  and that really killed the momentum of the meal 🦐⏳ Nothing ruins yakiniku joy faster than staring at seafood that refuses to cook.

Because of that  I’m docking one star ⭐️ The food and service deserve praise  but the grill system seriously needs improvement. A yakiniku place lives and dies by its heat 🔥👀 Fix the stove temperature and this place could easily be a 5-star favorite. For now  it’s a strong 4 stars — good food  good service  but please turn up the heat 🔧🔥⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side) restaurant food point_of_interest establishment General Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side) restaurant food point_of_interest establishment General Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side) restaurant food point_of_interest establishment General Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side) restaurant food point_of_interest establishment General Gyu-Ya Yakiniku True Digital Park (West Side) restaurant food point_of_interest establishment General

GyuBei Yakiniku There were no waitress or waiter serving the customers  seems like everyone is busy in the kitchen  even when someone served us and bring us to the table  the service was not great and the staff didn’t even stand near our table when we try to take our order.

The meat touches the kitchen curtain when it was carried out from the kitchen.

Overall disappointing even before we enjoyed our food. GyuBei Yakiniku The chateaubriand cut of meat was brown on the edges like the meat had been serving time in the fridge  the meat platter meats were unevenly sliced but good for the price. However we made the mistake of ordering their seafood platter (the calamari looked and tasted like plastic  the prawn was passable). If you avoid the seafood platter  the meal would be okay. GyuBei Yakiniku They regularly have promotions which makes it super value for money! GyuBei Yakiniku Brought my family here tonight to celebrate wifey’s birthday. Ordered 2 sets of the $78 1-1 special set. All of us enjoyed it so much. GyuBei Yakiniku food was decent  servers were great. but the payment experience can be much better - I was handled by a very indifferent cashier with almost zero manners.

Gyunobi Yakiniku  Gyunobi Yakiniku คุ้มค่าคุ้มราคา Gyunobi Yakiniku เคยกินสาขาทาวอินทาว พอยกเลิกไป เลยต้องตามมากินที่เมืองทอง ราคา800+ ของยังคุณภาพดี บริการดีเหมือนเดิม มีที่จอดหน้าร้าน Gyunobi Yakiniku  Gyunobi Yakiniku From someone who's been eating at this restaurant since it first opened  prices have started at just 300-400 baht  but now the highest price has skyrocketed to 900+ baht (including VAT). I can't bring myself to eat there anymore. As I've gotten older  I can't eat as much at buffets as I did when I was a kid. It's such a shame that I can't go there as often as I used to. 🥲❤️

HAJI land Saw some tiktok post raving about the food here. Firstly  reservations for a weekend are highly recommended  very limited seats for both floors. QR code scan to order and pay in website.
We ordered Pork Lard Fried Rice  Singapore Hokkien Mee  Roxy laksa  Singapore Fried KwayTeow  Rojak and Iberico Pork Satay.
Foodwise  I honestly only found the Pork Lard Fried Rice worth mentioning to try. Pork lard was crispy and fresh  fried rice had good wok hei  and the runny egg on top when mixed in elevated the taste.
The Singapore Hokkien Mee was good  but no wow. Prawns was fresh and big  Pork belly had a frozen porky taste  the gravy was umami but had a sweet after taste (sugar?)  the pork lard on top was sadly cold and not crunchy.
The Roxy Laksa tasted watered down  little lemak flavor  and the chilli spiciness was the only flavor that was present. Prawns  fish cake and Taupok was fresh and tasty  so it redeemed the dish a little.
The Rojak... i understand they are serving modern SG food  but it was missing the trademark prawn paste  the sauce was sweet  sour and spicy  nice but not representative of SG Rojak. YuTiao and Taupok was fresh and good though.
The SG Fried KwayTeow was okayish  portion was big  liver sausage quality was good  but there was very little WokHei to the dish  only sweetness prevailed.
Lastly  5 Iberico Pork Satay cost S$30  for $6 per satay  they better taste like the best ever.... Satay had a good char  meat didnt taste like Iberico IMHO  it was a little tough. Sauce was drizzled on top of Satay  it was spicy and tangy from the small cubes of pineapple.
Staff service was prompt and atmosphere was noisy and lively.
Overall  I would only come here again if I am hosting friends overseas  as they have a good variety of SG food which are above average but are really pricey  locals definitely knows where to get better versions of these food. HAJI land The egg fried rice and Mala Knife Cut Noodles were delicious! Would definitely come back when we’re back in Singapore! HAJI land Food and vibe at Fatt Choy is always good! Back this time as my extended family wanted to try. For a table of 10  we ate so much till we were rolling out of the store. Love their lo hei as it's truly unique  and must-orders include for Iberico Satay (which they currently have a bak kwa version of it)  Hokkien Mee & Roxy Laksa! HAJI land  HAJI land OX carrot cake was ok  chicken karaage was really tasty  but $6 for "homemade iced tea" which is clearly just Nestea in a glass  is silly. Best to get a reservation.

HAN ROLL BKK  HAN ROLL BKK The food was delicious. I liked the atmosphere and the decor of the restaurant.
However  the service from the staff was terrible; it was like they were forced to work there.
Even though there were no customers. HAN ROLL BKK This is a very unique sushi hand roll restaurant blending Star Wars and high end cuisine. The hand rolls are delicious. They are served one at a time and the seaweed is very crunchy. This is my fifth time here  and it is one of my go to spots in BKK. Good staff  good music  and good atmosphere. The chefs special was delicious. They had  new options this time on a new menu (I posted photos)! Can’t wait to come back! HAN ROLL BKK Everything was delicious. The food quality was excellent  the ingredients were very fresh  just like eating in Japan. The restaurant atmosphere was warm and inviting  like a private room. HAN ROLL BKK There is actually a very nice dining area in the back that you wouldn't know is there. Staff were not as friendly as they could be  but food was good and fresh and a fun experience for my son especially with the light saber chopsticks!

restaurant name: HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Sala Daeng
text:
HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Sala Daeng Ordered takeout
The beef noodle soup was amazing HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Sala Daeng We had a lovely lunch here during our stay in Bangkok 🇹🇭. I came with my sister 👯‍♀️ and we were so happy to find this little gem right across from our hotel 🏨. The food was absolutely delicious 🍜✨ — fresh  flavorful  and beautifully presented 🍤🥢. But what truly made it special were the adorable cat-themed decorations 🐱🖼️ — so cute and cozy  it felt like a little sanctuary in the middle of the city 🧡. Perfect for a relaxing break after exploring Bangkok’s vibrant streets 🛵🌆. Highly recommended if you love good food and charming vibes! 😻🍽️🌸 HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Sala Daeng The food was delicious  fresh  and very clean. The taste was exactly what I wanted. HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Sala Daeng HANJI-A Taiwanese Hot Pot Bar
A Taiwanese hot pot restaurant 🍲🇹🇼
They use a stone hot pot where they brush the meat before cooking it 🔥🥩
The meat quality is pretty good 👍
I ordered the fried food platter 🍤🍟
It wasn't as crispy as in Taiwan  but it was pretty good 😋
They served a green papaya appetizer and finished it off with a golden sauce. The atmosphere is comfortable 🛋️
It feels upscale ✨
But the prices are above average 💸
The waiter didn't explain what the dish was before serving 🤔
I had to ask again 🙋‍♂️
This could be improved 🛠️
Those who miss Taiwanese hot pot might consider this place ❤️🍲 HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Sala Daeng I often go to this place as the first customer on Saturday because the soup is mom's favorite. The service is very good and we like the most is papaya as appetizer.

HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Thonglor The signature soup is a must try  ingredients are super fresh. HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Thonglor It's Taiwanese food  yes  but it's terrible Taiwanese food. I'm speechless! 👎👎👎👎👎 HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Thonglor  HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Thonglor The Taiwanese-style hot pot is clean and hygienic. The Mongolian soup base has a rich herbal aroma  and the stir-frying before cooking is a big plus. The beef slices are large and fresh  the vegetable platter is plentiful  the braised pork rice is slightly broken  and the self-service dipping sauce is excellent. The ambiance is comfortable with its modern Chinese style and floor-to-ceiling windows. Parking is a bit difficult. At 800-1000 baht per person  the price is reasonable and worth a return visit. HANJI Taiwanese Hot Pot Thonglor Today I ordered the Shaoxing wine chicken set meal  which cost approximately 520 baht including tax and service charge.

The hot pot was cooked using the stone hot pot method  where the base ingredients are stir-fried first before being poured into the broth  similar to Taiwanese stone hot pot  which adds a touch of sentimentality.

The overall dining environment was clean and comfortable  with a good variety of sauces and two side dishes. Some 80s Taiwanese classics were playing  which was quite pleasant.

My personal drawbacks were: the Shaoxing wine chicken used frozen chicken legs  which were dry and not tender.

The Shaoxing wine chicken lacked the necessary wine flavor  making it taste bland. I asked the staff if I could add more rice wine  and the first answer was no. I clearly told them I ordered Shaoxing wine chicken  but it didn't have any wine flavor…it wasn't good. The staff then asked another colleague  and the answer was still no. I'm willing to pay for more wine  but it's not acceptable to simply say no.

A set meal cost around 500 baht  but the frozen chicken thighs were really unappetizing. The broth for the rice wine chicken was bland and tasteless  and they couldn't add any alcohol  which was a real shame.

Whether it's expensive or not is something you can only judge after experiencing it yourself. In short  tonight was definitely an expensive dinner.

HEY Mookrata - Charoen Nakhon Grilled beef shoulder with fermented fish sauce dip is delicious.
The crispy pork belly is also tasty.
They have smooth draft beer.
Plenty of parking.
Let's gooooo! HEY Mookrata - Charoen Nakhon The food was excellent  the service was great  the food was delicious  fresh  high-quality  and the dipping sauce was amazing. HEY Mookrata - Charoen Nakhon  HEY Mookrata - Charoen Nakhon Food
The service was quite slow  and the portions weren't worth the price; it was more of a casual meal.
The taste was average  but the dipping sauce was delicious.

Service
Not very impressive. I booked via LINE for their signature tiered cake (which is supposed to be on sale)  but when my friend arrived  she asked about it. The staff said they didn't have it and hadn't ordered it. We were confused.
They kept passing the buck  asking who to contact  and after a while  we came back for the cake she ordered.
They still said they didn't have it and hadn't ordered it. Then they went silent. We waited at the counter  but they didn't say they were checking. We had to ask if they were checking  and finally got the answer that they were. They found that the cake had been ordered via LINE many days earlier.

Note: The restaurant had already called to confirm before the reservation  and the customer had already been told to have the tiered cake ready.

The staff's tone and facial expressions showed their displeasure. If they can't hide it  I recommend they wear masks.

Atmosphere
Good  I liked it.

*For this price  the service was unacceptable. I don't even want to pay for it. 😑 HEY Mookrata - Charoen Nakhon The crispy beef salad tasted good  but the beef was tough.
The pork barbecue was just average.

HOUSE of TANGO Every time I eat here  I'm impressed. This time I came to celebrate Valentine's Day  and the new menu items were delicious. The atmosphere is beautiful and unlike anywhere else. 👍 HOUSE of TANGO - The restaurant is beautifully decorated and the overall atmosphere is good.
- Parking is limited but valet service is available.
- The food tastes rather average compared to the price. The menu isn't very diverse and the portions aren't large  yet the prices are quite high.
- The service could be improved. The staff seemed confused by general questions and served food to the wrong table  despite the 10% service charge. HOUSE of TANGO The restaurant is beautiful and well-decorated  but the food is just average. It's not bad  but it's not delicious enough to feel worth the price. The food is quite expensive  but if you consider the atmosphere  it's okay because the restaurant is truly beautiful.

The service was good  but the staff seemed confused throughout the restaurant. There were a couple of instances where staff seemed to bring food to other tables  and the staff taking orders seemed bewildered  having to run into the kitchen to find the answer to simple questions like "What is this dish?" when asked.

I don't think I'll return to this restaurant  but I believe it's a must-visit at least once because there aren't many restaurants in Thailand that create such a great atmosphere. Recommended!!! Hainanese Chicken Rice C.P Tower Silom Date of service: Thursday  October 30  2025  3:50 PM

This was my first time eating at "Hainan Chicken Rice." Initially  I planned to go to another restaurant.

But I saw a sign promoting a 10.10 Double Date promotion: a 99 baht chicken rice set  including a small plate of chicken  a bowl of chicken rice  and Chinese tea. I decided to give it a try. It wouldn't hurt  as I've loved chicken rice since I was a child.

So I went into the restaurant and ordered the 99 baht chicken rice set. I couldn't choose the part of the chicken  so I only had the chicken leg. (Which was fine.)

The restaurant's atmosphere is airy  spacious  comfortable  and clean  just like any restaurant in a shopping mall or office building.

I'd rate it using the following rating scale:
5 - Very delicious / Very good
4 - Delicious / Good
3 - Moderately delicious / Average
2 - Fairly delicious / Fair
1 - Delicious  needs improvement / needs improvement
0 - Not delicious at all / Not good at all

Chicken thigh: 2 - The thigh meat was hard  but not tender. Not impressive.
Pickled radish/carrot: 3 - The pickled flavor was faint and not distinct.
Garlic rice: 4 - The temperature was a little hot. The taste was quite good. I gave it the highest rating.
Sweet soy sauce: 3 - It's sweet soy sauce straight from the bottle  without any seasoning.
Soybean sauce: 2 - The ready-made sauce  everything is finely blended during the manufacturing process  and the flavor is very sour.
Soup: 3 - The flavor was faint and not as hot as it should be.

I actually expected the taste to be delicious. The first bite would be a surprise  a wow factor... but it turned out to be... It wasn't at all what I expected...

The staff provided excellent  if not excellent  service. Hainanese Chicken Rice C.P Tower Silom Good value for the quality of the chicken rice.
Clean  firm  tender chicken that's not tough.
The sauce is standard. Hainanese Chicken Rice C.P Tower Silom The taste is good  and the price is great with the Hungry Hub promotion. The boiled chicken is tender  the fried chicken takes a little while to prepare but comes hot and fresh. The rice is fragrant and delicious.

Hannari Cafe de Kyoto Thonglor Tucked away in a small lane beside Donki Mall Thonglor  Hannari Café de Kyoto feels like a little piece of Kyoto hidden in Bangkok 🇯🇵✨. It is said that in Kyoto dialect  “Hannari” means graceful and elegant  a word that perfectly matches the café’s atmosphere. 🌸

Partnering with Kyoto Prefecture  the café beautifully combines Japanese elegance with a warm  welcoming vibe. They serve comforting light meals  delicate Japanese traditional sweets  and soothing drinks. It seems they’ve also rolled out new kimono rental deals 👘.

The 2nd and 3rd floors feature charming tatami rooms that capture the essence of Kyoto: definitely worth going upstairs (many guests might not realize there’s more beyond the first floor). The staff are very friendly and attentive; a young lady with a ponytail patiently explained the promotions and menu to me 💁‍♀️

The ground floor displays many Kyoto specialties and unique gifts 🎎. Prices are slightly higher  but there are tiered discounts if you buy more (you can always ask the staff for details).

During this visit  the air-conditioning felt a bit cool for me 🌬  and the mochi was yum but slightly bigger than I expected. The plating and presentation were elegant and impressive  though the dark pink sauce holder wasn’t very photogenic — just my personal preference 🤭 The hot Hōjicha latte ☕ was absolutely delicious.

Overall  a peaceful and lovely spot that captures the gentle spirit of Kyoto right in Bangkok 🌸. おおきに 😋

Heights At BKK good view   nice buffet breakfast. service is great. especially staff called beer. come and visit here！ Heights At BKK  Heights At BKK beautiful view  good food and great service by Mind. 👏🏻👏🏻 👏🏻 👍🏻 👍🏻 👍🏻 Heights At BKK Dining on the 39th floor at Bangkok Heightz feels less like a rooftop stop and more like a carefully staged culinary experience above the city. The value here is not only the view: the real strength lies in the quality of the ingredients  precise preparation  and thoughtful plating. The kitchen delivers a flavor of Siam with western notes  balancing fragrant herbs and subtle spice with clean  structured techniques. Textures are handled with care  from crisp  lightly charred edges to tender  juicy interiors  and the sweet  spicy  and acidic elements are calibrated so each bite feels composed rather than loud. Aromas of lemongrass and citrus rise gently from the plate  and the presentation shows attention to detail without overcomplicating the dish. Service is attentive and well paced  with staff explaining components clearly and maintaining smooth timing between courses. The atmosphere is refined yet relaxed  allowing the skyline to frame the evening without overshadowing the food. Book a table around sunset for the best light and reserve in advance  especially on weekends  to secure a spot near the edge. Heights At BKK Beer welcomed us warmly

restaurant name: IGNIV Bangkok at The St.Regis Bangkok (One Michelin Star)
text:
IGNIV Bangkok at The St.Regis Bangkok (One Michelin Star) Dining at IGNIV was an interesting experience  but ultimately not one that justified its price point.

I visited for the Valentine’s special lunch  and while the menu was certainly creative  many of the combinations felt more experimental than enjoyable. The dishes were technically sound and thoughtfully presented  yet the flavors didn’t quite deliver the depth and cohesion I would expect from a one-Michelin-star restaurant. It was good  but not truly memorable.

What stood out the most was the service  friendly  attentive  and genuinely refined  which elevated the overall experience. The elegant setting inside The St. Regis adds to the occasion  though the arrival experience was surprisingly inconvenient. Parking was difficult  there was no valet service  and being charged a parking fee after spending 10K baht on lunch felt out of place.

While IGNIV shows clear ambition and craftsmanship  the overall experience didn’t fully align with the expectations set by its reputation and pricing. IGNIV Bangkok at The St.Regis Bangkok (One Michelin Star) Disappointing experience. Food is  average at best. A lot of the dishes did taste similar with an overtone of acidity for most appetisers and main dishes (except the vison). Bread was not fresh and portions are ridiculously small (even for a gastronomic restaurant). The sharing plates concept does not work - each plate is barely sufficient for 1 person and the sharing makes its overly complicated and not practical. In short  everything is looking good but the taste is not there. We were really looking forward to eat there but it was definitely not worth it. There are much better places in town. IGNIV Bangkok at The St.Regis Bangkok (One Michelin Star) Food was average  with many items being deep-fried. The place was empty  yet I was asked by the managing director not to take a call in the restaurant  which felt a bit odd — especially since there were no other guests and a staff member was scrolling through social media with the sound on.

The environment itself is nice  but overall I definitely wouldn’t return with a computer. IGNIV Bangkok at The St.Regis Bangkok (One Michelin Star) Delicious food.

restaurant name: JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok
text:
JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok I had lunch at the JW Buffet today. I've eaten there many times before  and what I remember most vividly is how delicious the English steak was. But today's Australian steak was awful. Previous steaks would have been a perfect 100  but today's was a complete 0. It was impossible to cut into or chew. However  the mango sticky rice was very delicious. JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok The service staff were excellent. They gave great recommendations and constantly checked on us.
The taste was fantastic. The sashimi was fresh  tender  and flavorful. The crispy pork was amazing – I loved it! The shrimp was fresh  firm  and sweet. The baked mussels with cheese were also good  and the lamb was superb. In short  everything was delicious.
We celebrated our anniversary and they even gave us dessert! So sweet and delicious. I highly recommend it  10/10. A truly satisfying and delicious meal. JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok  JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok Very good JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok Located on the lobby level of the JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok  the JW Café is widely regarded as one of the top buffet destinations in the city. It underwent a stunning renovation that replaced its older  darker tones with a bright  modern  and open-plan design that feels both upscale and welcoming.
​The buffet is organized into distinct "open kitchen" stations  allowing you to see the chefs at work.
​The Seafood Station: This is often the main draw. Depending on the day (dinner and Sunday brunch are particularly lavish)  you’ll find king crab legs  giant river prawns  fresh oysters  and rock lobster.
​The Carving Station: Their Beef Wellington is a signature item—perfectly pink in the center with a crisp  buttery pastry. The roasted wagyu and rack of lamb are also consistent crowd-pleasers.
​Japanese Selection: The sashimi is cut to order and remarkably fresh  featuring thick slices of salmon  hamachi  and tuna.
​Thai Favorites: For a local touch  don't miss the Boat Noodles or the authentic Thai curries  which maintain the true spice profiles of the region.
​Dessert Room: A dedicated climate-controlled room featuring everything from handcrafted pralines and French pastries to a live crêpe station and traditional Mango Sticky Rice.
​Ambiance: The space is massive but clever seating layouts prevent it from feeling like a "cafeteria." Large floor-to-ceiling windows let in plenty of natural light during the day  while the evenings feel sophisticated and warm.
​Service: In true JW Marriott fashion  the service is proactive. Empty plates are cleared quickly  and water glasses are rarely left empty.
While it is one of the pricier buffets in Bangkok  the quality of the ingredients—particularly the premium seafood and meats—justifies the cost. It is a reliable choice for a high-end dining experience where quantity actually meets quality #Marriott #Bangkok #JW #Cafe JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok buffet_restaurant restaurant point_of_interest food establishment General JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok buffet_restaurant restaurant point_of_interest food establishment General JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok buffet_restaurant restaurant point_of_interest food establishment General JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok buffet_restaurant restaurant point_of_interest food establishment General JW Café at JW Marriott Hotel Bangkok buffet_restaurant restaurant point_of_interest food establishment General

The duck was tough and difficult to chew and the pasta tasted like something you could easily make at home. Desserts were also underwhelming with a low quality  catered meal feel rather than something fresh or indulgent. On top of that  plates were slow to be cleared  which didn’t help the experience.

Overall  it felt overpriced for what was offered and there are much better buffet options available elsewhere at the same price.

The service quality was like a roadside stall  but they were charging us restaurant prices. The lobster sashimi arrived  but the sauce was only slowly brought over after we finished eating—just like how they only brought the wine after we finished eating. KO-YA Yakiniku The omakase experience was truly blissful! Every course  every dish  and every presentation was beautifully executed and clearly explained. The environment was very comfortable  and Chef Ivan Ting was extremely knowledgeable when explaining the dishes. He also answered all our questions very well.

The chutoro was the best I’ve ever tasted! It was so similar to otoro that I was shocked when the chef said it was chutoro. He explained that this texture can only be achieved with a high-quality tuna weighing at least 80kg. I really learned a lot from this dining experience.

Even the uni and monkfish liver were excellent. I’m usually not a uni person  but this was incredibly fresh and delicious. All the other sashimi and even the grilled Gindara was yummy delicious.

The dessert was also amazing — it was my first time tasting warabi mochi served warm  and it was absolutely delicious.

Instead of the usual green tea  mugicha (roasted barley tea) was served. Its fragrant aroma and smooth taste made it incredibly enjoyable  and I found myself refilling my cup repeatedly. Being caffeine-free was an added bonus.

My sister-in-law ordered the saba fish lunch set  which was boneless and beautifully cooked  remaining juicy and flavourful.

Considering the quality of ingredients  craftsmanship  and overall experience  the pricing was very reasonable. If I return to Singapore in the future  I will certainly visit again. Highly recommended. KO-YA Yakiniku The food was unexpectedly delicious! Every dish was made with fresh ingredients  and you could really taste the passion and creativity of chefs Ivan and Wilson in each bite. The place is cozy and comfortable which made the experience even better. It was a wonderful evening  and definitely recommend it.

The shrimp was firm and had a great texture  and the fried dishes  both the dried fish and crispy pork  were not soggy at all. 👍🏻 KORATA Siam Square  KORATA Siam Square

restaurant name: KROK-A-CAFÉ ESPRESSO BAR ครก อะ คาเฟ เอสเปรสโซ บาร์ @ MASON
text:
KROK-A-CAFÉ ESPRESSO BAR ครก อะ คาเฟ เอสเปรสโซ บาร์ @ MASON Mr. Apichart suggested this place for dinner and it was wonderful! The food was amazing and the view was picturesque with the beautiful sunset. The pizza and German pork knuckle  the famous dish here  are well cooked with high quality ingredients. The toast is SUPER YUMMY with lots of fresh fruits. LOVE IT! I recommend visiting this place for dinner. Perfect place for romantic dates. The staff were really friendly and welcoming as well:) KROK-A-CAFÉ ESPRESSO BAR ครก อะ คาเฟ เอสเปรสโซ บาร์ @ MASON The vibe and view is always so pleasant and welcoming here ☺️ partner and I had the Spaghetti Aglio e Olio  Ragu Beef Pasta  and Mac n Cheese. Every dish was on point 🤌🏼
Our servers Bank and Mimi were super kind and really made our night 😊 they also checked up on our allergies and were helpful - thank you! KROK-A-CAFÉ ESPRESSO BAR ครก อะ คาเฟ เอสเปรสโซ บาร์ @ MASON The food was average  but the view  atmosphere  and customer service were great.
There seems to be a hotel  so I thought I'd like to stay there next time. KROK-A-CAFÉ ESPRESSO BAR ครก อะ คาเฟ เอสเปรสโซ บาร์ @ MASON Great bar  even though it's costy. I recommend going in for a happy hour from 4 to 6 pm. The cocktails are great and the bartenders are awfully nice KROK-A-CAFÉ ESPRESSO BAR ครก อะ คาเฟ เอสเปรสโซ บาร์ @ MASON A tiny eatery within the MASON hotel dotted with few tables and 2 round lounge sofas lookout to amazing panoramic view of ocean and pool. Menu is edited with nice refreshing drinks  light bites and pizza.

Kaarla Wood-Fire Grill & Bar Awesome service despite the manpower crunch. The team is trying their best and nice to see them moving fast to assist with request. Glad to have the space for my big group to feel comfortable and yet being attended to Kaarla Wood-Fire Grill & Bar  Kaarla Wood-Fire Grill & Bar  Kaarla Wood-Fire Grill & Bar  Kaarla Wood-Fire Grill & Bar

Kaen  Kaen  Kaen Not sure what’s so premium about the buta don  I went to double check if I’d mistakenly ordered something else. Came for lunch and it was thin  dry  and with barely any fat on the meat. For $19++ on lunch! Staff and service were great though. Kaen  Kaen Love their sukiyaki!

Kaenkrung As expected from a Michelin star! 
Very delicious! 
High-end Isan cuisine! Kaenkrung •Today I dined at @kaenkrung  a Michelin Guide-level modern Thai-Isaan restaurant on Arun Amarin Road.
•It's part of the same chain as "Kaen" in Khon Kaen.
•The ingredients are carefully selected and locally sourced from Isaan.
•Fermented fish sauce from Kaeng Lawa  salt from Bo Krathin  Black Angus beef from Maha Sarakham  and free-range pork from Non Sila.
•Every dish was truly delicious!! Great flavor  no MSG added. I loved the rich taste  but not too spicy.
•The restaurant marinates its own ingredients  from side dishes to seasonings.
•Even their kombucha is brewed in-house and it's delicious! They also make their own beer!!
•The Bak Ngaew Kombucha (120 THB) is homemade  an Isaan dish with a slightly sweet and sour taste  perfect for digestion.
•The restaurant is decorated in a contemporary Thai style  with two floors. The service is excellent. The owner pays attention to every detail; even the plates and bowls are carefully selected.
•You can feel the dedication to providing delicious food made from high-quality  clean ingredients.
•A must-try dish is the Ha Dong (spicy minced meat salad). • Forgotten Menu Items: • Garlic and coriander root marinated pork neck  grilled to perfection  incredibly tender meat  coated in cumin sauce.
• My favorite dish today was the Jicama Salad with Grilled Shrimp (180 THB)! The jicama was crispy  absorbing the dressing created a delicious and well-balanced flavor. I really liked the fermented fish sauce here; it was savory  mildly salty  and not too strong in smell.
• Mixed Seafood Salad with Pickled Nuts (550 THB): Crab meat  squid  shrimp  and pineapple marinated in fish sauce  with a perfectly balanced and tangy dressing.
• Charcoal-grilled Sirloin Steak marinated in rice wine with Pickled Egg Yolk (190 THB): Unbelievably tender meat  well-marinated. When eating  dip the meat in the pickled egg yolk; slightly salty and delicious.
• Isaan Chagartari (450 THB): A platter of four types of homemade sausages: Italian tiger ear sausage  Isaan sausage  fermented pork  and Chinese sausage.
• Chicken Curry with Mixed Vegetables (320 THB): A refreshing soup with over 20 kinds of vegetables.
• I have to give a thumbs up to the sticky rice at this shop. It's incredibly soft  unlike any I've ever had anywhere else. The "Khao Niao Hang Fai" (60 baht) is the best! It's a fusion of sticky rice and regular rice; the texture is soft but not mushy  chewy like mochi. It's really good  you have to try it!!
• The "Khao Thapthim Chum Phae" (60 baht) is brown rice.
• Caramel latte with burnt coconut ice cream (165 baht). A latte served with burnt coconut ice cream. Slightly salty  fragrant  and delicious. Kaenkrung A relaxed eatery with seasonal and regional Thai dishes. Super helpful staff explaining everything. Well worth the journey. Kaenkrung Found this gem on the Michelin guide  worth the trip. Quality  food  presentation was on point. The staff is very sweet and the owner came over to the table and helped us choosing our drinks by giving us samples (lavender kombucha was my pick). Make sure you leave room for dessert!
The portions are big  we ended up over ordering so price per person will be less if ordering reasonably
For 2 people  we got 4 appetizers and 2 mains. Kaenkrung

Kagonoya Central Rama 3 The staff were unfriendly  answered customers curtly  and had a stern expression. Kagonoya Central Rama 3 I found a cockroach leg in my ice cream  and saw two or three more running around on the table. Kagonoya Central Rama 3 Delicious according to restaurant standards  good service Kagonoya Central Rama 3 Found a cockroach crawling onto the table. The restaurant took responsibility by moving the table and giving a 10% discount on the next dining experience. Kagonoya Central Rama 3 The restaurant might be a bit small  but food portion aren’t. The price is reasonable and food quality is top notch

Kagonoya Central Rama 9 Services sucks Kagonoya Central Rama 9 This review reflects a rather negative experience 😓
I took my family of 5 to eat here. The price per person is quite high at 699++ (approximately 823 THB/person net) and there's a 90-minute time limit. However  the overall experience wasn't worth the price paid.
The wait for food  especially the fried dishes and sushi  was very long  about 20 minutes per order. There were also ordering restrictions:
- For fried dishes and sushi  you can only order 1 piece per item  and 1 piece is one bite.
- You can order a maximum of 3 orders at a time.
This meant we could only eat 3 pieces in one sitting. Combined with the slow service  the 90-minute time limit was insufficient and felt like an indirect way of limiting how much customers could eat.

Overall  I felt it wasn't worth the price paid and I expected better management from the restaurant. Given the quality of service and the lack of value for money  I definitely won't be going back again. Kagonoya Central Rama 9 The buffet is supposed to be available until 9:15 PM  but at 8:38 PM they said the kitchen was closing and we had to order more. It was outrageous. The staff were also completely unhelpful; we had to call for everything. Kagonoya Central Rama 9  Kagonoya Central Rama 9 Every time I go  some items on the menu are always out of stock. This time I went (30 Oct) there were no tempura shrimp or kushiage shrimp. The salmon menu also had a fishy smell. I felt that the quality of the food was better than last year  but this year  every time I come to eat here  it's always bad. I'll have to find another restaurant instead.

Kagonoya Central World Not saying anything about the direct cause  but interestingly  all five of us who went there for lunch had to use the restroom right after leaving the place.

(This isn't to conclude it was the direct cause  but what's interesting is that all five of us who had lunch there had to use the restroom immediately afterward.) Kagonoya Central World Today I went to the CTW branch. The staff made me sign up for a queue number. There seemed to be empty tables in the restaurant  but there was no queue number on the list. I signed up at 2 PM  and it's almost 7 PM now  and no one has called me. Lol. It's like they don't want to sell anything. Okay  I won't eat there then. Bye. Kagonoya Central World If you enjoy being ignored and chasing staff just to get basic service  this is the place for you. Otherwise  save your time and go literally anywhere else.

•

Absolutely unacceptable experience. We were seated and waited 5 minutes just to receive the WRONG drinks and then never got the correct ones. That alone set the tone. After that? Total abandonment. No server  no explanation  no follow-up. Just silence  like we didn’t exist.

We sat there for another 10 minutes with ZERO interaction. No one took our order  no one checked on us  nothing. We eventually had to ask THEM whether we were supposed to order with a server or through a QR code because apparently basic communication is too much to expect here. Only then did they finally bring over a QR code  as if they were doing us a favor.

So we ordered. And once again  nothing happened. Another 10 minutes wasted. Not even the basic shabu pots  which should be on the table the moment you’re seated. Any place that knows what they’re doing would have that ready immediately. That’s Restaurant 101. This place clearly does not know how to operate.

In total  we wasted almost 30 minutes sitting there with no food  no drinks  no pots  no service  and no clue what was going on. At that point  staying would’ve been a bigger mistake so we walked out.

This wasn’t just slow service. It was complete operational failure. Avoid this place unless you enjoy wasting your time and patience. Kagonoya Central World Last Saturday  I went to eat because I saw that there was a promotion where KTC points could be redeemed. This was clearly the main reason I decided to go there. Before ordering  I asked the staff if I could use my KTC card. The staff simply replied 
"Yes  you can use your KTC points."

But when it came time to pay  I was told 
"Your KTC card isn't participating."

Honestly  I was shocked. If the staff had made it clear from the beginning that certain card types weren't participating  I definitely wouldn't have gone there. No one wants to go there and then find out later that they had to pay the full 1 6xx baht when they intended to use the promotion!

The problem is 
the staff didn't explain important details from the beginning  even though there were only a few types of cards that weren't participating.

The terms and conditions weren't posted at the front of the restaurant  but at the checkout  which was too late.

There's no responsibility in informing customers before ordering.

Honestly 
if I had to pay 1 6xx baht like this  I definitely wouldn't have gone there in the first place.

I chose this restaurant purely because of the promotion  not because I wanted to eat and then face this unclear charge.

I want the restaurant to urgently improve their communication. The notification was incomplete. It makes customers feel disappointed and like they were tricked into ordering food without providing the necessary information from the beginning. เนื้อ outside อร่อย Kagonoya Mega Bangna This shabu-shabu and Japanese buffet restaurant boasts high-quality meat  with a wide variety of cuts to choose from. You can select two soup bases; if you love meat  the sukiyaki soup is a must-try. Simply cook the meat until done  then dip it in the egg – it's delicious! They also offer a wide selection of Japanese dishes  including sushi  sashimi  and other cooked items. Personally  I enjoy the steamed egg custard and the clam soup. Kagonoya Mega Bangna I'm never setting foot in the Mega Bangna branch again. I'm going to generalize because I'm extremely angry. If there's a manpower shortage  I suggest hiring more staff  or the reason is that the staff are unwilling to provide good service. First  the staff member asked which buffet price we wanted and what drink we preferred. We ordered green tea. Ten minutes passed  and by the time we finished eating  our throats were dry  so we informed the staff member for the first time that we hadn't received our water. It still hadn't arrived. We ordered using the QR code provided  thinking they might have forgotten. The table behind us asked for a refill  and they immediately served it. Wow  20 minutes had passed  and we still hadn't received our water. Secondly  the sashimi was served without dipping sauce or wasabi. Okay  we asked again. Finally  I completely lost my patience. A staff member served water to the table next to us  so we asked for a refill. The staff member turned around as if unwilling to work  but the table behind us also asked for a refill  and another staff member served them very quickly and willingly. I was furious and completely lost my patience. Everything was so slow. The service was terrible; time was running out  and we still hadn't received our food. This is insane! They should just close the restaurant and hire more staff. Kagonoya Mega Bangna Shabu-shabu is amazing! But if you want to eat sashimi or sushi in a buffet  I wouldn't really recommend it. It's okay  but go for the shabu-shabu  it's truly excellent. Kagonoya Mega Bangna This is a delicious restaurant. The meat is good  the dipping sauces are great. It just lacks some fresh chilies  otherwise it would have been perfect. The fried chicken is delicious  the sushi is decent  but the tamago (sweet egg omelet) isn't sweet enough. The ice cream is absolutely amazing.

Kagonoya Mercury Ville Before entering the restaurant  the staff did not inform us about any items that were out of stock. They didn't ask which course we wanted  but simply led us to a table and gave us a QR code to order food. Overall  the staff seemed unwilling to provide good service. They even served the wrong dishes. When we called for a staff member  they ignored us. We were very disappointed. Kagonoya Mercury Ville  Kagonoya Mercury Ville I usually order the 699++ set. I just found out they have a promotion right now: 20% off for your first Grab Dine Out payment. That's awesome!

I've eaten at all the branches  and I don't know why  but I feel like the beef and salmon sashimi at this branch are the best.

However  I have to deduct a star because they were out of so many items  even though it was a Saturday. There was no shrimp  no sesame dipping sauce  and no gyoza.

This branch has the best view  but the other branches are delicious too. I love the soup; the dark broth is simmered until it's not too salty. It's better than many famous restaurants. I keep coming back to this one even though I've tried many others.

The fried items are also delicious. The sushi rolls and steamed egg custard are good too.

Another plus is that all branches are less crowded  so there's no need to queue. Kagonoya Mercury Ville This Japanese buffet restaurant offers shabu-shabu  sukiyaki  and sashimi. The food is delicious  clean  and not crowded  making for a comfortable dining experience. It's easily accessible from Central Chidlom. Kagonoya Mercury Ville As good as their standard. The wague beef is quality. The thickness of the slices is quite right. They serve a very good quality of eggs. It makes the beef taste even better.

Kagonoya Siam Discovery Compared to other branches I've eaten at  the food at this branch is worse. The steamed egg custard wasn't hot at all  and the tempura was disappointing with only one piece that was soggy. The presentation of everything was a complete disappointment for the price and didn't match the pictures from other branches. The taste was terrible. Kagonoya Siam Discovery The food was delicious  but there were few staff members. There was a promotion with the app  making it good value at 600 baht. The food was fresh and good. Kagonoya Siam Discovery The food was delicious and met expectations  but the service was a little slow. Kagonoya Siam Discovery Good but a few items are out of order. Kagonoya Siam Discovery I want to say the service at this place was terrible. The staff were clueless  the food came out very slowly  and the food quality was awful. The sushi was street-level quality but expensive. I wasn't impressed at all. The food came out slowly  and they charged me and closed the shop extremely quickly. 😒🤢

Kai Maeng-da Ta-lay Phao The place is absolutely a must-go place in Phuket for the food and the comfort from the place. Food is flawless and the staffs are professional and friendly.
I will come again if I have another chance for Phuket. Kai Maeng-da Ta-lay Phao We visited Ta Khai for an anniversary dinner after seeing the restaurant recommended by the Michelin Guide  and we are very glad we chose to visit.

Milk and her team delivered brilliant service  and we thoroughly enjoyed dining here and experiencing the extended tasting menu. It consisted of around ten courses  all of which were delicious.

The décor and layout are beautiful. We were greeted as soon as we entered and guided down to the restaurant  with a server explaining the backstory of the restaurant and its mission as we walked past the lily pond to the comfortably spaced tables.

One small note is that many of the main courses were served together. While we were informed this is more aligned with traditional Thai dining  we felt we would have preferred the dishes to be delivered separately or in smaller groups  as receiving three or four protein mains at once was a little overwhelming.

Overall  this is a brilliant restaurant for those who enjoy high-end dining. A very strong recommendation from us. Kai Maeng-da Ta-lay Phao We visited Pa Tong in February’25 for 3 weeks and ate in many restaurants - This was the best! The location is amazing and dining area is very different . They have their own herb garden and you can see straight into the food preparation areas. The food itself was amazing. Can strongly recommend the  tastings menu It’s not cheap but  well worth it! Kai Maeng-da Ta-lay Phao not worth Kai Maeng-da Ta-lay Phao

Kai Yang Samanong Phattanakan 53  Kai Yang Samanong Phattanakan 53  Kai Yang Samanong Phattanakan 53 Their ingenious seasonings set them apart from other Isan restaurants.
Their gai yang (grilled chicken) is especially delicious  with a rich flavor that reaches all the way to the inside of the meat!
They also serve Australian beef laab (spicy minced meat) and nam tok (spicy grilled meat).
Their black rice khao niao (sticky rice) is also delicious.

They moved from Pattanakarn Soi 53 a year ago. Kai Yang Samanong Phattanakan 53 The taste of the food is different from the Isaan restaurants in Bangkok. It's very delicious. The steamed bamboo shoots are very delicious. The minced pork salad is very good. It's an underrated restaurant. Kai Yang Samanong Phattanakan 53

Kaikatok Wutthakat  Kaikatok Wutthakat Update: it reduce price 299 but not variety of side dish. You have to order a la cate (price a little too much like one side dish (199). I would prefer 499 and same quality and side dish. It a little disappointment as a neighbor and regular customer.

Update Wagyu Beef is tender and like melting in the mouth.

Side dish got authentic taste. I would like to recommend octopus kimchi. Kaikatok Wutthakat  Kaikatok Wutthakat The taste is good but there is not much variety. You have to order a separate menu. Kaikatok Wutthakat Great Korean Food  similar taste to when I was travel to South Korean. Fish soup is da best !!!

Kaiwa Steak overdone. Plus I saw guy prepping with no gloves. He picked up personal  drink cup several lines and proceeded to cut scallions with no gloves.

Had the rock-roll sushi role. It was ok. Atleast the sushi chef had gloves on. Kaiwa Holy cow. We stumbled into this restaurant and left blown away. This was seriously great. Started with salmon carpaccio  cheese katsu  and crab rangoons. Crab rangoons were unique in a good way!!! They had a mix of imitation crab and real crab and were fried perfectly. Our server Summer gave us a great recs on the bento box  shrimp fried rice  and the volcano roll. She was extremely attentive to our table and also super kind. Highly recommend Kaiwa We were the only people in the entire restaurant on a Saturday evening  which should have been my first red flag.

After we sat down and ordered the supper special (served in a bento box) and a boba tea  I was told after ordering that our food had to be taken to go and that we couldn’t eat in the restaurant. My daughter was well behaved  and there was no rush or crowd at the time.

When I asked why  I was told they don’t want people ordering the supper special to dine in because the bento boxes take longer to clean. That explanation didn’t make sense given the empty restaurant. Even the waitress noted that as well.

Uncomfortable and upsetting experience. I won’t be returning  and I’m sharing this experience with others in the local military community. Kaiwa Food was amazing  service was top notch! Best sushi so far! Kaiwa When I heard there was a place in tampa that did Japanese AND Korean bbq ..... I was super intrigued as these are 2 of my fav cuisines. Kiwa totally exceeded my expectations. First off  the menu was even bigger than it let off online. They had so many specials and innovative dishes we jumped on immediately. I asked them for recommendations and our waitress and the hostess were beyond helpful and patient with us. We started with some Aperol spritz (huge!) and yellowtail carpaccio which literally melted in your mouth and had the perfect kick from the sriracha & jalapeño on top. The sauce it was in was absolutely lovely as well - a vinaigrette vibe. Then we had the cheese katsu which also had chicken. Aside from being served with rice and Korean slaw (YUMM) this really gave me chicken Parmesan vibes. It was so so delicious and definitely blew me away. We also had the crispy rice with tuna  avocado  and some veggies/salad which was the refreshers we needed. I loved their take on the crispy rice - the additives of the toppings did add a lot for me. We ordered diet cokes - they were super crispy & hit the spot as we went into the next 2 Korean dishes we ordered (bulgogi and spicy pork). Both came with rice  Korean mashed potatoes  & an orange garnish and all sides totally elevated the main dishes. They also came with miso soup which hit hard for me and I'm not usually the biggest miso soup fan. The flavors of the beef and pork oh my gawddd. The spices  the sauce  everything. I couldn't get enough. We were pretty full at this point but powered through the rolls we ordered - we went with Kiwa & Crunchy Krab specialty rolls. The Kiwa was the perfect balance to the crunchy Krab which was a deep fried roll our server recommended. Both were so delicious and as an avid spicy mayo lover  didn't need anymore toppings since they already had and were amazing as is. It was time to order some of their well known bobas and after careful consideration and recs from our server  we went with the Thai milk tea & Oreo milk tea both with tapioca boba - YUMMMMM. Refreshing and the perfect sweet treat..... then they surprised us with fried Oreos & vanilla ice cream and ugh it was the perfect meal cap. Can't wait to come back and try literally everything on the menu. The vibes were also great and the staff was perfectly friendly. No notes tbh.

Kalanan Riverside Resort Staycation I had planned to come during COVID 
but finally arrived on Valentine's Day.

I had booked both accommodation and restaurants.
But what I received was indifference and a lack of follow-up from the staff.

The booking was made through Agoda  which went smoothly.

The restaurant booking was made directly on the hotel's website.
On the day of arrival  although there were reservations  it was incredibly frustrating. The hotel ignored customers who had booked for a special day  resulting in a terrible Valentine's Day experience.
After inquiring with the hotel  frankly  the manager's handling of the situation was indecisive  failed to address the problem effectively  and was unprofessional.
There was a surprise attempt after a complaint was made  with a bouquet of flowers placed on the bed  but there was no arrangement whatsoever; they just placed it there because of the customer's complaint.
Furthermore  they entered the room after check-in  without informing the customer about their personal belongings and possessions  which is a severe invasion of privacy.
This is something the hotel should absolutely not do.

Regarding room amenities  cotton swabs and various bathroom items were only in boxes  without any contents inside.

Food items and price tags were hidden away.
I paid almost 4000 baht for the accommodation but only received two bottles of water.
Let me tell you  the price is expensive  but the attention to detail is lacking  especially considering the hotel's attempts at appearing luxurious.

Communication and feedback from staff were extremely slow. I booked about two weeks in advance 
but received no feedback  only automated email replies.
When I spoke to the manager  they said  "Oh  there's no one overseeing that."

And everyone tried to respond with "Please come again next time."

If this experience was so difficult  who would want to come back next time?
Handling immediate problems was terrible. Remember  if you can't handle problems effectively on the spot  it's the last time for your customer.

You had the opportunity to offer packages or promotions to customers for special occasions two weeks in advance  but no staff member took the time to do so  letting those important days go to waste.

Thank you for the Valentine's Day experience  unlike anything I've ever had before.

It was a Valentine's Day I'll remember forever  unlike any other  but in a negative way. Kalanan Riverside Resort Staycation An ideal place for a relaxing vacation.
The whole resort is so aesthetically pleasing. Kalanan Riverside Resort Staycation Kalanan resort; it's really awesome & have great staff and quality of services... Kalanan Riverside Resort Staycation  Kalanan Riverside Resort Staycation

Kam's Roast Central World Best quality every time I come and dine here Kam's Roast Central World I had a very enjoyable dining experience at this restaurant. The atmosphere felt lively yet comfortable  making it a great place to relax and enjoy a meal with friends or family. The signature roast meats were the highlight — perfectly cooked with crispy skin and tender  juicy meat inside. The flavor was rich but not overpowering  and the sauce complemented the dish very well. Portion sizes were reasonable for the price  and presentation looked appetizing.
Service was efficient and polite even though the restaurant was quite busy. Staff were attentive and helped explain the menu clearly. Food arrived quickly and everything was served hot. I also appreciated the complimentary grass jelly with longan juice after leaving a review — refreshing and not overly sweet.
Overall  this place offers quality food  good service  and a pleasant environment. I would definitely return again and recommend it to anyone looking for authentic roast meat in Bangkok. Kam's Roast Central World Excellent quality and taste for this very traditional Chinese barbecue and roast restaurant.
Friendly and efficient staff with quick service between ordering and food delivery.
Several branches  in Bangkok and often busy. Kam's Roast Central World Kam’s Roast at CentralWorld is a meat lover’s paradise. The Poro Char Siu is legendary—fatty  honey-glazed  and meltingly tender—while the Crispy Pork delivers that authentic  crispy Hong Kong skin. It’s easily some of the best Cantonese barbecue in Bangkok. Kam's Roast Central World Nice atmosphere 👍

Kam's Roast ICONSIAM I visited this restaurant while shopping at ICONSIAM and it turned out to be one of my favorite meals during the trip. I ordered the signature roast duck with rice and also tried the BBQ pork. The roast duck skin was beautifully crispy while the meat inside stayed tender and juicy. The sauce was aromatic and slightly sweet  pairing perfectly with the rice. The BBQ pork had a nice caramelized edge and a smoky flavor that made it very addictive.
What I liked most was the consistency — every bite tasted carefully prepared  not rushed despite the crowd. The portions were satisfying and felt worth the price for a restaurant located inside a popular mall. Food arrived surprisingly fast  hot  and nicely presented.
Service was professional and efficient. Staff were polite  helped us choose the menu  and checked on us regularly without being intrusive. The dining area was clean and comfortable even during peak time.
After the meal  I received the complimentary grass jelly with longan juice  which was refreshing and not overly sweet — a perfect finish after a rich roast meat dish.
Overall  excellent roast meats  reliable service  and a pleasant dining atmosphere. I would definitely recommend this place to anyone visiting ICONSIAM and I will come back again on my next trip to Bangkok. Kam's Roast ICONSIAM This was one of the most satisfying meals I had during my visit to Bangkok. The restaurant has a busy atmosphere but still feels organized and welcoming. The roast meats are clearly the specialty here — the skin was crispy while the meat remained incredibly tender and juicy. The seasoning was well balanced  flavorful without being greasy  and every bite tasted premium quality.
I especially enjoyed how consistent everything was  from presentation to taste. The rice and side dishes complemented the roast perfectly  and the portion size was fair for the price considering the location inside ICONSIAM. Food was served quite fast even during peak hours  which was impressive.
Service staff were friendly and attentive. They helped guide us through the menu and made sure our table was comfortable. As a nice bonus  the complimentary grass jelly with longan juice was refreshing and a perfect way to finish the meal.
Overall  great food  efficient service  and a reliable dining experience. Definitely worth visiting and I would happily come back again. Kam's Roast ICONSIAM I recently had an incredible dining experience at Kam's Roast ICONSIAM  and it certainly lived up to its Michelin-starred reputation. From the moment we walked in  the staff greeted us with warm hospitality and the service remained attentive throughout our meal.

We ordered their signature roast duck and crispy pork belly  and both were outstanding. The duck was succulent with perfectly rendered skin  while the roast pork had that satisfying crunch and melt-in-your-mouth fat that is hard to find elsewhere. Every bite was packed with authentic flavors.

The restaurant’s location inside ICONSIAM is also a huge plus  offering a comfortable and elegant atmosphere for a family lunch or a nice dinner. The portion sizes were generous and the pricing is very reasonable for the quality provided. If you are a fan of authentic Cantonese roast meats  this place is a must-visit in Bangkok. I will definitely be coming back for more!" Kam's Roast ICONSIAM The food tastes delicious  authentic style with juicy roast pork  roast duck  and soft noodles.

restaurant name: Keyaki at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore
text:
Keyaki at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore Celebrated 2 birthdays excellent teppanyaki cooking by Chef Alvin Ho who accommodated all our troublesome food allergies obligingly and big thank u to service team who despite being understaffed were prompt in clearing dishes topping up tea n sake - not cheap but enjoyable good place for special occasions ! Keep up the great food n service 😊 Keyaki at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore We went for teppanyaki set and it was quite fulfilling. The location is really cute with the Japanese garden . The chef with the moustache is a good entertainer and amazing chef . The food was delicious and for the service I didn’t expect less Keyaki at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore Japanese restaurant with Kyoto inspired beautiful garden & koi pond at Level 4 Pan Pacific Hotel serving sushi  sashimi  Teppanyaki  Tempura Japanese cuisine with good ambience and excellent
service at value for money for weekend brunch set where you get to sample courses from sashimi  a choice of yakimono of either prawns or cod fish  yakimono or nimono  teppanyaki choice of beef  chicken or scallops  mains of fried rice  chirashi don  udon or nigiri and choice of dessert. Highly recommended for food quality and good service from pleasant staff. Keyaki at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore — Keyaki Japanese restaurant at the Pan Pacific Hotel offers a tranquil Japanese garden setting for intimate wedding ceremonies  it was the perfect backdrop to D & J's wedding celebration  where D and J read their vows to one another surrounded by their guests.

(Photos by Ann Seojin @by__annopano) Keyaki at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore We celebrated my wife’s birthday here and were served by Joseph  who was professional  polite  and attentive to our dietary needs!

Khao Ekkamai Very delicious food  great service and a very nice atmosphere all for a decent prices. Khao Ekkamai The best thai restaurant for me in bangkok.  I know very well the thai food and the restaurant in Bangkok.  Ambiance food and service are premium.  Thank you very much to all the staff for your welcome Khao Ekkamai One Star House offers a modern take on traditional Thai cuisine. Khao Ekkamai This restaurant offers a wide variety of traditional Thai dishes  and the food is quite delicious  with each dish being particularly impressive.

However  the service was completely disproportionate to the quality of the food. Upon being seated  an empty water glass was placed on the table  but no one offered to refill it  which confused me. They should have charged for the water upfront and simply asked what I wanted to drink upon seating; otherwise  they shouldn't have provided an empty glass.

When the dishes were served  there was no distinction between appetizers and main courses; everything was brought out at once  leaving me bewildered. Since I was dining alone  I wanted to try a little of everything  but the portions were so large that I couldn't finish it all. At this point  the staff seemed to be chuckling  as if implying that I couldn't possibly finish such a large meal.

If that's the case  they should have explained this to customers when taking orders  or offered half-size dishes. There are many ways to address this  but this restaurant chose a very unfriendly approach for solo diners.

In conclusion  this restaurant is highly recommended if you're dining in a group; the food is truly exceptional and delicious. However  solo diners should think twice. Furthermore  the quality of the service is disproportionate to the Michelin sign on the wall. Khao Ekkamai The location is a bit off but the environment is very nice. The service crew are very polite and helpful. The food is great. I would suggest the restaurant to study the sequence of dishes being served. It is true that only one of us ordered a cocktail but if all the food came out nearly all at the same time  the customers would have the difficulty of what to eat first. If there are some deep fried dish  the taste of the food would be affected. So the kitchen should look at the items ordered and decide the sequence. Or the service staff should ask the customers if there is any preference. As a one star Michelin restaurant  the restaurant should improve on this.
Two problems on the dishes we ordered : 1) the sticky rice serving together with the barbecued pork was cold and too hard. I can see that the small bowl of sticky rice was prepared in advance and the kitchen just served it when the barbecued pork was ready - without really checking on it.
2) the sour soup with Thai vege: we chose beef but the sourness of the soup somehow alleviates the beef smell so it is better not to offer the choice of beef for this soup.
The captain was very kind to offer us free mango sticky rice and the sticky rice was moist and fragrant. We also ordered two more Thai dessert and they are very nice. We like this restaurant and yes  we will visit again. Thanks for giving us an enjoyable experience.

Khao Jaan-Prod CentralWorld The food wasn't very good  and the ambiance was so-so. The waiters were especially indifferent and dismissive; there was no smiling service  no greetings  and no polite language whatsoever. Adding a 7% VAT and a 10% service charge was unacceptable 😅. I asked if they could provide water  but it cost 30 baht. I regretted not reading the reviews before entering the restaurant. Khao Jaan-Prod CentralWorld Good good but small portion Khao Jaan-Prod CentralWorld It is located on 7th floor. Overall a good dining experience. The portions are a bit small but prices are expensive. Staff seem to be quite busy and not as helpful. It is stated as Michelin starred but the attentiveness of the wait staff is lacking  perhaps it is the language barrier.

Our favorite dishes are Tom Yum fish soup and grilled pork jows. The pork jows are tender and flavors are delicious  love the sauce that complements with it. We ate them too fast before I was able to take a photo.

I am trying to get a free Bingsu as a result of this review. Khao Jaan-Prod CentralWorld We dined at Khao Jaan-Prod (CentralWorld) and had a lovely experience. My husband ordered the Vogue beef and I had the tofu and potato green curry with jasmine rice  everything was absolutely delicious and tasted very authentic.

The only disappointment was that mango was not available so they couldn’t not serve mango sticky rice  which I was really looking forward to. However  the service was good and the food arrived very quickly. Overall  a great place for authentic Thai food  and we’d definitely come back. Seating is comfortable though. So if you come here for location convenience  you do get the KHAO food quality  but for a warmer atmosphere and better service head to Ekkamai. Khun Toom Kitchen Saimai Avenue It's very delicious. Khun Toom Kitchen Saimai Avenue I checked my bill around 1:10 PM on December 8  2015. I had just been discharged from the hospital due to diarrhea  and I wanted something easy and light.

I sat down at the beef noodle section  and the cheap dishes for 25/45/60 looked interesting. So I ordered stir-fried chicken with ginger  stir-fried minced pork with basil and bamboo shoots (I emphasized when ordering that it wasn't spicy at all)  one fried egg (the menu says 2 eggs in parentheses)  and one small bottle of cold water.

First  I want to compliment the waitress who wore glasses. She was a fair-skinned  slightly older woman. She was polite and well-behaved  and she took my order well.
1. 30 seconds after taking my order  she came back to ask me what I ordered. It was probably because she couldn't read her own handwriting (I tried  but I couldn't read hers).

2. She served me a normal-spicy stir-fried basil dish  so I asked her to take it back and fix it. After about five minutes  another dish arrived.

3. I called for the bill. She charged me 85 baht (3 dishes for 60 baht  one plate of rice for 10 baht  and one small bottle of water for 10 baht). There was a 7% VAT of 5.60 baht. I immediately objected to her and informed her that I wasn't served water and would no longer be taking it. She immediately replied  "I'll get you some water." She then returned with a bag of water for me to take home.

In short  I'm not bothered by her illegible handwriting  by her forgetting to order non-spicy  by the original price of a fried egg  or by the absence of water. However  I am slightly bothered by her own mistake and simply replacing the receipt. Before leaving  she tried to say something to me  but she spoke so softly that I couldn't hear her. If the owner sees this or has the opportunity to read it  please consider this lesson. 😊 Happy working! Khun Toom Kitchen Saimai Avenue The food is delicious and the staff provides excellent service. จอดรถได้ที่ park x ekkamai Kizuna Shabu & Hand Roll Kizuna Handroll & Shabu earns a solid 4-star rating for its impressive ability to balance two distinct Japanese culinary styles under one roof without sacrificing quality. The star of the show is undoubtedly the beef; the marbling on the premium cuts is exceptional  offering a melt-in-your-mouth experience whether you’re opting for the sukiyaki or the signature Tonyu (soy milk) broth. The handrolls are equally refined  featuring crisp nori and high-quality seafood like their creamy uni  which provides a fresh contrast to the rich  savory hot pot. While the table can get a bit crowded once the full spread of veggie platters and sets arrives  the excellent value of their lunch sets and the consistently high ingredient quality make it a standout destination in Bangkok for anyone who refuses to choose between sushi and shabu-shabu. Sad. Bring back the Korat that we knew! Kozen Sushi Bar & Restaurant Was a horror show of a meal. Came for the $188 1-for-1 Omakase meal for Valentine’s and for what little we paid  we still left horrified at the experience.

I’ll start with what we did like. Food started out strong with amazing appetisers and some dishes had flavours which pleasantly surprised us. The green tea had a nice roasted sesame note which was almost savoury and appetising. On to our qualms…

The issue that stood out most was the timing between dishes. Between the sashimi and lobster course  there was a 45-minute dry time that nothing was served  and with no explanation except that the sushi takes awhile. On another occasion between the beef and miso soup  the wait was about ~30mins. I’ve appended herein photos with timestamps.

Because the sushi took so long  the waiter helped push our lobster course ahead of the scheduled sushi course. This also got me to believe that their service plan was ill-planned.

It was also likely due to the wait that cooked food came out cold  or lukewarm at best. Taste was still acceptable though.

I also indicated that my partner couldn’t take beef before the service. Beef was still served and when rejected  his meal was delayed a full course for them to prepare a replacement. He ended up having that replacement with the miso soup together.

The beef dish also did come out with a dirty plate (see photo). Dish was very well seasoned but was sinewy.

Even the environment. The music cut in and out  and volume was constantly adjusted to be either too loud or soft. There was dead air for 20 minute instances as well.

My partner and I did notice some discrepancies between  our dishes in terms of volume. On a dish where he had 1 nugget of crab  I had ~3 slightly larger pieces. He also had a fish scale on one of his sushi.

Overall  even with the promo $188 price tag for 2 pax  I still struggle recommending this place. What would have been a perfectly executed 1.5-2 hour dinner service was stretched to well over 3 hours. It was to the extent that I felt bad for diners who went with the $288 menu. Kozen Sushi Bar & Restaurant Food

restaurant name: Kyo Roll En - Cafe & Meal Central Eastville
text:
Kyo Roll En - Cafe & Meal Central Eastville Good person Kyo Roll En - Cafe & Meal Central Eastville If you're craving Japanese-inspired sweets  this place is a must-visit. The desserts are beautifully presented and taste just as good as they look. I loved the matcha soft serve – rich  creamy  and perfectly balanced without being too sweet. The black sesame ice cream and mochi toppings were delicious and provided a very authentic Japanese dessert experience.

I also tried the strawberry dessert set  which was super cute and festive. The strawberry cake was light and fluffy  and the sauce added a nice touch. The overall presentation makes it a perfect cafe for photos and a fun treat with friends.

The cafe atmosphere is calm  clean  and cozy  great for relaxing in the afternoon. The service was nice  and the menu offers a lot of variety  especially if you love matcha  sesame  and Japanese flavors.
Definitely recommended for matcha lovers and anyone who enjoys elegant desserts.

If you like Japanese-style desserts  this is a place you should definitely try. All the desserts are beautifully decorated and perfect for photos  and the taste is just as good as they look. The matcha soft serve is especially rich  fragrant with green tea  and not overly sweet. The black sesame and mochi served with it are also delicious  offering an authentic Japanese experience.

The strawberry dessert is also very cute. The cake is fluffy and soft  and the sweet and sour sauce is just right. It's both delicious and incredibly photogenic.

The atmosphere is comfortable  clean  and cozy  perfect for relaxing or a casual date with friends or your partner. The service is good  and there's a wide variety of menu options. Matcha lovers shouldn't miss it! restaurant name: L'Entrecôte The Steak and Fries Bistro (Customs House)
text:
L'Entrecôte The Steak and Fries Bistro (Customs House) Food is delicious and the service is amazing L'Entrecôte The Steak and Fries Bistro (Customs House) I booked a Valentine’s Day dinner one day in advance via the platform. I emailed the restaurant to ask if I could arrive 30 minutes later  but received no reply. The reservation system also did not allow any timing adjustments  and there was no contact number provided in the confirmation email. With no way to clarify  I arrived at the original booked time.

Upon arrival  I was told that because my booking was “late ” I could only be seated outdoors  despite it having rained the entire day. The outdoor table and chairs were wet. I had clearly stated in my booking remarks that this dinner was for my wife and my anniversary  hoping for some consideration. At the same time  there were empty indoor tables available for my slot  but the staff insisted we could not be seated inside.

What was most disappointing was that it was another customer  not the staff  who noticed my wife’s disappointment and kindly offered us their indoor seat. It was quite telling that a fellow diner showed more empathy than the restaurant staff.

During the meal  we were told that water was “free flow”  yet we were later charged $3 per bottle. That is not free flow  and this should have been communicated clearly.

Towards the end of the meal  while we were already having dessert  a staff member abruptly placed a sauce dish from a previous main course on our table. It was clearly meant for another table. He had to reach across our spoons and dessert  so he was fully aware we were already at the last course — yet he still placed it there and walked away. The lack of awareness and care was surprising.

The food itself was acceptable  but nothing memorable. Unfortunately  the overall experience was overshadowed by:
•	A rigid and unresponsive booking system
•	Lack of empathy despite special occasion remarks
•	Misleading information about charges
•	Poor table awareness and service etiquette

For a restaurant that positions itself at a higher standard  these are basic service expectations. This experience left us feeling that our occasion was not valued  and it has made us unlikely to return. L'Entrecôte The Steak and Fries Bistro (Customs House) Their signature steak was mediocre  it tasted very bland without their signature sauce and it definitely does not have the beef taste. Would not go back for their steak again. L'Entrecôte The Steak and Fries Bistro (Customs House) Paying 6$ For mineral water?

We ordered two starters: salmon  avocado  and snails  but they arrived quite late... the starters were very good.
Then came the duck confit with potatoes  which was excellent  and the cordon bleu  which was far  far  far  far too coarse—inedible  even half would have been too much.
The chocolate mousse was way too rich in chocolate and  above all  too hard  but we finished it anyway.
A pleasant meal that deserves a greeting in French for a French restaurant of this style.
Lots of Bordeaux wines to choose from  but almost no Loire or Burgundy  and they didn't even have the bottle we ordered first!

Good Thai-style service: as soon as you finish a dish  they take it away 😄 even too much!
Perhaps the menu is too large; between the one under the plate  the one on the chalkboard  the menu itself  and another chalkboard on the wall  it's a bit confusing! We had two glasses of white wine as an aperitif and a bottle of red wine for €250  a bit pricey. L'Escargot Bistro Great cozy style place to hang out with friends and family. restaurant name: La Bisque at Le Bali Resort & Spa (Pattaya)
text:
La Bisque at Le Bali Resort & Spa (Pattaya) I recently stayed at La Bali Resort in Pattaya and unfortunately  my experience was not satisfactory.

First  there was no safety locker in the room  which made it inconvenient to store valuables securely. There were also no basic amenities provided such as a toothbrush or other essential toiletries.

Another issue was housekeeping. After returning from outside  I placed the sign requesting room service  but the room was not made up even after waiting four to five hours.

Regarding breakfast  there were very limited options for vegetarians  and the overall quality and taste of the food were not very good.

Additionally  the TV in the room was not a smart TV. There was no option to connect a phone  and there was no HDMI port available to connect a laptop either. So  the TV experience in the room was also disappointing.

Overall  I found the service to be poor  and both the room facilities and overall experience did not meet expectations. La Bisque at Le Bali Resort & Spa (Pattaya) I recently stayed at La Bali Resort in Pattaya and unfortunately  my experience was not satisfactory.
