La Tavola Renaissance Bangkok I am extremely disappointed and upset. Today  February 14th  I had pre-booked a Sharing Set course through Hungry Hub.
About 40 minutes before my reservation time  a staff member called to say that it was Valentine's Day and the chef would only be serving buffet.
I was shocked and asked  "What about my reservation?"
If I didn't want the buffet  she simply said that it was Valentine's Day and the chef could only serve that way  without any apology whatsoever. It was as if the fault was on the customer who had made the reservation…
She said she would consult with the manager and call back. I  who was planning to dine on Valentine's Day  didn't know what to do. I waited for a very long time  but no one called back. I had to find a new restaurant… There was no attempt to take care of the customer at all. As a Bonvoy member and loyalty member of Renaissance  I am very disappointed with the service here.

I contacted them again after having to find another restaurant. The manager said that the staff member had miscommunicated  and that I could actually still come to dine… but unfortunately  there was no communication with me until after my reservation time had passed. text:

1. The staff in the level lounge are well-trained.

2. I really liked the room's furnishings.

3. Family-friendly  lots of kids (?)

Cons:

1. The ceiling height is low; it might feel cramped if you're over 180cm tall.

2. The building's plumbing seems a bit old; people with a sensitive sense of smell might notice an odor in the restrooms and pool.

3. The towels in the pool and spa had a noticeable smell.

4. I love using the pool  but I really disliked this one.

5. Even lounge guests might be assigned to rooms on the 3rd floor overlooking residential buildings across the street.

6. The elevators are very small; the main building has 21 floors but only two 15-person elevators.

text:
Lacol Khaoyai - A Chatrium Collection (Khao Yai)  Lacol Khaoyai - A Chatrium Collection (Khao Yai) Fresh and frozen seafood  chicken parts.
Original shop in Warin Chamrap district  open since 1976  certified formaldehyde-free. Lacol Khaoyai - A Chatrium Collection (Khao Yai) Lacol dome is perfect for its visual appeal  but the food quality and service lag behind.

We reserved the dome seating and it was absolutely stunning  with a beautifully curated setup that looks amazing in photos. There was a minimum spend of 3000 baht for the dome  but totally worth it. The dinner tea tree dessert alone was around 1200baht  so it was not that difficult to reach the minimum spend. The dinner tea tree dessert presentation was a real highlight  especially perfect when paired with the dome. We had seen similar concepts elsewhere  but this is the biggest and most visually impressive one  making it perfect for photos.

Unfortunately  the actual desserts did not matches the presentation and were rather ordinary in taste. The main courses were also quite mediocre  which was disappointing given its higher prices. The restaurant was very quiet during our visit  with only a few tables occupied and spaced far apart. It was difficult to get the attention of the staff. We ordered sparkling water and also requested for warm water  but despite asking for the warm water three separate times  it never arrived.

This place is great for photos and some fun  but not somewhere for a quality dining experience. I loved me time here and the waitress (Pai) She is just so pleasant and has a smile that simply makes my day brighter! The food came fast and music was great! Definitely recommend. Best food I’ve had in khao yai! Lamaya (Khao Yai)  Lamaya (Khao Yai)  Lamaya (Khao Yai) I saw this venue on Instagram over a year ago and always wanted to go. When they opened in Bangkok I did visit and it was ok but this place is a thousand times better. My fiancé and I came all the way from Bangkok to celebrate Valentine’s Day and it was absolutely worth it.

Everything from the hospitality  super friendly staff to the food to the music  vibes and entertainment performances  Lamaya is doing it spectacularly.

We got the set menu that included appetizers  soup  two main dishes (beef tenderloin and a salmon Wellington)  a dessert  and two drinks. Each and every dish was mouth watering. I’m not a fan of pumpkin at all but the pumpkin soup was outstanding.

This is one of the signature dishes of this restaurant  and it wasn’t what I expected. It’s a light clear broth  no crab meat in it — mostly vegetables  and it has an alkaline veggie flavour of slimy greens and bitter gourd with only a delicate hint of crab sweetness. It lacks the salt or thickness to be a flavour bomb — more like a comforting broth to nourish someone who is recovering from illness.

restaurant name: Le' Bali Resort And Spa Staycation (Pattaya)
text:
Le' Bali Resort And Spa Staycation (Pattaya) I recently stayed at La Bali Resort in Pattaya and unfortunately  my experience was not satisfactory.

First  there was no safety locker in the room  which made it inconvenient to store valuables securely. There were also no basic amenities provided such as a toothbrush or other essential toiletries.

Another issue was housekeeping. After returning from outside  I placed the sign requesting room service  but the room was not made up even after waiting four to five hours.

Regarding breakfast  there were very limited options for vegetarians  and the overall quality and taste of the food were not very good.

Additionally  the TV in the room was not a smart TV. There was no option to connect a phone  and there was no HDMI port available to connect a laptop either. So  the TV experience in the room was also disappointing.

No beef tongue Long Jim Shabu Great! Long Jim Shabu Delicious food  calm atmosphere  and great service. It was my first time trying Jin Shabu  but the experience was wonderful and I can't wait to visit again.

They offer a sort of cook-it-yourself experience where you order up to two kinds of broths  and they bring them to you in a large pot that goes over your tabletop grill. You receive meats from your server  and have access to a self-serve bar of vegetables  noodles  seafood  and spices.

Because it is so customizable you can really experiment with flavors and come up with some delightful combinations. I was a big fan of the brisket and octopus in the spicy broth  and my fiancée enjoyed the pork belly in the sweet broth. Both pots also complemented each other quite well!

My favorite part of the ambiance was the privacy curtains. Because tables are all next to each other I imagine it would be easy to see what other diners are doing  but the curtains add a sense of privacy that made us feel like we were almost dining alone. Our service was also excellent. We had several people working in the restaurant come by during the meal to make sure we had all the broth  water  and meats that we wanted.

Definitely try Jin Shabu! It is delicious and well worth the money spent! Long Jim Shabu Always good food and a good price. We love that they offer options for vegans/vegetarians as well!

Long Lai New chicken rice find at Hougang! 🍗🍚

Was at Hougang Parklands Coffeeshop when we were drawn in by the amazing aroma of chicken rice wafting through the air. Had to check it out!

Discovered it's a newly opened stall - Lai Liang Pan Hainanese Chicken Rice 来两盘海南鸡饭.

What we ordered:
🔥 Roasted Meat 烧肉
🐔 Steamed Chicken End 白鸡尾

The chicken: Absolutely flavourful! You can see the chicken fats glistening on the meat - the kind you just cannot resist 🤤 The oiliness and fattiness were at such an acceptable  delicious level. Not too much  just right!

The sauces: Their ginger sauce and chilli sauce paired beautifully with the rice. These really made the dish sing! 🌶️

About the rice: We noticed the rice was a bit wet with some broken grains. When we asked the stallholder if he'd consider improving it  he explained it's intentionally made this way for the older folks living around the coffeeshop. That's so thoughtful - adapting to the community's needs! 👴👵

Bonus: Free flow chicken soup with vegetables and chicken pieces. Simple but yummy! 🍲

Overall  this is a good and honest stall run by someone who cares about his customers. Respect for catering to the elderly residents! Will definitely be back for more.

I visited Louk In Louk Jaan with high expectations  particularly given the visual presentation of the dishes  which appeared well-arranged and thoughtfully plated  as shown in the accompanying photo. Unfortunately  the overall experience did not meet acceptable standards.

The most concerning issue was the freshness of the food. The ingredients did not taste fresh  and the overall flavor profile fell below what would be considered satisfactory  especially for a restaurant positioned at this price point. The dish lacked depth  balance  and the quality one would expect for its cost.

In addition  the pricing felt significantly disproportionate to the quality delivered. For the level of freshness  taste  and execution  the menu prices are difficult to justify.

Most disappointing  however  was the service attitude from some of the staff members. While I understand that service roles can be demanding  I encountered behavior that felt aggressive  unfriendly  and lacking basic hospitality. This greatly impacted the dining atmosphere and overall comfort.

I believe this establishment has potential  especially in terms of presentation  but there is a clear need for improvement in ingredient quality  flavor execution  pricing fairness  and staff service mindset.

I hope this feedback is taken constructively and used to enhance the experience for future guests. Louk In Louk Jaan  Louk In Louk Jaan I took my foreign friends to this restaurant and they loved it. Macaria Bangkok Don't miss this Southeast Mexican gem for their incredible homemade dipping sauces—especially the Viuda Negra—the best spicy sauce  and my personal favorite: the amazing churros! Macaria Bangkok Best Mexican place in Bangkok. Authentic  great service  and amazing atmosphere. Macaria Bangkok I had a wonderful time on Valentine's Day. The food was delicious! Macaria Bangkok 📍 Macaria 🇲🇽 🎭🍋🥙 Bangna • I recently tried Mexican food that's more than just tacos or burritos. Macaria takes us beyond that 🌮🔥 with contemporary Mexican cuisine that aims to tell "stories  memories  and culture" through dishes from the Yucatan state in southern Mexico 🌵🇲🇽

I can tell you it's easier to eat than I expected and definitely suits Thai palates. The food from this state is distinctive and unlike other states in Mexico. Simply put  it's like our Northeastern Thai food – with a clear  local flavor. The recipes were developed in collaboration with a renowned Executive Chef from Mexico  who traveled to learn about the ingredients  food culture  and Thai tastes to create authentic Mexican flavors that Thais can appreciate. This makes every dish feel familiar  even for first-time diners.

The restaurant's interior beautifully conveys the charm of Mexico  including 🤍 rope chandeliers inspired by "heineken " the plant used to make rope  a significant export of Yucatan in the past; 💛 hand-painted walls that create a warm  homey atmosphere; and 🧡 artwork throughout the restaurant. Sourced from villages across Mexico  the heart of the restaurant ❤️ lies in the family portraits on the wall  featuring Ms. Macaria  the owner's mother figure  who embodies the value of care through heartfelt meals—the very beginning of this restaurant.

Recommended Dishes:

🐟 Pescado Zarandeado: Whole grilled sea bass  deboned  marinated in black Mexican curry  served with avocado salsa  zipecks  lime  and rice. A light and easy-to-share dish.

🍍 Pastor Negro Taco: Mexican-marinated pork taco served with grilled pineapple  jalapeño sauce  and homemade corn tortillas. A perfectly balanced flavor.

🥑 Chilaquiles: A popular Mexican breakfast dish  here made with the restaurant's secret salsa  tossed in fried tortillas. Deliciously crispy with generous toppings!

🥩 Steak and Eggs Rancheros: A juicy new take on a classic breakfast dish  featuring Australian Angus Picanha steak served with two fried eggs.

🧀 Marquesitas: Thin and crispy crepes  a street snack from Yucatan. These are incredibly addictive  filled with Dutch Edam cheese.

We want everyone to experience the charm of Mexican food  which is not just about taste  but also tells stories and showcases culture through the vibrant details of each dish.

Atmosphere: Good. We went on a Friday evening and there was a DJ playing music  but I personally found it a bit loud. I liked the decor  which had a tropical  Hawaiian feel.

Variety: The menu isn't very diverse  with just one page  but the drinks are plentiful. There's a variety of craft beers on tap. If you're a drinker  give it a try.

Food: It looks good and tastes good  and it's not boring. We ordered the:
Flank steak (440 baht) cooked medium-rare  so it was perfectly seasoned and didn't need any additional dipping sauce. The meat was thinly sliced.
Orange rocket (260 baht) - a very refreshing salad with balsamic reduction. Highly recommended.
Calamari (220 baht) - well-made  with firm  not-tough squid. Personally  I was surprised by the small portion size for 220 baht. It's quite expensive for a bowl.
Bacon Corn Rosi (380 baht) - I saw this in everyone's reviews  so I had to order it. It was truly delicious and flavorful. I especially liked the puffed  firm  and soft rice grains.

The food was very enjoyable to eat  and it wasn't boring. I think the price is a bit high  but considering the quality  it's a good value. But it might be a bit difficult to go back.

I ordered three glasses of umeshu for two people  which came to over a thousand baht per person.

I recommend calling to make a reservation in the evening  as the place is usually packed and the restaurant isn't very large  with about 20 seats.

Mae Oil Soijuu kimchi mac n cheese  kimcheese bacon pancake  kimchi stew  and honey butter chicken was scrumptious. truly a 10/10 meal. ambience was great  makgeolli was yummy and songs were amazing (they play old school kpop songs) Mae Oil Soijuu To brand itself as a makgeolli bar is a disappointment. We felt that in Singapore  to enjoy a good makgeolli means it has to be expensive. After coming here  we concluded that cheap and good makgeolli is something you can only find in South Korea itself.

Firstly  matcha makgeolli here is a huge let down. It tastes like cheap makgeolli mixed with a lot of matcha powder. It wasnt creamy and tasted bitter.

Next  the grape makgeolli. Making it a slushie version is so wrong. When the ice melts  you see a layer of water. It dilutes the taste so much that you cant even call it makgeolli.

Lack of coordination. The staff didnt even know that upstairs was already full. Climbed up the steps only to be asked to climb down again. Small issue.

Parts of the grilled corn ribs were burnt. If you have attention to details  you should cut the burnt parts off.

Lastly  the atmosphere was not too pleasant. A group near us was just shouting at each other even when the ambient music was not that loud that they can actually just speak with their normal volume. Nothing the bar can control.

2 makgeolli  one stew  one pasta  and one corn rib. Even with a 10% discount  it was about sgd 120. I guess we have no choice but to satisfy our cravings when we return to Seoul  where a whole bottle of good makgeolli is at most 15 dollars.

The rice was cold and we waited for an hour. When we asked the staff  they just said  "Did you already order?" and then disappeared. The staff didn't inform us or apologize at all. Mahasawat Cafe & Restaurant On Valentine's Day  we were a group of five  including a two-year-old child. We spent 3000 baht. It was an hour's drive  and the prices were high-end  but the service was low-end.
A middle-aged female employee in a dress  working in the outdoor area on the left  approached our table and irritably said  "Do you need anything? I've seen you press the button several times." She then snatched the call button from the child's hand and said  "Don't press it playfully!" Everyone at the table looked confused. We didn't realize there was a fish-shaped sign attached to the call button  and the child had picked it up to play with. Why the scolding? It was very disappointing.
When it came time to order  it took forever to get the attention of the staff. We waited 15 minutes for a single bucket of ice. Three different staff members came and went  each looking exhausted from their work.
The cafe staff  however  were friendly and cheerful. But you had to walk to the cafe to order and wait; there was no table service. This is strange. If the food is expensive  having to walk to order drinks  wait in line to pay  and then carry everything back yourself is contradictory to the price level and target market.

The food was beautifully presented with a Thai touch  and the taste was okay. However  I overheard the owner scolding the staff (meaning trying to improve their performance)  indicating a lack of good service mindset and an unsure of their responsibilities. I encourage the owner to improve the quality of their staff. Mahasawat Cafe & Restaurant I've been impressed since Baan Rai I Arun in Ranong  and now I'm grateful to have visited Baan Rai Mahasawat. Thank you so much for creating a restaurant for people in the city. The atmosphere is so relaxing. Khun Best and the team always pay attention to detail in the food  drinks  and desserts. 🫰 Mahasawat Cafe & Restaurant My experience today was quite disappointing. From the moment I entered  I had to stand and wait without any staff to greet me. I had to go and inform them that there were two of us  and then waited a long time for a menu. When I finally received one  there was only one menu  making it inconvenient to choose food. As for the food taste  it was just average  nothing impressive  especially considering the long wait. Only the rice arrived; I didn't know whether to start eating or wait for the rice to arrive  only to have it get cold. I hope the restaurant will improve its service in this regard.

Service was great! Mama Diam 妈妈店 Great food! Great service. No complains! Mama Diam 妈妈店 Found this hidden gem on Prinsep Street and it was such a great experience! Mama Diam looks like a vintage mom-and-pop store from the outsid  but inside it's a chic speakeasy with a great atmosphere. My friends and I came for their Chinese New Year set and it didn't disappoint. The highlight was definitely the free Yu Sheng included in the set  as we had a blast doing the prosperity noodle toss - loud  messy  and exactly what we needed for the festive spirit! The food was a creative twist on local flavors and the service was top-notch. Highly recommending this place if you want a cool concept with solid food! Mama Diam 妈妈店 A true Gem in Singapore ! Nostalgic vibes and great cuisine!

Mama Yaya  Mama Yaya  Mama Yaya The taste was ordinary  nothing amazing considering the price. Every dish tasted like home-cooked food from my mother. I expected a much more exceptional flavor. The portion size per dish was small.
The price is too high for what you get. Mama Yaya Southern Thai-Malay Restaurant
This restaurant is Halal.
The menu offers a mix of familiar and innovative dishes.
Everything we ordered was delicious. I highly recommend the pomelo salad; it's very refreshing. The deep-fried tofu with coconut milk is incredibly smooth and bouncy  like pudding – so delicious! The braised beef with coconut milk  served with the rice as recommended  is fantastic!!!
Both desserts we ordered were delicious.
The Thai tea was fragrant and strong.
The restaurant is beautiful with a great atmosphere. Highly recommended!
There is a service charge.
There is also parking across the street  and there's plenty of parking space. Mama Yaya Everything was delicious  like eating at a friend's house.

restaurant name: Man Chan Korean BBQ Buffet Seacon Square Srinagarindra
text: Man Chan Korean BBQ Buffet Seacon Square Srinagarindra Closed. I drove across the whole city! Man Chan Korean BBQ Buffet Seacon Square Srinagarindra Don't go! Tip's shop is closed and they haven't removed the map. I almost died walking there. Man Chan Korean BBQ Buffet Seacon Square Srinagarindra I visited the Seacon branch yesterday  but the store was closed. Is it closed for renovations or something? It's a real shame  I went all the way there just to eat there. I was really disappointed. Will it reopen? When will it reopen? I'm waiting for it! I really like the food  especially this style. (Please answer my questions!) 🙏🙏🙏😊😊😊 Man Chan Korean BBQ Buffet Seacon Square Srinagarindra Everything was good. The meat was good  the marinated shrimp and salmon were good  and the mushroom soup was good but a little too sweet. I go back very often and could keep going back without a problem. Excellent! Man Chan Korean BBQ Buffet Seacon Square Srinagarindra The food was generally delicious  but the staff should be more attentive to customers. There weren't enough staff; I only saw 3-4 people serving at the front counter. The restaurant was full. At our table  we refilled our drinks  and after finishing our water  we waited for a staff member to refill it. They walked past 4-5 times  looking at our glasses as if they'd seen us  but still didn't pay attention. They should add more server staff. (Seacon Srinakarin branch)

Man Fu Lou  Man Fu Lou The staff were attentive  but the food tasted great. The dim sum was too salty  and the Peking duck had thick but crispy skin  though it smelled a bit oily.
The place is beautiful and the atmosphere is nice. Man Fu Lou  Man Fu Lou The food wasn't hot  and the wait was long. Man Fu Lou Nine of us went and used the 4-for-1 promotion  spending over 8 000 baht. The food was slow to come out and incomplete. We arrived at 1:30 PM and quickly ordered  but the kitchen closes at 2:30 PM. We didn't get the fried rice or the cold pork knuckle  and there was no notification that they were out of stock. We missed our dessert order  and the staff didn't follow up or inform us. When we did ask about the order  they claimed the kitchen was closed. We ordered grilled prawns  but didn't get them; we received a plate of 10 prawns  none of which were hot or fresh. Most of the food wasn't hot. The dim sum was very salty. The Peking duck was greasy and had many feathers; we asked for the duck sauce three times. The staff were overwhelmed and didn't understand the orders; we had to call someone else to replace them. Compared to other places  they would ask if we wanted dessert before the kitchen closes. None of us ate anything afterward  but four of us experienced nausea  vomiting  and severe diarrhea. We're currently analyzing which dish was poisonous  but we definitely won't be going back. restaurant name: Man Fu Yuan Kitchen Samyan Mitrtown
text:
Man Fu Yuan Kitchen Samyan Mitrtown My wife and I were looking for late lunch and found this Chinese restaurant offers interesting afternoon dim sum tea set. As we were not very hungry  we decided to try two different afternoon dim sum sets; small set for my wife and big set for myself  assuming that these sets can’t be big size. It turned out too much for us; one big set would actually be sufficient. Nevertheless  we enjoyed our orders and the shrimps in the dim sum were fresh. Man Fu Yuan Kitchen Samyan Mitrtown Man Fu Yuan Samyan Mitrtown
หม่านฟู่หยวน
Highlight menu ของร้านนี้อร่อยดี
มาลองกันได้
#ชิมกับเชอร์ Man Fu Yuan Kitchen Samyan Mitrtown This Chinese restaurant is located in a shopping mall. The front is open but not too cramped. The chairs are comfortable. The menu is extensive  focusing on dim sum. I didn't order any today  preferring to try their more traditional dishes. I ordered stir-fried green beans with minced pork  sweet and sour fish  and Hong Kong-style crispy pork. The first two were delicious and had the typical Chinese flavors  but the crispy pork had a slight rancid smell. However  after dipping it in the manta ray  it was passable. Overall  it was good. Man Fu Yuan Kitchen Samyan Mitrtown Quality dim sum at pretty reasonable prices is a big reason we keep coming back to Man Fu Yuan (MFY) since it opened two or three years ago in Mitrtown.

Service is consistently good  there are a lot tables  and we can always get a car park in the mall - so big ticks for folks living in Bkk with elderly relatives.

I especially like the duck cheong fun and teochew dumplings. Food from their ala carte menu is also reliably good. Oh  there is also a banquet room for private events.

Though you don’t need to book  MFY gets busy at lunch times so it pays to arrive a bit earlier. Man Fu Yuan Kitchen Samyan Mitrtown Delicious Chinese food  perfect for families.

Recommended: Stir-fried green beans with minced pork.
Fried seafood with XO sauce.
Squid with salted egg.
Fried tofu with chili and salt.
Crispy pork.
Chinese turnip cake.
Shrimp dumplings.

A few other dishes that are not the same.
Fried shrimp with chili and salt. The shrimp were not large and were coated in a thick layer of batter.
Roasted duck with tea leaves.

The staff provided excellent service.
There were mosquitoes in the restaurant  so the staff quickly went to get mosquito repellent.

Will definitely come back again.

restaurant name: Man Ho Chinese Restaurant at JW Marriott Hotel
text:
Man Ho Chinese Restaurant at JW Marriott Hotel  Man Ho Chinese Restaurant at JW Marriott Hotel Our cousin treated us here during our visit to Hong Kong last year and it was amazing. The food art was so exquisite and everything tasted so good. The decor is bright and pretty. The service was exceptional and we had a very memorable experience. Our server explained the menu and each plate with detail. He even brought us an osmonthas jelly dessert on the house! Man Ho Chinese Restaurant at JW Marriott Hotel Our family of four ordered from the dim sum menu at lunch and we thought it was incredible. The dim sum were some of the best we have ever had and many of the pieces were so beautifully made that they were as visually appealing as tasty.  The barbecue pork was so good we had to order a second dish. The siu mai topped with morel mushroom was incredible and memorable. The restaurant also has an extensive wine list with some of the better prices I’ve seen for a restaurant. The noodles we had were fabulously cooked and tasty. Friendly and attentive service.  I would highly recommend Man Ho for a great dim sum experience! Man Ho Chinese Restaurant at JW Marriott Hotel Impressed.  Amazing food especially the abalone. Fresh and tender. Man Ho Chinese Restaurant at JW Marriott Hotel Excellent restaurant with a Michelin star: Hong Kong cuisine.
Try the tasting menu  which is served at just the right speed. #ManeeSoup is uniquely delicious with the addition of shrimp paste.

The quality of the food was excellent  and there was a wide variety. I highly recommend everyone come and try it! Manee Me More Esplanade Ratchadaphisek Amazing food and amazing service! Today I chose Hongtang soup and Okinawa Sukiyaki soup. It's a unique but delicious dish.

Besides the meat  the large  fresh  and clean Sai Nai Xiang Ji is a must-try for those who love offal.

Noodle lovers will love it. There's chewy Chan noodles and fish noodles with a unique flavor. It's delicious. Manee Me More Esplanade Ratchadaphisek It's not bad  it turns out there are four soup flavors to choose from. There's plenty of meat  and the price is pretty reasonable.

Refillable drinks.

Manee Me More Fashion Island The food tasted okay  like typical shabu-shabu. The price was normal. The dipping sauces were good  both the peanut sauce and the house's own recipe.
However  the service was terrible. We ordered duck and almost finished eating when we finally asked about it  only to be told that it hadn't been on the menu for a long time. They should have told the customer that it wasn't available.
We asked for chopsticks and refills of soup  and still didn't get them even when it was time to pay the bill. Manee Me More Fashion Island The buffet offers great value for money. The ingredients are of very high quality  and the food is deliciously authentic Thai  which is hard to find these days. Highly recommended. Manee Me More Fashion Island Delicious  my regular restaurant. Manee Me More Fashion Island The food was delicious and flavorful  and the service was good. restaurant name: Manee Me More Homepro Charan Sanit Wong
text:
Manee Me More Homepro Charan Sanit Wong Brandy soup goes really well with the angel pork. It's really delicious!🤩 Manee Me More Homepro Charan Sanit Wong The Jim Jum set tastes good. The meat is fresh. The bitter jaew sauce is okay. The food is okay. What's not okay is the staff. It's like they don't want to serve. It's hard to call them. Whatever you order  they say "Huh?" and they even swear  but it's not a bad word. I'll go back again  but I might have to ignore the staff. But if they improve their service  the food will be even more delicious. Manee Me More Homepro Charan Sanit Wong Called at 8:00 PM and the staff said they were no longer accepting customers because the shop closes at 9:00 PM. Some days I called and they said they were still accepting customers. They were completely unreliable. I was not impressed. It was like I had to beg them to give me money. The staff didn't want to serve me. Manee Me More Homepro Charan Sanit Wong We didn't order the buffet  so we ordered the set menu because we weren't hungry and the price was a bit high.
I liked the two-flavor dipping sauce  which was delicious. Ordering the set menu for three people was just right.
It's a small restaurant in HomePro. There's not much seating  but it's not very crowded. The staff are a bit difficult to get ahold of.
There are two soup counters  and there are many different soup bases to choose from  some of which require an additional fee. Overall  the taste is good. Manee Me More Homepro Charan Sanit Wong The staff is terrible  especially the store manager. They need a lot of improvement.

Manee Me More Homepro Rama 3 The food is good  but the staff doesn't pay much attention to customers. When ordering more food or paying  I can't find the staff. Manee Me More Homepro Rama 3 Oom Manee Me More Homepro Rama 3 The staff didn't mention that there was a buffet at all.
My girlfriend and I finished ordering food and were about to wash our hands before eating when we realized there was a buffet at a very attractive price.
We only found out from a friend who had complained to us that they booked through a red app and got a buffet discount. The staff didn't mention anything at all. I saw that the drinks were refillable  so I chose the refill option. The drinks were very sweet. There weren't many choices; I only realized this when I went to get them myself. It's a shame  if I had known  I would have just chosen plain water. The staff didn't tell me anything. After the food arrived  the glass shrimp wasn't very fresh. The pork looked old. The fish noodles were rock hard. The frozen fish noodles were tough and shriveled.
The dipping sauces were traditional  edible  but it would be better if there were more options. The seafood sauce tasted very much like bottled lime juice.
But the pork liver was delicious! I highly recommend it. The pork was good value  and the vegetable set was worth it. The bill was reasonable for a mall restaurant. If they adjusted a few things  I think it would be great. Manee Me More Homepro Rama 3 This is another restaurant I like. The soup is delicious and must be brandy. As for the shrimp soup  I'm indifferent. It's more expensive. The sauce is a bit too sweet  but it's a strange taste. The quality is good. There's a lot of food. At that time  I ate there but there wasn't a buffet. Now there is a buffet. And the staff at this branch provide thorough service  but the food in the kitchen comes out slowly. MoVida BKK  MoVida BKK  MoVida BKK I made a reservation based on the reviews. It's a restaurant located in an alley  not visible from the main road  but there's plenty of parking. The atmosphere inside is nice  perfect for a special occasion.
For the first dish  I ordered what I liked – Jamon Iberico. I'd heard it was the best ham. When it was served  it came with crispy  toasted bread  grated garlic  and a lightly seasoned tomato sauce. The amount of ham on top was huge; I recommend getting 6 slices per piece of bread. The taste was incredibly umami  an explosion in my mouth!
The next course consisted of small  tasty bites. There was cangrejo (crab)  the sauce was delicious and the crab was okay; croqueta (lobster croqueta with bell peppers on top); and croqueta with Iberico ham  which was the best of the three.
For the main course  I ordered bistec  beef served with broccoli  which was incredibly fragrant. The sauce enhanced the flavor of the dish perfectly.
Another dish was octopus. The chef said the sauce was made from pork sausage  bell peppers  and other ingredients I can't remember.
The staff recommended a very good white wine; it was easy to drink and paired well with the food. I liked everything.

All the dishes had a variety of complex flavors. Overall  I was very impressed.
Considering the price  I'll need to save up for a while longer! 5555

We reported it on the second day too - we were offered a change of room but we were leaving the following day so it seemed like too much hassle.

They offer a 19-course Signature menu to start with  but since I was there  I went for the Ultimate course  the biggest one with a full 23 courses! Yes  you read that right  23 courses! 😆 At first  I thought  "A woman with a big appetite like me will be fine " but in reality  this was one of the few times I've had an omakase meal where I felt incredibly full. The chef really went all out!

Each dish had a playful fusion twist  making it anything but repetitive. The ingredients were high-quality and vibrant. The chef was friendly and fun to talk to  creating a relaxed and comfortable atmosphere at the counter. And the ice cream at the end was pure bliss – you could get as many layers as you wanted! 🤣

Overall  it was an omakase experience that was fun  great value  and beautifully photogenic. For those who enjoy fusion omakase with intricate details  character  and a playful touch  this restaurant is another place you should try at least once. 🍑🍣

P.S. Convenient parking is available.

- The food focuses on quality ingredients  with minimal sauces  emphasizing skillful cooking. The seafood dipping sauce is spicy and delicious.
- For guests staying at the hotel  there's a fitness center and meeting rooms available.
- Activities within the restaurant include free pool tables  foosball  and hockey.
- There are seating areas both inside and outside  separate from the restaurant itself. Sitting outside is very relaxing in this weather.
- There's a DJ playing beats to make dining even more enjoyable.
- What impressed me most was the abundance of staff; they're always available to assist you  and their service mind is exceptional.
In short  I highly recommend it every Friday  Saturday  and Sunday evening. Mr.Park BBQ Sukhumvit This place is a total mess.
It was the worst restaurant in 2025.
I chose the 500 baht beef course
and ordered kimchi stew and soft tofu stew. The kimchi stew was served in a ttukbaegi (earthenware pot)  but it tasted like leftovers. The soft tofu stew tasted...unbelievable. I cooked it myself with a generous handful of minced garlic.
They served about 3 handfuls of plain rice.
The most surprising thing was that they said it was a premium BBQ buffet  but there was no kimchi. LOL
How can a Korean restaurant that sells pork belly not have kimchi? LOL
The side dishes were nothing to eat. Just seasoned bean sprouts  pickled radish  quail eggs  and potato salad.
I wasn't expecting much from the meat. The beef was probably shabu-shabu meat 
so it was inedible because it lost moisture while grilling.
The pork was probably macro meat  so it was just okay. The highlight was that they didn't have any lettuce wraps  so I asked why  and they brought them out. They filled all four vegetable containers with lettuce  lol. If you go to the store  they have 3-4 different types of lettuce wraps. I wasn't expecting perilla leaves  but they were so insincere. It's just... no kimchi... Mr.Park BBQ Sukhumvit to charge 200 per person for a few leftovers is really ridiculous. now I know why this place is empty all the time ... Mr.Park BBQ Sukhumvit The staff at this branch seemed uninterested in serving me. They were often out of stock and didn't refill it. When I asked the staff  they said it was out of stock  even though I had just arrived at 7:00 PM. I've been out of stock several times  sometimes with side dishes and appetizers. I hope this branch can improve and compete with the first two. This branch is in the city  but the staff seems unwilling to serve customers.

text:
NEXT2 Cafe at Shangri-La Hotel Bangkok Club Sandwich was amazing and juices were very fresh. Great spot for lunch along the Chao Phraya River NEXT2 Cafe at Shangri-La Hotel Bangkok That day  I went to use the service at Next2. I saw an Asian-looking male staff member who was not wearing a suit; he was wearing a beige shirt and shouting at the waitress who was making coffee. I thought she was doing her job well  so the situation was very unpleasant. I came to enjoy a relaxing breakfast  but instead I had to witness something very disturbing. 😕👎🏻

As for the food  I thought it was delicious and of good quality. The prices were quite reasonable and not too expensive.😉 NEXT2 Cafe at Shangri-La Hotel Bangkok We go to the buffet every year – preferably seafood on weekends – at the Next 2 Shangri-La. We're always hotel guests and also Shangri-La members. Unfortunately  the quality has deteriorated drastically. There were no more oysters… but there were lobsters – apparently more popular with the Chinese guests. They used to have them too  but alongside the oysters and some other special seafood delicacies. Nothing against Chinese guests  but for us (Germans and Europeans)  the oysters were a particular highlight. The international dishes are as good as ever. However  compared to previous years  quite a few things about the buffet were different: the staff seemed uninspired… lacking empathy and enthusiasm. The service has always been rather slow. But now: we ordered a wine for 3600 THB and waited 20 minutes for it  then a waitress came and explained that she no longer had the wine in stock… She couldn't tell us which wines were available. That pretty much says it all. The prices are high: 2400 THB net for the buffet – excluding taxes and VAT – per person. Including taxes plus 18%. We always enjoyed it so much  and we tried again this year. Thanks to our early reservation  we even got a great spot. But it doesn't have the same effect. NEXT2 Cafe at Shangri-La Hotel Bangkok Food was great. Service is mostly good  but it depends on customer volume. (Some days our coffee takes very long  it’s because the staff are busy)

restaurant name: Nan Yuan Chinese Restaurant Avani Ratchada Bangkok
text:
Nan Yuan Chinese Restaurant Avani Ratchada Bangkok The atmosphere is fantastic; luxurious and modern with a perfect blend of Chinese influences. It's warm  private  and ideal for family gatherings  dates with friends  or casual celebrations. It's incredibly convenient to get to – just take the MRT to Phra Ram 9  Exit 1  and you're there!

Highlights ✨
Over 40+ items of dim sum  all freshly made and served hot in every basket.
From bouncy shrimp dumplings and generously filled steamed siu mai to gooey lava buns 
and a complete range of steamed and fried dishes  the taste lives up to its reputation as a premium Chinese restaurant. Nan Yuan Chinese Restaurant Avani Ratchada Bangkok It didn't live up to my expectations. I had some expectations  but as expected  I shouldn't expect the same quality as Chinese restaurants in Singapore  Hong Kong  and Taiwan. It wasn't a great value for the price. My family isn't a huge eater  so I'm not sure if I made a mistake by choosing options like the all-you-can-eat dim sum.

The portions were satisfactory  but the taste was average. Nan Yuan Chinese Restaurant Avani Ratchada Bangkok The restaurant has a great atmosphere. The tables are large and comfortable  perfect for families or friends.
There's a huge selection of dim sum  over 40 varieties  all freshly made and served hot in every basket. Everything is delicious  especially the shrimp dumplings  steamed shrimp siu mai  steamed chicken feet with black bean sauce  crab claws  and the fried items which aren't greasy. The lava buns/cantaloupe sago are amazing. It's conveniently located near the MRT Rama 9 station. Great value  delicious  and premium. Highly recommended! Nan Yuan Chinese Restaurant Avani Ratchada Bangkok I ordered the 880 baht Hong Kong-style dim sum buffet  and the chef's cooking skills were as superb as ever. Nan Yuan Chinese Restaurant Avani Ratchada Bangkok The restaurant is located in the Avani Hotel in the Fortune Tower. There's dedicated parking in the hotel zone  but it's very limited and crowded even during the day on weekdays  so we had to use the Fortune Tower parking lot.

I booked through Hungry Hub for over 1 000 baht per person. The included dishes were fish maw soup  roast pork and crispy pork  and shrimp salad.

The roast pork was delicious  tender  and juicy. The crispy pork was crispy and delicious  not too tough.
The shrimp salad was a bit disappointing. I thought it would be a fried shrimp salad like other places  but it turned out to be just a few boiled shrimps sprinkled on top of the salad  so I was a bit confused about how it would go together.

The dim sum was delicious. The siao long bao had thin  fragrant  and delicious skin  just as recommended.
The har gow and shumai were delicious  with firm  soft  and bouncy textures.
The pork bones were well seasoned  but a bit tough  making it difficult to break apart.
The rice noodle rolls were delicious  but the skin was too thick  making it difficult to taste the sauce or filling.

The highlight was the Peking duck. I really liked the duck skin. It was crispy  fragrant  and not too oily. The sauce was sweet and salty. Try adding the crispy rice crackers served with the duck skin. It adds a nice crunch to counteract the richness of the dish  and it's a great change of flavor.
The staff were excellent  constantly refilling water. The food came out very quickly.

Nikuro Yakiniku + Good quality meat
+ All food is freshly prepared  meat is sliced ​​fresh to order
+ Excellent service
+ The elevator is incredibly scary (on the way back down the front stairs  there was a huge mess). It was a peak experience.
- The time limit is a bit short  an hour and a half (because each order requires waiting for preparation and slicing)
- There's a grill fee for solo diners. The cheapest buffet set is 100 baht  the middle one is 50 baht.
- The grill is small with wide gaps between the grates  resulting in: 1. Uneven heat distribution; you have to hold your hand to feel where it's hot. 2. Charcoal is too close to the grate; if you're not careful  the thinly sliced ​​meat edges will fall onto the charcoal almost touching the grate.

Overall  I give it 4.5/5 because the food and service were excellent and I was impressed. Nikuro Yakiniku I checked Google Maps and found another restaurant that was full  so I came to this one. I came without any expectations  just wanting to try a new place. It turned out to be surprisingly good! The food was amazing; the meat was delicious. Everything I ordered was tasty  even the sausage  which is hard to find well. They grill everything over charcoal. The restaurant is on the top floor  probably around the third or fourth floor. You have to take the stairs from the front of the restaurant or the elevator next to it  as the other floors aren't in use. Once inside  it felt like I was in Japan! You can even see the trains going by. The restaurant is spacious and comfortable  with a good ventilation system. It's great! If you want to try a new restaurant  I highly recommend this one. Oh  I forgot to mention  it's an all-you-can-eat buffet with both pork and beef  and they even have an all-you-can-drink beer buffet! 🍺 Nikuro Yakiniku This yakiniku restaurant is near my house. Normally  parking is difficult  but on New Year's Eve  there were parking spaces available in front of the restaurant  probably enough for two cars. The restaurant is on the 3rd floor  accessed via a service elevator like in a spy movie – adding a bit of excitement to life!
The 3rd floor is a converted rooftop  with cool air conditioning. Today  I ordered a large set of beef and a small set of pork. The grill was a bit small  perfect for à la carte eating without rushing. If it were buffet style  I'd think twice about grilling fast enough.
Regarding the taste  I liked the brisket and crying tiger beef. The other cuts were too tough. The pork looked a bit pale and bland  but it was delicious  tender  and not dry at all. Both dipping sauces were tasty. The vegetables were fresh and sweet. The smoke extraction system was too weak  filling the room with smoke. Nikuro Yakiniku The food tasted great and the ingredients were high quality. I'll definitely come back again. Nikuro Yakiniku The food was delicious. Booked through Hungry Hub  399 baht per person.
The service was very fast. The food came continuously without interruption. The fried items were hot and fresh. The meat was delicious and not tough at all. The dipping sauces were very tasty. The charcoal was strong.
This price is very worthwhile.

Nimitr Rooftop by 137 Pillars We had a fantastic suite on the 32nd floor with a magnificent view. The room service deserves special mention – absolutely top-notch  attentive  and reliable both morning and evening. The breakfast is highly recommended and of a very high quality.

In our opinion  the location is the only real drawback: While the train station is within walking distance  there's almost always traffic congestion around the hotel  making arrivals and departures unnecessarily slow.

Overall  a very good hotel with excellent service and great comfort – but the location is a bit of a letdown. Nimitr Rooftop by 137 Pillars A Refined Urban Sanctuary Above Bangkok

What makes 137 Pillars Suites & Residences Bangkok so special is how effortlessly it blends boutique intimacy with elevated city luxury. This marks my 5th Small Luxury Hotels of the World property  and it remains one of my absolute favorites. The hotel is also proudly recognised with the prestigious 1 Michelin Key award  a true testament to its exceptional quality and experience.

Set in the Sukhumvit area near Phrom Phong  the hotel feels calm and residential despite its central location. With a limited number of suites  the atmosphere is intentionally intimate  offering privacy and highly personalised service. I stayed in the Ayutthaya Suite  which perfectly reflects the hotel’s refined  residential feel.

The design combines contemporary elegance with warm Thai influences  creating spaces that feel polished yet welcoming. One of the highlights is the rooftop infinity pool  offering uninterrupted views over Bangkok’s skyline and a serene  exclusive atmosphere. Wellness continues at Nitra Spa & Wellness  reinforcing the sense of calm that defines the property.

Dining completes the experience  from refined Thai cuisine at Nimitr to relaxed international flavours at Bangkok Trading Post Bistro & Bar. Every detail feels thoughtful  measured  and quietly luxurious  making this 1 Michelin Key hotel a true urban retreat above the city. Nimitr Rooftop by 137 Pillars I spend on average more than 6 months a year in hotels. This hotel is clearly one of the best I’ve stayed in. As a Diamond Member for All and Platinum Hilton  I get benefits  I got all those benefits at 137 on my first stay. I’ll definitely be back. It was an 8 night stay in a suite  so any issues would’ve worked there way out if there were any. There really wasn’t. Nimitr Rooftop by 137 Pillars  Nimitr Rooftop by 137 Pillars This is my second stay at 137pillars. My first stay was a memorable one – the comfortable rooms  the bright and airy atmosphere  the friendly staff  the amazing infinity pool  the beautiful dining environment  and the delicious breakfast. This time  everything was wonderful from check-in to check-out  and I hope to have the opportunity to stay here again. The salmon and hamachi were sliced ​​incredibly thin  while the tuna was sliced ​​just right—much thicker than the salmon and hamachi  to the point where it was noticeable. At first  I thought this was normal for the restaurant  as it was my first time there. However  after returning and looking at other reviews  I noticed that other customers hadn't received sashimi as thinly sliced ​​as our table. I suspect it's more a matter of the chef's inconsistent portion control. I'd like to share this with you.

The other dish I tried was the Hitsumabushi with beef for 320 baht. It was delicious  especially the broth in the pot  which had a distinct flavor. The egg on top was fresh and didn't smell fishy.

Niwa Cafe By Rangnok Land Take the escalator next to Kyoto Theater at Kyoto Station to the 7th floor. ^^
A modern space enveloped in light and art. 🎨✨

✔︎NIWA Ohagi (rice balls) 1 160 yen
(Drink set)

A tea room in the center of the restaurant creates a wonderful atmosphere. 👌
Recommended for a quiet  relaxing space. 🍃

Enjoy Kyoto ingredients on the menu  including Kyoto dashi curry  Kyoto oden  and Kyotanba beef sukiyaki burgers. 🍢🤍

The menu remains the same from 11:00 AM to 10:00 PM  making it a great place for a late lunch. 🍽️

There's also a counter  so you can drop in by yourself! Niwa Cafe By Rangnok Land Good place to have a cup of coffee while waiting for train. Unlike other places in the station  it was not busy around 1 pm weekday. Niwa Cafe By Rangnok Land I went there during the daytime on a weekday. I was the only customer  which made me a little unsure  but I didn't have time to look for another restaurant.
The atmosphere  menu  and prices all felt catered to foreign tourists  which put me off.
There were no comfortable seats  so it might be fine for a quick tea or a drink.
It took a long time for the water to be brought after I sat down  and I wondered if it was self-service...? The plastic cup was dented when I held it  but it was clear  so I thought it was glass  and picked it up without looking  which surprised me. It felt somehow cheap  and didn't fit with the restaurant's artistic concept.
The portions of the food were so small I wondered  "Is this really for this price?" 🤣 The rice balls were barely warm  but the obanzai dishes were all cold. The flavors were incredibly strong. It's been a while since I've experienced the wide range of hits and misses in Kyoto restaurants.
The interior seems elaborate  but why is the music so loud? I understand if it's a bar at night  but it's a shame there's no relaxing element. So I personally recommend sitting by the window and looking out the window. I was grateful for the tables where you can charge your devices. Niwa Cafe By Rangnok Land Very delicious matcha espresso  a unique drink combining the greatest caffeine mixture. Ambiance and vibe is 10/10 . In a tucked away hidden part of Kyoto station on the 7th floor but  unique experience taking the escalator after and through the skybridge after and seeing all the steps from above Niwa Cafe By Rangnok Land A cafe with a great design.
The interior is like a museum  with the cafe feeling like an afterthought.
It's so stylish that it feels a bit unsettling.

Cashless payment options available.

Niyomkom  Niyomkom 😁 Niyomkom I have been an avid fan of Nakhon for many years. But today  I found a cockroach in the kailan. Absolutely disgusting. Never coming back here. Niyomkom The foods were good  but all the portions are quite small. Hope they could improve the service a little bit. We were asked to order the food outside before we can be seated  and the waitress that having the dental braces and a mole on her cheek took our order  I asked her (pointed the fish on the menu) if this is spicy  she said no  then I pointed another one and asked again  she answer no too  as I saw the picture of that fish  it has many chili on it  I just asked to be sure since my toddler daughter can’t eat even just a little bit spicy  and in case she wasn’t aware I had pointed to another fish  I mean this fish  is it spicy? She became impatient  said to me  “you asked me  and I already told you is not spicy”. I was abit shocked with her reaction  told her no need to be angry  she started to say  “I’m not angry  but you asked me if it’s spicy not  I said no  I already said no and you still want to ask me if it’s spicy”. I just ignore her and ordered the rest. When we got our seat and going in  she was behind us and mumbling with her country language  only use one in English “angry” which obviously want to let me hear it.

I came here for few times  understand they were very busy and it’s always long q  I can also understand if some mistake made or it’s slow  and I always believe respect should be given mutually  thus I always said thank you each time the waitress serve the food. This is really came out of nowhere. Niyomkom It’s been years since my last visit to Nakhon Kitchen  and I’m happy to report that the food standards haven’t dropped a bit. I arrived at 5:00 PM and was seated immediately  though I’d definitely recommend coming during off-peak hours like this  as the queues during peak times are legendary.

The ambiance is spot on; Thai music  Thai servers  and condiments right on the table. It feels very authentic  like stepping into a local eatery in Bangkok.

What I Ordered ($17 total):
• Basil Chicken Rice: Very tasty and hit the spot. My only minor gripe was the fried egg; the yolk was fully cooked/dry  whereas I was hoping for that classic runny yolk.

• Tom Kha Soup: Absolutely delicious. It was savory  creamy  and not overly spicy. I polished off every drop! The fried fish and prawns were fresh  though there was only one prawn in the bowl.

• Thai Green Milk Tea: A perfect  refreshing companion to the meal.

The Verdict:
For $17  the quality and portion sizes are excellent. It’s clear why this place remains a staple for Thai food lovers. Despite the small "egg situation" and the single prawn  the overall flavors and experience were fantastic.

Pro Tip: Get there before the dinner rush to skip the line! I’ll definitely be back.

restaurant name: Oishi Buffet The Mall Bangkapi (New Concept)
text:
Oishi Buffet The Mall Bangkapi (New Concept) Good food  hardworking staff  good service. The food is delicious. Oishi Buffet The Mall Bangkapi (New Concept) This new branch format offers various price options. Ordering food via QR code is convenient; you don't have to walk to the buffet yourself (except for soup and drinks). I secretly feel that this branch tastes better than others  probably because the food is cooked dish by dish  especially the sushi and sashimi  which are fresh and don't have to be left sitting in the air conditioning waiting for customers to serve themselves like at other branches. Oishi Buffet The Mall Bangkapi (New Concept) The food is delicious and there's a wide variety to choose from  depending on the set you select  including sushi  grilled dishes  various spaghetti options  salads  and much more.

The desserts are also tasty  even if you order the most value-for-money set; there's still a decent selection of fruits  ice cream  cheesecake  and banoffee pie.

Some dishes take a very long time to come out  and orders are sometimes incomplete. If your order hasn't arrived yet  be sure to ask the staff first  otherwise the food will come later all at once  and you might not be able to finish it all. Oishi Buffet The Mall Bangkapi (New Concept) I had a late lunch. You can scan the barcode to choose from the menu. The sashimi is average  and the sushi is decent. It's nice to be able to order a variety of dishes when you're really hungry  but the taste itself isn't particularly great  so keep that in mind. (I was surprised that they served local EST cola  not Coca-Cola or Pepsi.) Oishi Buffet The Mall Bangkapi (New Concept) I really like the larb sauce. Everything is good. The menu is unique. In terms of quality  I think for the same price range  it's not comparable to other nearby restaurants. An Update on Buffet Locations You Have to Try Your Luck Day By Day 🍱🍣
"If you're looking for a Japanese buffet with ingredients that are more premium than the average  Oishi Eaterium is still the answer... but it has to be admitted that the 'wow' factor seems to have changed slightly."

Today  I visited Oishi Eaterium at Central Rama 9. It's known for its Japanese food district-style decor and upgraded ingredient quality compared to the original Oishi Buffet. However  from my recent visit  there are a few things I want to share to help you prepare before you go!

🌟 Things That Remain Standard (Good Points)
The quality of the fish and main ingredients is still better than most buffets. The fish is fresh  beautifully marbled  and you can really feel the premium quality that justifies the price.

The staff at this branch provide excellent service. They are cheerful  attentive  and constantly clearing plates. I have to commend them for that.

⚠️ Things That Are a Little Disappointing
The Familiar Menu: Anyone hoping for "new menus" or seasonal menus might be a little disappointed  as the menu remains quite consistent. There's nothing particularly exciting like during the initial launch.

Attention to detail: I noticed that some of the hot dishes I ordered were served "not hot " as if they had been prepared in advance and then delivered. This resulted in a loss of flavor and texture (I wish the restaurant would improve their food temperature control before serving).

Desserts: Personally  I felt the dessert section had "dropped" in quality. The presentation wasn't as appealing as before  and the taste was average  not quite a memorable ending to the meal.

💡 Tips for visiting:
Focus on sushi and sashimi: If you're coming for this  you'll definitely get your money's worth.

Check hot dishes: If you receive food that isn't hot  I recommend informing the staff immediately. This branch has good service  and they're happy to prepare a new dish.

Suitable for: Families or groups of working professionals who want to try a variety of Japanese food in one place but aren't overly concerned with innovative menu items.

Overall conclusion: Oishi Eaterium remains reliable in terms of ingredients. However  if the restaurant diversifies its menu and focuses on the freshness of hot dishes  it could easily regain its position as a top-rated buffet.

Okura Cruise The pinnacle of Chao Phraya River dinner cruises.
This dinner cruise is even more special than the larger dinner cruises.
Even Japanese people find the food delicious  and it's sure to impress non-Japanese guests even more.
The service and courtesy are beyond what you'd expect from a typical restaurant staff member  and they're very welcoming.
I arrived at the reception at Asiatique at 6:00. There was a private waiting room with tea or coffee  and the air conditioning was on  so even if I arrived early  I could relax until the time came. I was then guided to board. Until departure  I sat on the rooftop  watched the sunset and the Asiatique illuminations  and ordered drinks and toasted.
Since the teppanyaki course started at 7:15  I went downstairs to the teppan counter. There were counter seats for 9 or 10 people  one on each side. If you sit on the left side  facing the direction of travel  you could dine while looking out the large glass window at Wat Arun  illuminated around the time of the meal.
The boat docked in that area for about 5 minutes  so I had plenty of time to go up to the rooftop and take photos.
I also enjoyed the teppan performance. If you move to the rooftop  they will bring your dessert there.
The cruise is designed to avoid large dinner cruise ships with loud music  so even though it's fast  there is very little rocking  making it a stable and luxurious dinner cruise.
The rooftop has tatami seating  so you can take off your shoes and relax.The flat design makes it feel like you're on a giant magic carpet floating on the river  enjoying your drinks and  if you're lucky  seeing fireworks  the night view with icons  and bridges passing overhead.
It was a very satisfying  top-class dinner cruise. Okura Cruise  Okura Cruise Wonderful experience- the teppanyaki menu was delicious and the boat itself is beautiful  spacious and relaxing. Omakase Ten I was very impressed. The food and staff were of excellent quality. 5 stars! Omakase Ten Absolutely impressed with Omakase Ten. Every course was beautifully crafted with top-quality ingredients  and you can truly feel the attention to detail in both flavor and presentation.

The chef explained each dish clearly and with passion  which made the experience even more enjoyable. The progression of flavors was well-balanced  and nothing felt rushed.

Service was warm  professional  and attentive without being intrusive. The atmosphere is calm  elegant  and perfect for a special occasion.

Definitely one of the omakase experiences I would happily return to and recommend. Omakase Ten I would give Omakase Ten a two-star rating. While the ingredients themselves seemed to be of good quality  the overall execution fell short of expectations.
The biggest issue was the menu design. It felt like the chef tried to combine too many elements without fully thinking through how they would work together. Several dishes came across as a mishmash of ideas that didn’t quite harmonize  leaving the meal feeling disjointed rather than cohesive.
Toward the end of the meal  we were served a soup that was very ordinary. Unfortunately  that final dish erased much of the “fancy” or special feeling the earlier courses were trying to build. Ending the meal on such a basic note felt like a missed opportunity and  frankly  a mistake in pacing and concept.
Another challenge was the lack of interaction with the chef due to a language barrier. In a restaurant that appears to aim for a more curated or thoughtful dining experience  that personal connection matters  and its absence made the experience feel less engaging.
Overall  the food was made with decent ingredients  but the preparation and conceptual planning were not refined enough to let those ingredients shine. With better menu cohesion and execution  this could be a much stronger experience  but as it stands  it did not justify a higher rating. Omakase Ten We had an amazing omakase experience. Opium Bar 🇯🇵 Japanese Version

[The Mysterious Opium Bar  Renovated from a 120-Year-Old Pharmacy]

Amid the hustle and bustle of Yaowarat  a sudden silence emerges in the dusty  hot wholesale district  and the eerie  flickering sign of "Opium Bar" appears.

Even from its very presence  a special atmosphere emanates...

I entered the bar as soon as it opened at 4:00 PM.

Taking a retro elevator to the fourth floor  I found myself in a breathtakingly beautiful space  with the setting sun streaming through stained glass.

The antique  dark atmosphere creates a unique atmosphere.

I ordered the "Inseparable" from "Song Wat." This cocktail  incorporating traditional Chinese medicine  was surprisingly delicate and profound—a true "liquid surreality." The pork fried rice was also delicious. 😋

The staff were also warm and helpful  gently easing the tension of my solo travels.

I was impressed to see how a former opium den had been transformed into such a sophisticated space.
This is a special place I would definitely like to return to if I visit Bangkok again.

★★★★★

⸻

🇬🇧 English Version

A Hidden Gem in Yaowarat – Opium Bar

In the middle of Yaowarat’s heat and chaos  surrounded by dusty wholesale streets  a sudden silence appears — and the mysterious sign of Opium Bar gently flickers. From that very moment  you know you’ve found somewhere special.

I arrived right at 4:00 PM. A vintage elevator took me to the 4th floor  where soft sunset light poured through colorful stained glass  creating a breathtakingly beautiful space. The antique  dark atmosphere gives the bar a truly one-of-a-kind character.

I ordered “Inseparable” from the Song Wat section. The herbal-infused cocktail was delicate  complex  and unforgettable — truly a “Liquid Surreality.” The pork fried rice was excellent as well😋

The staff were incredibly warm and welcoming  easing the nerves of a solo traveler like me.

It’s It's inspiring to see how a former opium den has transformed into such a refined and artistic destination.
I will definitely return on my next trip to Bangkok.

I ordered the calamari appetiser and beef wellington main.

The calamari wasn’t fresh. It was quite chewy but that’s not why I’m leaving the review.

The beef wellington pastry was completely uncooked under the surface layer. I could still mould the dough into different shapes with my cutlery.

I ate the meal but left 100% of the pastry untouched.

I asked for the bill and no questions were raised about my meal or the whole untouched pastry on my plate. Upon receiving the bill I told the waiter the pasty wasn’t cooked. He acknowledged my opinion and walked away with the cash and he brought my change.

I received no apology or further communication from any staff member. And I paid full price.

The venue inside is quite nice but I would never come and eat here again. Otter in The Rain (Phuket) The restaurant is beautiful and the mood is great. text:
PING PIMAN Amdaeng by NJOY Prestige Hotel เข้าพัก 1 คืน วันที่ 14 กุมภา 2569 ก่อนไปได้มีแชทคุยกับทางโรงแรม ซึ่งทางโรงแรมบอกว่าจะมีการจัดดินเนอร์พิเศษสำหรับสำหรับผู้ที่จองมาเท่านั้น เราเลยสอบถามไปว่าถ้า เราไม่จองดินเนอร์พิเศษสามารถสั่งเป็น a la carte มาทานได้ไหม ซึ่งทางโรงแรมบอกว่าได้แต่อาหารอาจจะได้ช้าหน่อยเพราะต้องบริการให้ลูกค้าที่จองก่อน อันนี้เข้าใจได้ไม่เป็นไร แต่พอไปถึงที่พักเข้าเช็คอิน พนักงานบอกว่าจะไม่มีการทำอาหารให้ลูกค้าที่ไม่ได้จองคอร์สพิเศษ ซึ่งหมายความว่าเราต้องออกไปหาอาหารทานเอง แถม rooftop bar ก็จะไม่มีให้บริการ เรียกได้ว่าผู้ที่เข้าพักที่ไม่ได้จองคอร์สพิเศษ โรงแรมไม่มีอาหารให้บริการเลย ไม่ตรงกับข้อมูลที่ได้คุยกันก่อนจะมา ที่บอกว่าจะดูแลเป็นพิเศษ บลาๆๆๆ ผิดหวังแล้วหนึ่ง มาถึงเรื่องห้องพัก แอร์ไม่เย็นเลย คิดว่านอนให้มันจบๆ อีกวันกลับบ้าน มาถึงอาหารเช้า รสชาติแสนจะธรรมดา ในเมนูที่บอกว่าเป็นซิกเนเจอร์ของโรงแรม ไม่เห็นมีอะไรพิเศษตรงไหน รู้สึกผิดหวังจริงๆ ราคาที่จ่ายสวนทางกับสิ่งที่ได้รับมาก ทานอาหารเช้าเสร็จ รีบเช็คเอาท์ ให้เร็วที่สุด เอาจริงๆ เรามองว่าในส่วนที่ดีของโรงแรมนี้ก็คือวิวแม่น้ำเจ้าพระยา แค่นั้นจริงๆ

==================

Stayed for 1 night on February 14  2026.

Before arriving  I had a chat conversation with the hotel. I was informed that there would be a special dinner available exclusively for guests who had made reservations. I then asked whether  if we did not book the special dinner  we could order à la carte instead. The hotel confirmed that it would be possible  although the food might take longer as priority would be given to guests who had reserved the special course. That was completely understandable.

However  upon arrival and check-in  the staff informed us that there would be no food service at all for guests who had not reserved the special dinner course. This meant we had to go to find food ourselves. In addition  the rooftop bar was not operating. In other words  guests who did not book the special course were left with no food or beverage options at the hotel. This directly contradicted the information we were given before arrival and was very disappointing.

As for the room  the air conditioning was not cool at all. We decided to simply get through the night and leave the next day.

Breakfast was equally disappointing. The taste was extremely ordinary. Even the dishes described as the hotel’s signature items were nothing special.

Overall  the price paid was completely disproportionate to what we received. After finishing breakfast  we checked out as quickly as possible.

Honestly  the only positive aspect of this hotel is the view of the Chao Phraya River — and that is truly the only highlight. PING PIMAN Amdaeng by NJOY Prestige Hotel Amdaend was amazing!!! Ee spent 4 night there while in Bangkok and it was a wonderful experience. The staff was super sweet  very helpful  especially Peter. Super nice guy. We had a room with the view of the river - gorgeous. The balcony was super cute  the room with the bathtub  comfy bed... I wanted to place that had a beautiful decor as opposed to the typical "hotel room feel" and this had that. The aesthetic was on point. If you like priclvacy  note that the bathroom area is only separated by a curtain. But we lo Ed it. The river views. I love that you choose your breakfast the day before -very sustainable. It's a small hotel and felt like home. We also had drinks and snacks at the rooftop cafe - recommended. Everything was great and we will 100% be back to Amdaeng. PING PIMAN Amdaeng by NJOY Prestige Hotel This hotel is more style over substance. The low rating is primarily because it claims to be something it is clearly not and charges accordingly. This is not a 4 star hotel and even though most of the staff are lovely the facilities are lacking in quality and functionality.

Cons: Average facilities and poor practical design- for example Light switches are incorrectly wired  shower and toilet have glass windows facing the bed with only a thin curtain to provide privacy  poor noise insulation - could hear telephone ringing down at reception constantly and shower being used from above  toilet seat would not stay up which despite being a minor issue it is not what you would expect from a 4* hotel. Mediocre restaurant with limited breakfast and unjustifiably expensive bar. The Swimming pool is not what they advertise it to be  it is most of the times kept covered and looks poorly maintained and likely unsafe  which seems to be a long term problem according to other reviews but this is not mentioned on their website or booking.com. No spare key for room and had to hand in our key to reception to have the room cleaned. Overall the facilities and service do not justify the price  which is on par with other hotels in the area who offer vastly better facilities.

Pros: Nice working class area with very friendly local people where you can get a sense of the real Bangkok. This includes a number of very authentic good street food vendors. Nice views of the river  even though constant riverboat cruises passing by can be slightly annoying. Nice decor and an interesting building. PING PIMAN Amdaeng by NJOY Prestige Hotel The hotel room was nice.

The hotel is somewhat remote and difficult to find.

The pool was terrible. It was always covered and not attractively landscaped. There were no comfortable loungers  as it was right next to the entrance.

The view from the room was not nice.

Definitely too expensive! We expected more for the price. PING PIMAN Amdaeng by NJOY Prestige Hotel Located right next to the Chao Phraya River  approximately 1.5km (15 minutes) walk from Iconsiam and Iconsiam 1 ferry wharf. Small but quaint hotel.
The staff are friendly but upon arrival gave very limited information about the stay  nothing about breakfast service or WI-FI. Room was decent  with all amenities. Just a little dated with patchy paintwork.
The rooftop bar (Hidden Gem) was quite good  with a great view overlooking the river and Bangkok cityscape. Food at their restaurant for evening was very good and well priced.
There's also a saltwater swimming pool out the front/lobby entrance (8m x 4.5m x 1.1m).

restaurant name: Pacific Emporium at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore
text:
Pacific Emporium at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore We visit on Saturday for Afternoon tea. The pod seating is full so we already accepted another random seating. All other staffs are fine and professional enough  however  Molly  a lady staff with eyeglasses gave us an unexpected shock when she happens to serve us  her attitude and words used were rude and not appropriate  which made us feel totally unwelcome and uncomfortable  we simply asked for some hot water to refill our chamomile tea and she told us “No  order your second drink”. We were in shock but still politely responded “We want little more time til second drink  could we just have some hot water” and she still insisted “No you must order your second drink now”  while we’re not even half way into our afternoon tea time. We didn’t expect to receive such unprofessional service at a higher end hotel afternoon tea. So if anyone is looking for smooth experience  I don’t recommend to try this. If anyone expects to pay $80ish per pax and has a chance of being served rudely  you may try. Pacific Emporium at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore Overall  the food quality and the service staff were good and met expectations. The team was attentive and professional  which I appreciated.

However  the live music from the Edge was far too loud for a buffet lunch setting. It became disruptive and genuinely annoying to the point that I couldn’t enjoy my meal and left immediately after finishing. Live music simply doesn’t suit a relaxed atmosphere  especially when guests are there to dine and converse.

On Sunday around 2pm  the “Happy Birthday” song was also played repeatedly  multiple times in a row  and felt excessive. The constant repetition added to the noise level and made the experience feel chaotic rather than celebratory.

It’s a shame  as the food and service were good  but the noise level significantly affected the overall dining experience. Pacific Emporium at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore I had a good dinner  comfortable setting in the hotel lobby.
The salmon was done well and lightly seasoned  perfect for myself.
Hokkien noodles have great umami taste. Pacific Emporium at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore Had high tea with my wife and mother in law  food was alright. However  when we first sat down  was served by a lady and then another Indian guy. The table we sat on still had another person's bill and we couldn't scan the QR code on the table to order ala carte on top of the afternoon tea set. The Indian guy took the QR code away and forgot to come back. Despite pressing the service button  no one came. I decided to wave to them  and a guy named Li Hong came to assist us. We explained to him the situation  he instantly came back with a new QR code. Following that  he continued to keep a lookout for our requests constantly  and his joyful attitude and attentive service made our afternoon tea great. The food was okay  but Li Hong certainly made the experience a 5 star worthy one. GHA titanium members have 25% off  so honestly quite a good deal. Pacific Emporium at Pan Pacific Hotel Singapore Pacific Emporium is our go-to brunch spot. The food is consistently good  the service is wonderful  and the atmosphere is quiet  comfortable  and relaxing. It’s a lovely place to enjoy a peaceful brunch without the crowds. Always a great experience.

It was extremely hot  and the hot wind blew against our backs and the faces of the person I was dining with. We asked the staff to close the door two or three times. Sometimes it was left almost halfway open  and sometimes we had to reach out to close it ourselves  but the staff saw us. I understand if they were carrying things to serve the outside area  but they didn't close the door when walking back empty-handed. Pasta AMA Siam Center Un choix dingues de nourriture Pasta AMA Siam Center If you're looking for a satisfying pasta fix in the heart of Siam  Pasta Ama at Siam Center is a must-visit. Located in the open-air food zone on the 2nd floor  the restaurant offers a relaxed  communal vibe right in the middle of the action.

​Service & Atmosphere
​Even though it's located in an open mall layout rather than a private room  the seating is surprisingly comfortable. What truly stands out here is the service. The staff is incredibly attentive and caring  ensuring a smooth dining experience from start to finish. Plus  the food comes out impressively fast!

​The Food
​The portions here are very generous - you definitely get your money's worth. Every dish we ordered was a hit  but there were two absolute stars:

​Spaghetti Tomato Sauce with Champignon Mushrooms: This was the highlight of the meal. The flavors were perfectly balanced (กลมกล่อม). A quick heads-up: this dish is a little bit spicy  which adds a nice depth to the sauce!

​Superboost Smoothie: This drink is top-tier. You can tell they don't skimp on the ingredients; it tastes rich  fresh  and fully "boosted" as the name suggests.

​With great flavors  big portions  and excellent service  I’ll definitely be coming back for more. Pasta AMA Siam Center The prices are comparable to upscale Italian restaurants. I was very thirsty and had a dry throat after eating  unlike at a typical Italian restaurant. I was a little disappointed because the reviews were 4.9. The prices on the menu don't include 7% VAT and 5% service charge. The bill for two people was 1400 baht. Pasta AMA Siam Center The food is delicious! I even got a free birthday cake. Every time I come  I'm always impressed. Pasta AMA Siam Center The food was delicious  and the service was excellent.
