PengYou Cuisine & Specialty Bar Ekkamai  PengYou Cuisine & Specialty Bar Ekkamai This dim sum buffet eliminates the need to search for char siu (roasted pork) in Hong Kong anymore!! It's the buffet with the best char siu ever – the pork is tender  fragrant  and incredibly delicious. The shrimp wonton soup is incredibly fragrant and smooth. The pumpkin buns have soft dough and a smooth  creamy filling. The turnip cake is delicious and fragrant. Anyone who hasn't tried it yet must! PengYou Cuisine & Specialty Bar Ekkamai So good Chinese food PengYou Cuisine & Specialty Bar Ekkamai I had the opportunity to dine at PengYou Cuisine & Specialty Bar  and I was impressed from the moment I stepped in until the end of my meal. If you're looking for a Chinese restaurant that elevates the modern experience to a new level  I highly recommend this place.

Food Taste and Dishes Ordered:

• BBQ Platter: This dish was amazing! The char siu pork  crispy pork belly  and duck were all excellent. The meat was tender and fragrant with a delicious grilled aroma. The crispy pork belly was perfectly cooked – the skin was incredibly crunchy  yet the meat remained juicy. It paired perfectly with the variety of sauces available.

• Crispy Shrimp Rice Noodle Rolls with Shrimp Roe: The soft rice noodle wrapper contrasted beautifully with the crispy filling and the crunchy shrimp roe on top. It was a dish with a fun array of textures in every bite.

• Shrimp Toast: Generous portions of shrimp  the bread wasn't oily  and it was generously sprinkled with sesame seeds for a fragrant aroma. It was delicious served hot.

• Other Special Dishes: The plating was exquisite  incorporating truffles and premium ingredients  making the Chinese dishes more interesting and adding complex flavor dimensions.

Atmosphere and Service:

The restaurant's ambiance was luxurious yet maintained a warm and inviting feel. This place is perfect for both family meals and business meetings. The service deserves a full 5 stars; the staff are attentive  constantly refilling water and offering friendly menu recommendations  which greatly enhances the dining experience. PengYou Cuisine & Specialty Bar Ekkamai The food was delicious  every dish was of high standard. The foie gras was excellent  surprisingly paired with the dumplings. The roast duck was also delicious 🥰 My favorite was the crispy shrimp rice noodle rolls.

Phahon View Watcharapol Pahol View Restaurant is a Thai restaurant with a unique and distinctive atmosphere  situated next to a lake. The views from the tables are expansive  offering stunning views of the water and sky  perfect for dinner or evenings when you can fully appreciate the sunset. It's a popular spot for taking photos and checking in 📸. Live music is played almost every night  creating a relaxed ambiance for your meal.

The menu offers a diverse selection of Thai  Chinese  Northeastern Thai  and seasonal local dishes  ensuring you'll never get bored. From seafood dishes and curries to spicy salads and various Thai-style spicy dishes  there's something for everyone. The restaurant is also famous for some dishes like its "crab curry with abundant meat" or its seafood fried rice  packed with shrimp  crab  and other ingredients – many customers say it's great value for the price.

The overall price range is moderate to slightly high  but the quality and quantity match the price. Convenient parking is available  a plus for those driving their own vehicles. Furthermore  the restaurant is open until late  from afternoon until 2 AM  making it a great option for both meals and after-work gatherings.

Overall  the service is praised for being attentive  polite  and helpful. However  overall review scores vary slightly depending on the taste of the food or certain dishes that customers described as "just okay " not particularly outstanding. Therefore  anyone who enjoys a good atmosphere  beautiful views  and a diverse selection of delicious Thai food will definitely get both a great dining experience and the fun of listening to music here. 😊

In summary  Pahol View is suitable for family dinners  gatherings with friends  or special occasions  offering beautiful views  live music  and photo opportunities. It's definitely one of the restaurants you should try at least once. 🍽️🌅🎶 Phahon View Watcharapol Fried chicken and crab fried rice are delicious. The restaurant has a great view and lots of food.
The atmosphere is nice  and the food is great too. Phahon View Watcharapol Once is enough. Phahon View Watcharapol  Phahon View Watcharapol

restaurant name: Pool Bar by Maitria Mode Sukhumvit 15
text:
Pool Bar by Maitria Mode Sukhumvit 15 Had an amazing experience - special thanks to Khun Pleng for her excellent service. The portions are quite big.  Loved the olio spaghetti and Khao Soi - however  the phat thai wasn’t so good. Pool Bar by Maitria Mode Sukhumvit 15 genuinely impressed Pool Bar by Maitria Mode Sukhumvit 15 I stopped by this rooftop spot mid-day to take advantage of a promotional deal  and overall  it was a solid experience with a few standouts.

I ordered the beef burger — an impressive  beefy  thick-cut burger. If you’re into juicy  hearty burgers that feel like a proper meal  this one’s for you. On the other hand  if you’re more into thin  crispy-edged smash burgers  you might want to skip this and look elsewhere on the menu.

I added cheddar cheese for an extra 89 baht  but honestly  it wasn’t worth it. The burger was so thick and flavorful that the cheese got completely lost — I couldn’t really taste it or feel its presence in the texture. It didn’t add much to the overall experience.

The wedge-cut fries were a pleasant surprise — nicely seasoned and perfectly crisp on the outside  soft on the inside.

The service was excellent. The staff went above and beyond — friendly  attentive  and clearly cared about the customer experience.

The only downside was the atmosphere during the time I visited. The rooftop setting definitely has potential  but on a cloudy  hot afternoon  it wasn’t at its best. I imagine the ambiance would be much better after sunset when the lights come on and the temperature drops.

All in all  worth a visit — especially if you catch a good deal or come in the evening to enjoy the full rooftop vibe Pool Bar by Maitria Mode Sukhumvit 15 BAD service period Pool Bar by Maitria Mode Sukhumvit 15 The go to spot every Sunday for a great Salsa/Bachata night!

There was no proper explanation or apology. Eventually  we decided to check out and leave without getting the paella because we were tired of waiting. Only then were we told the food was ready to be served.

The poor communication and repeated inaccurate updates completely ruined the experience. Delays happen  but misleading customers and only reacting when they decide to leave is unacceptable. restaurant name: RIB ROOM & BAR Steakhouse The Landmark Bangkok
text: RIB ROOM & BAR Steakhouse The Landmark Bangkok  RIB ROOM & BAR Steakhouse The Landmark Bangkok Great service and food. A tad pricey but absolutely worth it imo. RIB ROOM & BAR Steakhouse The Landmark Bangkok Beautiful restaurant  very good staff  friendly and helpful. Food doesn't match the price. Sorry but it is way over priced. I had a discount card  even with that spent 29 000 baht about US$900 for three people  and my friend purchase a wine and added 10 000 baht. That's 39 000 baht for three people. Yes we had appetizers  salad  wine and steak. I travel the world and even go to the fancy Oriental Hotel  yet there  I hosted 10 people for a price of 26 000 baht. Rib room is crazy expensive. There are many steak restaurants in Bangkok that are reasonable. I didn't believe the food matched the price point. That is my honesty. If you see my overall reviews you will see I give 5 stars more often than not. I did like the view and the beautiful restaurant. Go for drinks and appetizers  save your money. RIB ROOM & BAR Steakhouse The Landmark Bangkok The view from there is simply incredible. Combined with the fantastic food  it's a truly unique experience. We sat right by the window at sunset and were absolutely blown away. The service was attentive and quick  and the wine selection was also very good; they even had a German Riesling. The steak was incredibly tender  and the side dishes were delicious. There's also a small smoking balcony  so you don't have to go downstairs to smoke. All in all  a very successful evening! RIB ROOM & BAR Steakhouse The Landmark Bangkok

text:

Raffles Courtyard at Raffles Hotel Singapore We celebrated our little one’s 2nd birthday at Raffles Courtyard  and it was absolutely perfect for the occasion!
The outdoor setting made all the difference for our celebration. The courtyard offers a wonderful ambiance that’s both elegant and relaxed  which can be hard to find when dining with toddlers. What really stood out was the generous amount of space - our birthday boy had plenty of room to explore safely while we enjoyed our meal. It’s such a relief to find a restaurant where you don’t feel like you need to keep your child confined to their seat the entire time.
The atmosphere was lovely  with beautiful surroundings that made our family photos turn out beautifully. The staff was accommodating and understanding with our energetic two-year-old  which we truly appreciated. The food was delicious  and the outdoor environment meant we could relax without worrying too much about typical toddler noise levels.
If you’re looking for a family-friendly spot that doesn’t compromise on quality or atmosphere  especially for celebrating special milestones with young children  Raffles Courtyard is an excellent choice. The combination of great food  beautiful setting  and child-friendly space made our baby’s birthday dinner memorable for all the right reasons.
Highly recommended for families with little ones! Raffles Courtyard at Raffles Hotel Singapore Raspberry Fizz   perfect for a hot day Raffles Courtyard at Raffles Hotel Singapore Really wonderful service by the team at Raffles Courtyard. We made a reservation for 5pm. It was raining cats and dogs and and the team did everything to make us comfortable. They had umbrellas ready  took our baby stroller to keep in a room  wiped down and gave us a clean baby chair for our 2 year old. Just excellent excellent service. Special mention to Mr Suren for making all the difference to our experience. He was so attentive. Brought us all the way to a toilet with a changing room for our baby and made sure we found our way back to our seats. He and the team were so attentive the entire time we were there. We will be back another time for proper lunch or dinner on a sunny day for sure.

Just wanted to drop a note about our recent stay at SO/ Bangkok  and particularly our experiences at the Red Oven.

Chef Ms. Da had very thoughtfully arranged a festive cupcake and cookie decorating session for all the children. It was an absolutely wonderful idea. She set everything up beautifully  and it created the most cheerful  festive atmosphere for the little ones. My son was utterly delighted he’s still talking about his ‘masterpiece’. It’s these kind of considerate  shared activities that make a family holiday feel so special.

A special mention must go to Ms. Esther from the hotel team  who we understand was instrumental in organising it all. It was clearly done with such care. We also saw several of the restaurant staff  including Praew  Ice  and Nook  milling about and offering smiles and encouragement to the budding young bakers  which added to the lovely  communal feel.

It was a small thing  perhaps  but it made a world of difference to our stay. A genuine thank you to everyone involved for creating such a happy memory for the children. Red Oven SO/ Bangkok The Sunday Brunch buffet was generally of standard to good quality. All the dishes were easy to eat and delicious.

My favorite part was the oysters – incredibly fresh  sweet  juicy  and unlimited – excellent value. The Alaskan crab was fresh and had firm  tasty meat  although there were times when the refills weren't as consistent as expected. Other dishes were generally well-prepared; none of them disappointed.

The only downside was the lobster  which was limited to one per person  very small  and had way too much sauce  making it look bigger – a minor drawback.

However  overall  the rest of the Sunday Brunch was delicious  great value for money  and perfect for those who love fresh seafood.

I would definitely come back again; I still love it! 🙏🏻☺️

Rern I never thought Southern Thai cuisine could be explored in such depth before.
Until I ate at The Lost Taste at RERN.

It's not Southern Thai food that's been toned down 
but rather flavors that feel familiar.
Sour  spicy  salty—bold and uncompromising.
Like flavors lost over time  now brought back.

The meal started with a fresh seafood salad; the first bite was invigorating.
Then it unfolded into a medley of dishes  each complementing the others.
Intense  deep  warm  and full of the chef's dedication in every bite.

What I loved most is that they don't try to be "modern "
but remain true to the original flavors.
The flavors can be strong  bitter  and spicy  just like authentic Southern Thai cuisine.

If you love Southern Thai food and want to understand it better 
I highly recommend trying it yourself. You'll understand what "flavor you've missed" means without further explanation. 🤍 Rern Ruen Restaurant  a Southern Thai restaurant owned by the same person as Khamin Restaurant  is located on Kaset-Nawamin Road and can be accessed via Soi Phahonyothin 34. Rern This is my third visit to The Lost Taste  a new course  and I'm very impressed with how much it has improved. The flavors are bold  especially the coconut milk-based dishes and curries. If you're looking for fine dining Southern Thai cuisine  I highly recommend this place. It's also great for larger groups  with a lovely atmosphere and authentically delicious food. Overall  I'm very impressed with this restaurant. Rern Rern:
“Every bite is a day-long journey to Southern Thailand  meticulously crafted by a chef who tells a story with every detail 🥥🌿”

It was a truly impressive lunch  a 14-course fine-dining Southern Thai meal from Rern.
Every bite was designed to be “intense and complete” from the very first mouthful.

It started with crispy sole fish miang  which awakened the taste buds – a perfect balance of saltiness  richness  sourness  sweetness  and spiciness that was truly exciting.
Next was my favorite dish  salted fried Tapi River prawns – perfectly cooked  fresh and bouncy  served with pureed Sang Yod rice.
And the pickled jicama perfectly balanced the flavors. This dish was incredibly enjoyable.
Even the smaller bites  like the sweet mango with shrimp  were delightful. Still full of "intense" flavors.

A Southern Thai dining experience that's rich  fun  and warm  like visiting a friend's house 🧡

“A southern Thai fine dining journey — 14 dishes  each packed with flavor from the very first bite. 🌶️✨ #rernbangkok

restaurant name: River Barge at Chatrium Hotel Riverside Bangkok
text:
River Barge at Chatrium Hotel Riverside Bangkok  River Barge at Chatrium Hotel Riverside Bangkok Great overall! River Barge at Chatrium Hotel Riverside Bangkok The River Barge buffet dinner at Chatrium Residence Riverside Bangkok starts at 6:00 PM. We dined with 3 people  and the total price was 3 250 THB (after discount). The price and food quality were excellent. We'll definitely be back!

We recommend calling to reserve a table beforehand. There are two seating areas:
1. An air-conditioned area near the food  nice and cool.
2. An outdoor area by the Chao Phraya River  not air-conditioned  near the live music stage.

The atmosphere is great. The restaurant is right by the Chao Phraya River  with boats passing by  their lights illuminating the darkness at dusk.

There's a live music stage playing slow  beautiful country music.

The service was good. We mentioned that the cockles were cold after being blanched and asked if they could be re-blanched  which they did. The staff were friendly and greeted us well  frequently clearing plates without being asked. They even refilled our water glasses when they noticed our glasses were running low.

>
>>Seafood on ice includes blanched river prawns and large white prawns...>> >>15-16 pieces per kilo  blanched cockles.
>>Fresh grilled seafood such as mantis shrimp  fresh river prawns  beef (some hotels use "pre-boiled river prawns" which are then grilled again).
>>Fresh seafood such as fresh Normandy oysters from France  which are thin and small  with a salty taste  different from Surat Thani oysters which are thicker  larger  and chewier  and different from Korean oysters.
>>Japanese food such as maki roll  sushi  salmon sashimi  tuna sashimi  sweet egg omelet  tako (octopus)... tempura shrimp  smoked salmon  smoked tuna.
>>Thai food such as Tom Yum  pork crackling  Som Tum  pork and chicken satay  pomelo salad  Miang Kham  Pad Thai  beef stew noodles  and various chafing dishes.
>>Other items: cheese  bacon  salad bar.
>>Desserts  cakes  ice cream  Bingsu  fondue  Tokyo pancakes  seasonal fresh fruit.

>
1. Fresh Normandy Oysters from France
2. Fresh Grilled River Prawns: The chef grilled the prawns perfectly; the heads still had plenty of creamy roe  and the meat was fresh and not mushy. This isn't like some other hotel buffets that use "pre-boiled river prawns" for grilling.
3. Grilled Mantis Shrimp: Fresh and the meat wasn't mushy.
4. Large White Prawns  Boiled on Ice: Approximately 15-16 prawns per kilo.
5. Boiled Cockles: The shells were just right  not dry or chewy  fresh and sweet.
6. Salmon Sashimi: Fresh  served with seafood dipping sauce  wasabi soy sauce  or as a salad in bite-sized portions. Alternatively  it can be used in a salmon salad with black and white sesame seeds for added calcium.
7. Gelato Ice Cream: Decorate your own toppings. Delicious; each person ate two scoops.

***Areas for Improvement and Development***
In hotel buffets  mango sticky rice is served in individual portions and kept in an air-conditioned environment; this is common in all hotels  as the sticky rice tends to be hard. Therefore  I would suggest creating a "live-making station" or "preparing a model of mango sticky rice and having staff specify how many pieces you want." In the kitchen  the chef could reheat the sticky rice in the microwave before having staff serve it  or the chef could make each piece fresh to order and have staff deliver it to customers' tables. Alternatively  you could offer only two service rounds  with customers placing orders simultaneously  such as 7:00 PM and 8:00 PM. "Fresh...new...and tastier too!" River Barge at Chatrium Hotel Riverside Bangkok I have eaten here for breakfast  lunch  and dinner. All I need to say is 10/10!!! The food is amazing  great customer service  and good vibes. It’s right in the river with fantastic views!

Ask for Mr. PORNSAK he remembers the drinks you get and always has a smile on his face! River Barge at Chatrium Hotel Riverside Bangkok

I chose the Sens Of Nature program  which included foot  neck  shoulder  and back massages – incredibly relaxing! Plus  there are 15 onsen baths to enjoy.

The Xtreme ATV riding activity was much more fun than I expected; it wasn't as difficult as I thought. It's even more fun in a group!

Both breakfast and dinner were delicious – flavorful and never bland. I was very impressed! The rooms were clean  and there were so many activities to do  all of which were fun! We enjoyed massages on the raft  soaked in the onsen  ATV riding  and a relaxing rafting trip all day long. We arrived at the hotel early  at 9:00 AM  after a 12-hour flight  and our room wasn't supposed to be available until 3:00 PM. Since we were tired and wanted some peace and quiet  we asked if we could check in earlier. Surprisingly  after paying an extra 500 baht  the room was ready immediately.

We were also told that our stay only included one breakfast for two nights. So  on the second day  we had to get breakfast ourselves. However  we had definitely booked two nights WITH breakfast.

Unfortunately  the woman wasn't wearing a name tag.

In any case  this was very annoying and cost us money. That's not right!

text:
Ruen Petch Suki Nonthaburi Bypass Road Classic Thai suki vibes from the moment you sit down 🍲🔥 broth bubbling like it has life advice to give  vegetables stacked like you’re pretending to be healthy 🥬😌 while secretly planning extra meat on the next round 🥩

Flavors are old-school and confident 😏 clear broth but not boring  dipping sauce doing all the heavy lifting 🧄🌶️ every bite feels familiar in a “this place has fed families for decades” kind of way  no gimmicks  no fusion  just straight-up comfort food energy 💯

Not fancy ❌ not trying to impress influencers ❌ but solid  reliable  and perfect for long meals with people who talk more than they eat 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦🍽️

restaurant name: Ryota Shabu Buffet Central Airport (Chiang Mai)
text:
Ryota Shabu Buffet Central Airport (Chiang Mai) Rating: 1/10 – Extremely Unhygienic and Disgusting
This was honestly one of the worst and most unhygienic dining experiences I’ve ever had.
The moment you enter  the place smells bad — like dirty feet. That smell alone should have been a warning sign. The restaurant is small  cramped  and poorly maintained  and the floor looked filthy  sticky  and worn out  as if it’s rarely cleaned properly.
The conveyor belt where the food moves was absolutely disgusting. It looked dirty  greasy  and badly maintained  like it had not been properly washed in a very long time. Watching food move on that belt already made me uncomfortable about eating anything.
The manager was outside the dining area  standing on his phone  scratching his belly  and completely ignoring what was happening inside. There was no visible supervision  no care for cleanliness  and no respect for customers.
Serious hygiene issues (very important)
When we asked for more soup  they did not bring it.
Instead  they told us to pick the soup ourselves from old containers stored inside a wooden cabinet.
Those soup containers looked extremely old  stained  and dirty  honestly like they had never been properly cleaned. This is a major hygiene and food safety concern. A restaurant should never let customers serve themselves from containers that look unhygienic and poorly maintained.
Food quality (very poor)
Overall food quality: 3/10
The meat was so thin it was like onion skin  almost transparent
I highly doubt the meat was ribeye  even though it’s advertised on the 319 THB menu
The fried fish sitting in the warmer tasted strongly of old  reused oil  making it almost inedible
On top of that  drinks are not included. You need to pay extra (around 40 THB) for refills. It’s not a lot of money  but considering the terrible hygiene and food quality  it feels unreasonable.
Right now it’s 23:50  and I honestly feel nauseous after eating here.
Final thoughts
There are many much better places in Chiang Mai — cleaner  better service  better food  and cheaper prices.
There are Yakiniku & Sushi buffets around 299 THB  all-inclusive  clean  with excellent service and food quality.
❌ Final verdict
Do NOT go to this place.
Not even if the food were free.
Extremely unhygienic  unpleasant  and not worth the risk. Ryota Shabu Buffet Central Airport (Chiang Mai) The environment was so-so  and the food was just so-so. S'More Beach Restaurant and Bar (Pattaya)  S'More Beach Restaurant and Bar (Pattaya) Wonderful beach S'More Beach Restaurant and Bar (Pattaya) good atmosphere S'More Beach Restaurant and Bar (Pattaya) S'more Beach Cafe is a Korean-style beachfront cafe located in Pattaya. It's popular for its unique glass window photo spot offering panoramic ocean views.

Facilities and Highlights:
Atmosphere: The cafe offers a tranquil and private atmosphere with a private beach perfect for strolling along the shore. The minimalist white-toned decor evokes the feeling of Busan  South Korea.
Photo Spots: There are numerous photo opportunities  including the popular spot by the large glass window overlooking the sea. The cafe has a queue management system for this photo spot  limiting time to approximately 10-15 minutes per group to ensure everyone has a chance to take a picture.
Menu: They offer a variety of coffee drinks (such as Americano)  non-coffee drinks (such as Thai tea  iced milk)  as well as bakery items and cakes. The beachfront bar and restaurant also serve Thai and international cuisine.
Pets: This is a pet-friendly cafe  allowing dogs in strollers or carriers to sit in the outdoor area. S'More Beach Restaurant and Bar (Pattaya) Beautiful place but you can NOT see the sunset from here.

restaurant name: Saffron Contemporary Thai Signature Rooftop Banyan Tree Bangkok
text:
Saffron Contemporary Thai Signature Rooftop Banyan Tree Bangkok The Thai food was delicious  I was very impressed. The atmosphere was great and the staff provided excellent service. Saffron Contemporary Thai Signature Rooftop Banyan Tree Bangkok Saffron delivers an exceptional Thai dining experience that strikes a rare balance between authenticity and accessibility. The flavors are unmistakably Thai—aromatic  layered  and thoughtfully composed—yet calibrated so international guests can enjoy them comfortably. For Thai diners  spice levels can be adjusted on request  allowing the cuisine to retain its true intensity without dilution.

My dining experience is structured as a course-based menu  guiding guests smoothly from appetizer  soup  salad  main course  to dessert. This approach elevates the meal beyond casual dining  creating a cohesive and refined culinary journey.

One particularly impressive detail is the presentation of steamed rice  which is treated with the same respect as the main dishes. Guests can choose from four carefully selected rice varieties:
•	Saffron rice
•	Rice berry
•	Brown rice
•	Jasmine rice

This thoughtful offering highlights the depth of Thai food culture  where rice is not merely a side dish  but a central element of the dining experience.

The atmosphere is elegant yet understated  featuring clean design lines and a serene ambiance complemented by sweeping panoramic views of Bangkok and the Chao Phraya River. It’s a setting that feels luxurious without being overstated—confident  calm  and well-balanced.

Service is delivered at a true five-star level. The staff are attentive  professional  and genuinely warm  with precise timing and an intuitive understanding of guest needs throughout the meal.

A must-not-miss highlight is the restaurant’s signature dessert  Saffron Khao Niew Mamuang (mango sticky rice). Beautifully presented and perfectly balanced in sweetness and texture  it offers a refined interpretation of a beloved Thai classic and provides a memorable conclusion to the dining experience.

Saffron preserves Thai culinary identity at 100% while presenting it in a globally refined  five-star format. An outstanding choice for international guests and discerning Thai diners seeking authentic Thai cuisine in an elevated setting. Saffron Contemporary Thai Signature Rooftop Banyan Tree Bangkok A fantastic experience @ Saffron Contemporary Cuisine located on the 52nd floor of the Bamyan Tree Hotel. Top Notch service and food with spectacular customer service. Thank you Mew Korn for being a wonderful host for our dining experience! Highly recommend. Hope to see you again Saffron Cruise by Banyan Tree Fabulous experience  food was amazing and loved the Hanuman. Our waiter was lovely  explaining each dish and telling us about Bangkok. Would recommend Saffron Cruise by Banyan Tree I booked a dinner cruise in Bangkok as a surprise gift for my mum’s 60th birthday. Given how special the occasion was  I carefully reviewed several options and chose this cruise based on its reviews and food selection. Unfortunately  the experience was very disappointing.
The food was average at best and did not justify the price. The entire evening felt rushed  with little opportunity to relax or enjoy the meal. The dining area was overcrowded  with too many guests packed into a small space  which took away from the overall experience.
The biggest issue was the service. Our server was rude and unprofessional. He did not explain the dishes  served the food carelessly by slamming plates onto the table  and showed no attention to proper service. We noticed that other tables received much more professional service  which made our experience even more frustrating.
At one point  the server attempted to clear our plates while we were still eating  and I had to remind him that we weren’t finished. I was close to asking the manager for a different server. Thankfully  the manager noticed the situation during the main course and stepped in  which slightly improved things.
The views were nice and worked well for a few photos  but they were not nearly enough to justify the price tag. Overall  this dinner cruise was not worth it—especially for such an important celebration. I would not recommend it and would definitely consider other options when visiting Bangkok again. Saffron Cruise by Banyan Tree This Chao Phraya River cruise program allows you to enjoy a meal with your family  complete with a nighttime view. This is a wonderful dinner course  where you can savor a course meal prepared by the Banyan Tree Hotel while enjoying the nighttime scenery from the comfort of your cruise ship. If you're looking for a relaxing trip to Bangkok with your loved ones  this is highly recommended. text:
Sai Bistro Restaurant at The Sinae (Phuket) We stayed at the hotel for 8 nights so we had a lot of chances to try out various dishes from the restaurant's menu.
First of all  the breakfast is well varied  offering both international (western or Indian) as well as local Thai varieties.
For lunch and dinner  the Thai (both Phuket and Siam variations) impressed us with the rich flavors!
We also tried out a Thai cooking class at the restaurant which was a very pleasant experience.
The atmosphere is relaxed  with soft music and warm lighting.
Staff were also very attentive  trying their best to always accommodate our needs. In particular Natcha  Tonic and Nam have taken good care of us. Sai Bistro Restaurant at The Sinae (Phuket) Excellent Indian food!! I highly recommend it!! Sai Bistro Restaurant at The Sinae (Phuket) Food and atmosphere were amazing! We stayed at the resort and were skeptical of dining here for dinner because of other reviews   -  but we are so glad we tried it!   All of our dishes were great and full of flavor.  We also had breakfast here a fee of the mornings  and it was fantastic with tons of options and variety Sai Bistro Restaurant at The Sinae (Phuket) Had food positioning after eating Indian veg food: dal tadka  palak paneer and nan bread. Disappointing for this type of establishment. Sai Bistro Restaurant at The Sinae (Phuket) The environment is truly beautiful  and the staff were very friendly and helpful — thank you for that! 🙏

However  we found the Indian dishes a little disappointing in terms of flavor. We ordered palak paneer  dal tadka  and naan  but unfortunately they all tasted quite bland and the naan didn’t really resemble traditional naan.

Just a gentle suggestion: it might be lovely to play some beautiful Thai music instead. Many guests travel from far away and would truly appreciate hearing traditional Thai melodies in such a wonderful setting  rather than English songs sung slightly out of tune.

Thank you again for the lovely atmosphere overall! 😊

Except for the seafood platter  which was a bit salty  everything was delicious. Coming from Chiang Mai  the prices were a bit steep  but since it was my birthday  I decided to flex my muscles. with a full-fledged Thai cultural show ✨

📍 Sala Lakorn
🏨 Anantara Riverside Bangkok Resort

Enjoy your meal by the river
while watching the show. The atmosphere is amazing 💓

The performance isn't just a passing act 
but a full-fledged portrayal of Thai culture 
including music  dance  and theatrical performances 
woven together into a cohesive story.
The stage  lighting  sound  and river view
create an incredibly immersive experience ✨

🕰️ Time
🍽️ Buffet: 6:00 PM – 10:00 PM (4 hours)
🎭 Show: 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM (2 hours)

The food selection is diverse 
including Thai and international dishes  desserts  and fruits.
Enjoy your meal and watch at your leisure.
The overall experience was excellent 🤍

❤️ If you come on a weekend 
you can even see the Vijit Chao Phraya show 
including fireworks and drone shows  adding to the impressive experience ✨ Sala Lakorn at Anantara Riverside Initially  seeing the price of 2 900 baht per person  I hesitated  wondering if it was too expensive for just a dinner buffet with a performance. However  Anantara Riverside's convenient location  not far from Asiatique The Grand  and the shuttle boat to Sathorn Pier made it a perfect fit for my itinerary  so I decided to give it a try. The overall experience was even better than I expected  definitely worth it.

The buffet had a wide variety of dishes  including seafood  freshly cut steak  and BBQ; there were also Chinese  Indian  and Thai cuisines  all of good quality  with the Indian dishes being particularly authentic. The dessert section was also excellent  with a variety of unique Thai desserts and snacks; I was delighted just by enjoying the desserts.

The performance  which started at 7 pm  far exceeded my expectations. I initially thought it would be a simple performance accompanying the meal  but it turned out to be a full-hour professional stage show showcasing the culture of different regions of Thailand. The performers were very professional and performed up close in the audience area  creating a very immersive experience and allowing me to clearly see every dance move. They even invited audience members to come on stage and dance together  creating a very joyful atmosphere. Afterwards  they even took photos with everyone at each table  which was a really thoughtful touch.

Also  a heads-up: drinks aren't included in the buffet. We ordered a separate bottle of wine to go with dinner  and we thought it was quite good.

Overall  I highly recommend Sala Lakorn. Although it's on the pricier side  the food  atmosphere  and performance made it well worth it  and it was the most memorable meal of my Bangkok trip.

Savio at Chatrium Grand Bangkok I give the food two stars. First star for the freshly shucked oysters—extremely fresh  the saltiness of the sea combined with the minerality of the oysters and a touch of citric acid was perfect.

Second star for the North Indian cuisine: the mutton biryani had a perfect spice blend. The yogurt-roasted chicken was tender and juicy.

The Japanese sashimi lacked freshness; the fish was powdery. The halibut fin nigiri sushi rice was too sticky and lacked shine.

The Spanish seafood paella was cooked like Italian risotto; the rice core was undercooked—a half-baked chef.

The Turkish dessert only had the shape  not the flavor.

The Italian bacon and cheese pasta was terrible.

The roast duck skin was so dry it could be used as leather.

The shrimp dumplings…forget about them!

I have to say  apart from the Indian chef  everyone else was a mediocre cook!

The stir-fried water spinach was delicious! I must mention it.

The service was excellent; they were always attentive to our needs.

The restaurant was infested with mosquitoes  tons of mosquitoes  an overwhelming number!

It's hard to believe this is a five-star hotel restaurant!

Not worth the money; it looks fancy but has no real flavor. When you first walk in  however  the restaurant does not feel very organized. The drink station and the area where guests can mix their own sauces are located very close to the kitchen. The kitchen itself is quite open  which can be interesting  but unfortunately everything looked a bit messy and not as clean or hygienic as you would hope. In the dining area  there were also quite a lot of mosquitoes and some flies  which was annoying and slightly distracting while eating.

That said  the food was the highlight of the evening. We tried a wide variety of dishes  including different types of sushi such as wagyu sushi and salmon sushi. Both were very fresh and tasted excellent. We also ordered a large lobster sushi and a lobster with cheese. Personally  I did not enjoy the lobster dishes very much  but that is mainly because I am not a big fan of lobster in general. Objectively  the dishes were okay and well prepared. The sushi overall was very good and definitely the strongest part of the menu.

We also had shabu  which was decent. The meat quality was fine  and all the ingredients were acceptable and met expectations. Considering the food quality  the price is very fair. We paid around 400 Baht per person  or about 1 050 Baht in total for two people  which is reasonable for what you get.

If the restaurant improved the front area  made it more visually appealing  and did something about the mosquitoes  this place could easily deserve five stars. For now  I would give it 4 out of 5 stars. Shabu Henmee Watcharapol Delicious food  fresh ingredients  good price Shabu Henmee Watcharapol It's so delicious  I have to recommend it! Shabu Square At For You Park Bangna  Shabu Square At For You Park Bangna - Wagyu Angus Chuck Roll was delicious.
- Some salmon sashimi dishes were good  others weren't very impressive.
- Wagyu sushi was delicious.
- For the fried items  we liked the sweet potato with cheese filling; the kids loved the cheese fries.
- The beef in the rice bowl was very dry.
- For dessert  the grape jelly and vanilla ice cream were delicious.
- Häagen-Dazs ice cream had one flavor per day; today it was strawberry cheesecake.
- Booking through Hungry Hub gave a net price  with no added VAT or service charge.
- The prices at the restaurant showed a promotion for senior citizens with a 30-50% discount depending on age.
- Food was served quickly  although some dishes were slow.
- The staff provided good service  constantly clearing plates  smiling  and speaking politely.
- When paying  the restaurant asked for license plate numbers for 4 hours of free parking.
- Food was ordered via QR code  and the staff refilled drinks (bottled water costs an extra 25 baht).
- Here are some screenshots of the menu.😆 Shabu Square At For You Park Bangna I came here far from home to try this. Someone recommended it to me and I wasn't disappointed. The soup was delicious  the sauce was okay  but the meat was incredibly good.
I also ordered sushi. I tried the eel  which was just as good as the famous sushi restaurants. Most importantly  there's a huge menu. The minced beef with basil rice was amazing. I can't even describe it. The desserts were delicious. They also had Häagen-Dazs ice cream. And most importantly  people over 65 get a 30% discount. Shabu Square At For You Park Bangna I'm giving it 5 stars! Everything was amazing. The food was fresh  there were many soup options to choose from  and the appetizers were plentiful and delicious. The Karala Wagyu beef was of excellent quality – tender  juicy  and incredibly flavorful.
The Angus Chuck Roll  US Bacon  and Beef Brisket were also incredibly delicious  melting in your mouth 🥩
There was a wide variety of soups to choose from; this time I chose 4.

The price was definitely worth it considering the quality. I will definitely be back again! 🍲🥩

ShoSha I was searching for the perfect place to eat for my birthday celebration when I came across Shosha on TikTok. There were so many good reviews and honestly  that made me even more excited to give it a try. The videos made the food look so delicious and unique  so I knew I had to check it out for myself.

The restaurant is located opposite Mustafa Centre and sits on level 2. Just a small reminder  if you’re coming with a wheelchair or stroller  you’ll need to climb the stairs  so it’s good to be prepared. The location itself is easy to find once you’re in the area  and the vibe already felt welcoming when we arrived.

We were warmly served by Deen  who was friendly and attentive throughout our visit  which made the dining experience even more pleasant. Little details like good service really add to the overall atmosphere  especially during a special celebration.

For our meal  we ordered the peri-peri chicken sizzler  the signature Shosha chicken sizzler and the pink pasta chicken. For the pasta  you can choose between penne or spaghetti  which I think is a nice option depending on your preference. The pink pasta chicken was m good  creamy and comforting but honestly  the two sizzlers completely stole the show. The flavours were bold  rich and something different from the usual pasta taste you normally expect.

My absolute favourite was the signature Shosha chicken sizzler. The chicken was juicy  the sauce was full of flavour  and every bite felt satisfying. It’s one of those dishes that makes you immediately want to come back again. Their food felt unique and memorable  which made my birthday meal extra special.

For drinks  the Dasani water displayed on the menu is priced at $2.50  excluding GST and service charge  so just keep that in mind when ordering.

I really loved my experience here and will definitely return to try more items on the menu. If you’re visiting during Ramadan  they also have a buffet option  which sounds worth trying. text:

The hotel hired Liu Peng  a master chef from Chongqing who has worked in Shanghai for over 10 years.

The restaurant is located on the first floor of Hotel Clover. After more than ten days in Thailand  we finally had the best food we could eat  and we had a wonderful chat with Chef Liu! He's super nice!!!

If you didn't like the food in Phuket  you absolutely must try Chef Liu's cooking here!

Potxxxxx ShouSushi  ShouSushi  ShouSushi I stopped by this restaurant simply because it was close to my hotel — and I’m so glad I did! I’d heard that Bangkok has some really good Japanese places  and this one absolutely lived up to the reputation.

One of the highlights for me was a special roll with wagyu beef — I tried it for the first time and it was incredible. The fish was fresh  rich in flavor  and every dish I ordered turned out delicious. The prices are reasonable too  which makes the whole experience even better.

The atmosphere is calm  clean  and pleasant — a great place to relax and enjoy good food. The only small thing that could be improved is the service: after bringing the food  the staff didn’t check on the table again  so I had to look for someone to ask for the bill.

Overall  I really enjoyed my visit and would happily come back next time

restaurant name: Shui Xin Chinese Restaurant Novotel Bangkok Bangna
text:
Shui Xin Chinese Restaurant Novotel Bangkok Bangna The fishmongers provided very good service. Shui Xin Chinese Restaurant Novotel Bangkok Bangna Food was okay to eat.
Order fried rice and it took 30min to arrive when all the food was already eaten. Shui Xin Chinese Restaurant Novotel Bangkok Bangna Chui Xin Novotel Bangna today has a dim sum buffet promotion: 4 for 3. They serve a variety of steamed dim sum dishes  high quality and delicious  such as har gow  drunken chicken  shrimp dumplings  and rad na. Refills are quick  and the atmosphere is modern and luxurious. We used a private room. The service was good  but many dishes aren't included in the buffet; you have to order a la carte  such as crispy shrimp noodle rolls  xiao long bao  and traditional Chinese-style Peking duck. It's truly delicious  with crispy skin and fragrant  fatty meat. (Warning: You should book in advance  as Peking duck is discounted at around 500 baht per duck.) Shui Xin Chinese Restaurant Novotel Bangkok Bangna Outstanding Chinese Restaurant at Samut Prakan. Located right at Novotel Hotel with Dim Sum Buffet section twice daily during Lunch time and Dinner time. Delivery Of course  a la carte available all the time. Dim Sum  is absolutely Guangdong authentic and made to order. Price is reasonable as well. Shui Xin Chinese Restaurant Novotel Bangkok Bangna Today  we had a la carte Chinese food. We arrived a bit late for lunch  and there weren't many customers in the room. However  the staff were welcoming and gave good recommendations. We ordered six dishes: Xiao Long Bao  shrimp dumplings  shrimp dumplings  fish maw with crab meat  fried turnip cakes (forgot to take a photo)  and fish with soy sauce (a normal size serves 2-3 people).
The food was delicious. I liked how the Xiao Long Bao was served with a spoon  making it easy to eat the soup. It didn't ruin the flavor if the soup leaked out. However  the dough was a bit thick. The fish maw soup was average for a Chinese restaurant. Everything else was okay  and delicious. The restaurant atmosphere was nice  comfortable  and not cramped. There's also a private room available.

Accor Plus members can enjoy the a la carte menu for two people  with a 50% discount. We paid 712 baht  and were completely satisfied.

P.S. The staff informed us that the lunch buffet is currently on promotion  with a 10% discount for Accor Plus members. For dinner  it's 1 299 baht  and Accor Plus members can enjoy the discount as per their normal privileges.

Siam India Asiatique the Riverfront How do you describe a restaurant where everything is so delicious that everyone puts their phone down  stops talking and eats … until everything is gone and then looks at each other… « like wow »
That is the magic of Siam India.
Don’t hesitate. Go. Eat. Enjoy.
We had : Pani puri  vegetable samosa peshwari naan  roti  palak paneer  chicken biriyani and raita. Siam India Asiatique the Riverfront Food was good 👍🏻 but the  owner itself was taking the part while serving asking if we need anything which was overwhelming i loved 🥰 this gesture Siam India Asiatique the Riverfront  Siam India Asiatique the Riverfront Wonderful food!  Great staff! Siam India Asiatique the Riverfront Food was good and the 2 curries very different and seemed freshly cooked. The butter chicken had only small amount of chicken though.
Finally I did not like being served on plastic plates.
For a change of food style in Bangkok it’s a good option.

restaurant name: Skyline at Avani+ Riverside Bangkok Hotel
text:
Skyline at Avani+ Riverside Bangkok Hotel Today we ate at the hotel. Every dish was delicious. After we finished eating  we went up to the pool floor to have a drink and enjoy the view of Bangkok. Every dish was delicious. I will come back again. Skyline at Avani+ Riverside Bangkok Hotel Skyline Restaurant is located on the 11th floor of the Avani+ Hotel.
Price: 1 990++ THB per person.

The food selection is quite limited compared to the price. The highlight is the Phuket lobster on ice (to be honest  it wasn't very good; one per person is enough. It would be better if they offered a hot kitchen option  but given the restaurant's location  that's not ideal). Other items include fresh oysters  shrimp  and a Japanese food station  but on the day I went  there was only salmon sashimi  tuna  and small sweet shrimp; no hamachi (which at this price point should be available). The only freshly cooked items were pho and teppanyaki skewers.

The atmosphere is nice  with a beautiful river view in the evening  perfect for photos.
There weren't many staff members  but the service was adequate. You might need to ask for things like drinks  tissues  and table clearing occasionally.

In summary  if you don't have any promotions or discounts  the price is overpriced. The view might compensate for it  but the breakfast buffet is better than dinner  offering more variety. The egg dishes were delicious. However  personally  I wouldn't choose to come back (unless I'm staying at the hotel and need breakfast). Skyline at Avani+ Riverside Bangkok Hotel The buffet is available on Fridays and Saturdays from 6 PM to 10 PM.
It's on the 11th floor and walk-ins are welcome.
When I went  the staff recommended:
Booking through Eating  which offered a buy-one-get-one-free promotion  cost 1999 THB per person. The total bill including tax and service charge would be around 2500 THB.
Luckily  I almost booked through Hungry Hub  which was 1699 THB per person.

The food includes Thai  Western  and Japanese options.
The Japanese food mainly consists of sashimi and miso soup.
The Western options include steak and bread  ham  and cheese.
There's also fruit  desserts  honey  and seafood.
My mom ate three plates of Pad Thai.
But what surprised me was that the first bowl was delicious  the second bowl started to taste less good  but not by much.
The third bowl was soggy and inedible. Very confusing.
Maybe the longer you stir-fry in the pan  the worse it tastes  haha.
P.S. It rained heavily the day I went  and the windows were foggy.
So I don't have any pictures of the view from the restaurant. Skyline at Avani+ Riverside Bangkok Hotel Review of Skyline at Avani Hotels: A Bright Breakfast Experience

Skyline at Avani Hotels is a fantastic spot for breakfast that truly sets the tone for a great day. The restaurant is flooded with natural light  creating a warm and inviting atmosphere that makes dining a pleasure.

The food is excellent  offering a well-balanced menu that caters to various tastes and preferences. Whether you’re in the mood for a hearty meal or a lighter option  there’s something for everyone to enjoy. The quality of the ingredients is evident  and each dish is thoughtfully prepared.

The team at Skyline is friendly and attentive  ensuring that your dining experience is smooth and enjoyable. Their commitment to service enhances the overall experience  making you feel welcome and well taken care of.

Overall  I highly recommend Skyline for a delightful breakfast experience. With its bright ambiance  delicious food  and great service  it’s a wonderful way to start your day at Avani Hotels! Skyline at Avani+ Riverside Bangkok Hotel The view is stunning. Every table and every corner is beautiful.
Some of the food is good  but the ingredients are not outstanding  making some dishes seem unworthy of their price.
The desserts were not impressive  but edible.

Skyview Hotel Sukhumvit Soi 24 Staycation  Skyview Hotel Sukhumvit Soi 24 Staycation An excellent hotel in a fantastic location. It's super close to two large shopping malls and the BTS station. The rooms are brand-new  clean  and spacious enough for a multi-day stay. The gym and swimming pool are fairly basic  but they are well-maintained and large enough to satisfy most guests. Definitely a wonderful and satisfying stay overall. Skyview Hotel Sukhumvit Soi 24 Staycation  Skyview Hotel Sukhumvit Soi 24 Staycation Beware of fake positive reviews

When we arrived to check in  we were told that they had a “gift” just for us. The gift was that we could access our hotel room before the official check-in time if we immediately left a 5/5 review on TripAdvisor (it was 13:30  and the normal check-in time is 14:00). The receptionist gave me a note with the names of everyone working at the front desk at the time (Aim  Ps  and Saiaiw) and asked me to mention these names in the review. I was also clearly instructed not to mention any of this in the review and told that this “special offer” was only for us.

I was quite taken aback  but we were exhausted after a long journey and just wanted access to our room  so I did as she asked. This meant I had to write the review right there at the reception desk  without even knowing whether the hotel was actually good  as we had not even checked in yet.

Before I was given the room keys  I had to show her that the review had been posted  and she took a photo of the review with her mobile phone. This incident was extremely unprofessional and completely ruined our overall impression of the hotel.

As for the hotel itself  it is decent but does not feel like a 5-star hotel. We did not receive a welcome drink upon arrival  which we have received at every other hotel in Thailand we have stayed at (both 4- and 5-star).

The pool area is far too small for the number of guests who want to use it. While we were at the pool  we were drinking our own bottled water  and the bartender came over and told us that outside drinks were not allowed. This also seems like a strange policy when we are paying to stay at and spend time on the hotel’s premises.

In the evening  when we wanted to visit the sky bar  we were told that we could not go up because it was fully booked with guests from outside the hotel. The hotel should have reserved areas for its own guests with priority access. We were not even allowed to go up just to see the view.

On a positive note  the bed is very comfortable  and we slept well every night. Unfortunately  the service completely ruins the hotel experience. Skyview Hotel Sukhumvit Soi 24 Staycation Hotel close to the main BTS station and numerous shops. Rooms: large  clean  and meticulously cleaned daily. Staff: friendly  helpful  and attentive  as well as highly professional.
Breakfast: wide selection (Asian  European)  quality buffet food  and eggs cooked fresh to order. Two restaurants  but not tried. Rooftop Bar (38th floor): beautiful city views with a bar and a very friendly atmosphere. (Not accessible for people with reduced mobility  as the elevator only goes up to the 35th floor.) Pool and Gym (8th floor): very pleasant pool with a terrace and bar  well-equipped gym (cardio and weight training).
Another bar (35th floor): visited but not tried (the rooftop was too good)  very friendly and pleasant.

At Isan...Where "spicy" meets "Japanese sophistication"

If you think all Isan food is the same  you should try At Isan on Sukhumvit 33/1. This isn't just a restaurant; it elevates papaya salad and grilled chicken to an international level of refinement.
Why should you come?

The perfect blend of flavors... Authentic Isan recipes from Khun Pae  blended to create a harmonious taste by her Japanese husband  Keiichi. The result is a deliciously "smooth" flavor—spicy but not overpowering  you can eat it continuously without getting bored.

Highlight menu items... Crispy-skinned  juicy grilled chicken  and the refreshing pomelo and salmon salad that you'll want to order seconds of.

Great vibe. The restaurant has a warm  home-away-from-home feel. Very easy to get to  just a few steps from BTS Phrom Phong.

Recommendation: "Premium Isan food at affordable Sukhumvit prices  with warm service like eating at a friend's house." Sompohnpak Isaan So Good So good! I went during offpeak hours and they are still pretty busy. Basically every table taken downstairs plus clearly some upstairs too. Friendly  fast service and delicious food. I really enjoyed!

Somtum Der Tha Tien An additional 17% tax is charged.
This was the worst experience.
The interior is small  but the sound echoes like a market. I couldn't tell if the rice was going into my ears  and I vomited after eating it all.
The food was as salty as I expected  but the corn ssamtam was salty  like eating a lump of salt. Maybe it was because of the salty egg  but the ssamtam tasted salty overall. As a ssamtam lover  I had high expectations  but this place had the best Zero Coke. 😭 Somtum Der Tha Tien Finding Somtum Der in the quiet alleys of Tha Tien felt like discovering a stylish secret tucked away from the chaotic tourist energy of the Grand Palace. The restaurant manages to strike that rare balance between a polished  modern aesthetic and the raw  unpretentious soul of Northeastern (Isan) cooking. Walking in  you’re greeted by warm red tones and a buzz of energy that makes you feel like you’ve found the "cool" spot in the neighborhood.

The food itself is an emotional rollercoaster for the palate. I started with their signature Tum Thai  and it was an immediate wake-up call—the crunch of the green papaya against the sharp  citrusy lime and the slow burn of Thai chilies felt incredibly authentic. It didn’t feel "watered down" for travelers; it tasted like a home-cooked meal from the countryside  served on a beautiful plate. The Moo Ping Kati Sod (grilled pork skewers marinated in coconut milk) was the standout for me. The meat was so tender it practically melted  and that hint of coconut sweetness offered a much-needed hug after the fire of the papaya salad.

What kept me from giving it a perfect 5/5 was the reality of the space and the price. The dining area is quite intimate (some might say "cramped")  and during peak hours  it can feel a bit rushed. You’re also paying a "Michelin-recommended" premium; while the quality is undeniable  you can certainly find similar flavors on a street corner for a fraction of the price. However  the hygiene  the attentive service  and the comfort of an air-conditioned retreat make that extra cost feel like a fair trade-off.

Ultimately  Somtum Der is a place of beautiful contrasts. It’s where you go when you want the bold  spicy heart of Isan food without sacrificing the comfort of a curated dining experience. Leaving the restaurant and walking back out toward the river  I felt satisfied and genuinely impressed. It’s a 4/5 experience that I’d recommend to anyone who wants a "safe" entry point into the wild  spicy world of authentic Thai flavors. Somtum Der Tha Tien  Somtum Der Tha Tien Very expensive compared to other restaurants. On top of the prices  there is an additional 17% service charge and tax. This means that in the end you will pay almost three times more than you would at most other restaurants in Bangkok. I paid 500 baht for one shake  one soup  and one rice dish  which is simply unreasonable for what you get. The food is average  nothing special  and the service was a bit slow. The soup was extremely salty and full of herbs and roots that were not edible  which made it unpleasant to eat. It was also quite noisy because of the loud music and the staff talking out loud. I’m giving two stars instead of one because the staff is nice. However  I honestly would not come back. It felt like a robbery  and there is no justification for that. Somtum Der Tha Tien After strolling around the Grand Palace area  I stopped by Somtum Der Tha Tien. As a small restaurant with a Michelin-recommended sign  the overall experience was mixed. The basil rice I ordered was decent—the minced meat was reasonably fragrant  the basil and chili were flavorful  and the fried egg was cooked perfectly. However  the overall seasoning was conservative  lacking the impactful flavor of typical street-style Thai food. It felt like it had been adjusted to cater to tourist tastes. What was slightly off-putting was that despite looking like a typical street-side eatery  they charged a 10% service charge  which was somewhat unexpected.

However! I must mention their 360-degree observation deck on the fifth floor—completely free! The view is amazing. On one side  you can clearly see the golden roof of Wat Pho  and on the other  Wat Arun stands on the opposite bank of the Chao Phraya River. Especially at dusk  the sunset casts its glow on the ancient temples  making every photo postcard-perfect. It might not be worth it just for a meal  but combining dining with a scenic hike makes for a unique Bangkok experience. I recommend avoiding peak meal times; coming up to enjoy the breeze and take photos is the real hidden gem of this place!

Sorsi & Morsi The food was absolutely delicious! The ingredients tasted very fresh . My favorite part was the heart-shaped cake .I highly recommend this place and would definitely come back again . Sorsi & Morsi  Sorsi & Morsi An unforgettable Italian dinner at Sorsi & Morsi in Thonglor.

From the moment we sat down  everything felt warm  welcoming and genuinely Italian. Even the bread basket set the tone. Fresh  warm and inviting  served with silky butter  it was simple but done right. The kind of detail that tells you the kitchen cares.

The homemade burrata was outstanding. Creamy  delicate and beautifully balanced with ripe tomatoes and olive oil. It tasted fresh  clean and indulgent without being heavy. Easily one of the best burratas I’ve had outside of Italy.

The chicken liver pâté was spectacular. Smooth  rich and deeply flavourful  paired perfectly with toasted bread. It had that perfect balance of sweetness and savoury depth that makes you pause for a second after the first bite.

The seafood linguine wrapped in parchment paper was a highlight. When opened  the aroma alone was intoxicating. The pasta was cooked perfectly al dente and soaked up all the briny  tomato-kissed seafood juices. Mussels  clams and seafood were fresh and generous. It felt rustic yet refined.

The rib eye steak was beautifully done  tender and flavourful. What elevated it was the addition of balsamic and 24-month Parmigiana  which cut through the richness and brought balance to the dish. Every bite had depth without feeling overwhelming.

And then came the cannoli. Constructed tableside for us  which made the experience even more special. Crisp shell  lightly sweetened ricotta filling and just the right texture. Authentic  elegant and not overly sugary. A perfect ending.

You can tell the chef is Italian. There’s authenticity in the flavours and confidence in the execution. Nothing felt gimmicky. Just honest  well-crafted Italian food.

Service was impeccable. Flavio and Anna were attentive without being intrusive. They took the time to explain every dish  changed our plates between courses and made sure everything flowed seamlessly. It elevated the entire experience.

Sorsi & Morsi delivers not just a meal  but a proper Italian dining experience in Bangkok. Warm hospitality  excellent ingredients and thoughtful execution. I will absolutely be back. Sorsi & Morsi A wonderful restaurant  excellent both in its selection of dishes and the quality and care taken with all the ingredients. I ate a very delicate and fresh sea bass. The atmosphere is warm and elegant  complemented by a Thai/Neapolitan kindness. Sorsi & Morsi The food was excellent. I personally enjoyed the seafood pasta  ribeye steak  and lamb—especially the lamb sauce  which was outstanding. The prices are reasonable  and the restaurant has a great vibe. Highly recommended.

Initially  we found this place because we were looking for tiramisu in the area. What better place than an Italian restaurant? I'm not a fan of tiramisu but I'm a fan of this particular one. More balance than a gymnast — perfect 10 points from me. It's non-alcoholic so maybe order a shot of Grappa or whiskey to go with it. I'd pass on the unremarkable panna cotta though. Spaghetti - Italian Food Our Italian dinner was a masterclass in exceptional service and mouth-watering cuisine. From the moment I stepped into the restaurant  I was greeted by the charming Guest Relations Manager  Drex  who welcomed me with a warm smile and a witty quip about leaving work stress at the door - it instantly put a grin on my face and set the tone for an unforgettable evening.

Our waitress  Amanda  was simply amazing. She was attentive  knowledgeable  and clearly passionate about the menu. She took the time to understand our preferences and offered tailored recommendations that perfectly suited our tastes. Throughout the meal  she checked in with us numerous times  asking about the food and if there was anything else she could get for us. Her genuine care and attention to detail made us feel like we were dining with family.

But the cherry on top was the surprise visit from Operations Manager Sam  who not only ensured everything was to our liking but also brought over a little treat to sweeten the deal. It was these personal touches that truly made our experience special.

If you're looking for a truly Italian experience that will leave you feeling like royalty  look no further! This restaurant has it all - fantastic food  top-notch service  and a warm atmosphere that will leave you wanting more.

My only complaint is about this Google review form - why can’t I give more than 5 stars?!

Recommended dishes:

Spaghetti Mushroom Sausage (Signature dish)
Spaghetti with mushrooms and sausage. The sauce is rich and flavorful  slightly salty but acceptable. Overall  it's delicious and a dish you won't regret ordering. The mushrooms and sausage go well together  making it very enjoyable.

Braised Beef Cheek Steak
The beef cheek is incredibly tender  soft yet still retaining its texture. The accompanying sauce is a perfect match. It feels like a dish made with care  not just a typical steak.

Drinks:

Stella Palace 79th floor at Baiyoke Sky Hotel 79th floor A perfect combination of food for Chinese food lovers. A dim sum buffet with specialties like roasted pork  roasted duck  Peking duck  and sashimi  rice balls  and drinks.

The roasted pork and Peking duck are roasted in-house and served with unlimited changes. The day I went  I changed them 5-6 times. The roast pork was incredibly tender  and the roasted pork was incredibly crispy. It was so satisfying. Buying a whole roasted duck wouldn't be worth it.

They have a full range of Thai dishes  salads  and desserts  with plenty of variety.

They also have seafood  grilled squid  som tam  salads  and soups.

Parking is available on the 6th floor and above. Then  register and pay on the 18th floor  then proceed to the dining room.

After your meal  enjoy the Bangkok view on the 77th floor and the 360-degree revolving floor on the 84th floor.

The staff are very quick and efficient in clearing dishes and tables.

The number of Chinese tourists has decreased significantly  while the number of Thais has increased. Traffic in front of the hotel is congested due to the construction of the skytrain at Ratchaprarop. Suan Bua Centara Grand Ladprao Food as medicine  its a great experience here. The dishes were really flavorful and full of herbs and spices. Great atmosphere too. Great service from staff Ms Na  Ms Green and Mr Leo Suan Bua Centara Grand Ladprao Savor exquisite Thai royal cuisine  the charm of food under the concept of "Food as Medicine" for sustainable health ✨

At Centara Grand at Central Plaza Ladprao Bangkok

Suan Bua Restaurant presents a superb Thai dining experience under the concept of "Food as Medicine " seamlessly blending delicious flavors with health benefits.

👩🏻‍🍳 The core concept of the menu emphasizes the use of natural ingredients and rare Thai herbs to promote optimal health benefits.
- All dishes are prepared without added sugar  using natural stevia as a sweetener.
- Ingredients are sourced from the Sirirukchat Natural Science Park  an organic farm home to over 1 000 plant species.
- Dishes are crafted to retain authentic Thai flavors  rich and well-balanced  with meticulous attention to detail.

✨ A La Carte Menu Highlights:
1. Muan Chottipya Osot (Traditional Thai Herbal Roll)
2. Kiang Lab Pega Kung Lum Nam Chao Phraya (Spicy Shrimp Salad with Pegasus Leaves)
3. Yam Som O Nakhon Chai Si Pu Nim Chanthaburi (Pomelo Salad with Soft-Shell Crab from Chanthaburi)
4. Pla Kapong Sam Nam Tom Som Khamin (Sea Bass in Three Sauces with Turmeric and Orange Broth from Surat Thani)
5. Pork Belly Curry Rolls with Cha Muang Leaves (Chanthaburi Style)
6. Kurobuta Pork Marinated and Grilled with Makhaewan Chili Paste (Nan Style)
7. Chaiya Leaves Stir-fried with Salted Egg and Cold-Pressed Coconut Oil
8. Miang Kham with Ruby Red Tilapia (Lam Ta Khong Style)
9. Panang Curry with Beef Shank (Si Khiew Style)
10. Som Chun (Sour Fruit Salad)

🌾 Rice options:
1. Buriram Volcanic Soil Jasmine Rice
2. Buriram Brown Jasmine Rice
3. Phatthalung Sang Yod Rice

🤵🏻‍♀️ Amenities:
- Private Dining Room
- Indoor and Outdoor Dining Areas
- Warm and Friendly Service

text:

Sushi Hiro Shabu Buffet Central Rama 2 The Tom Yum soup was delicious  and the sashimi was truly fresh. At 941 baht  considering how fresh the ingredients were  it wasn't expensive at all. Almost every dish tasted great. Sushi Hiro Shabu Buffet Central Rama 2 This was my first time at this restaurant  but all my friends had been here before and invited me along. I wanted to try it  and they said the ingredients were good  the taste was good  and it was worth the money. We went with 5 people  divided into 2 groups with separate pots – one group eating beef and one group not eating beef. Each pot had two compartments for the broth. The staff asked about any allergies  which I understand is good practice for informing customers about any allergens in the broth.

The ingredients were fresh and of good quality. There was a wide variety of sushi and other dishes. I liked that the fried items were made to order  including the tempura shrimp  gyoza  and the fried salmon salad – I especially loved that one  it was flavorful and the fish was freshly fried and hot. The staff were attentive and refilled our water without any problems.

For the beef pot  we ordered a beef rice bowl with an egg yolk on top. It looked very appetizing and was served in a square bowl. Seeing that  we also ordered a pork rice bowl. When the staff brought it  they said  "Here's the pork rice bowl." I noticed it was in a round bowl  unlike my friends' which were square. We noticed the difference in the shape of the containers.

But my friend  who was about to put it in her mouth  said  "Why does it look so much like beef?" I confidently said  "That's the round bowl. The square one was beef." But she insisted it looked very much like beef. I jokingly said  "Just eat the pork. If it's beef  you'll know." And when she tasted it  she knew it wasn't pork. We were careless; we didn't think the restaurant would make a mistake because we saw the different containers and we didn't ask the staff beforehand.

Then we called a staff member over and asked  "Is this beef or pork?" The employee immediately replied  "Pork." We believe she said that because she saw the round bowl. We then said  "But it's not pork." The employee quickly said they would check in the kitchen.

Then she came out with a new bowl of pork rice and apologized  saying  "We're sorry  the previous bowl was actually beef." We just said  "It's okay. Please be more careful next time."

Because if the restaurant encountered a very serious customer  they might just eat it without being careful because they trust the restaurant. On the other hand  our friend  who only eats beef and doesn't eat pork because she's Muslim  might have ordered beef rice. Then the restaurant served the pork rice bowl  and after they ate it  I don't even want to think about what happened next.

Our friend  who ate it due to a misunderstanding  simply spit it out and didn't eat any more. They just said  "Never mind." We didn't want to dwell on it because the staff came out and apologized on behalf of the chef/cook.

But I just want to tell the restaurant: please don't let this happen to other customers in the future  because we don't know how accepting or serious other customers will be about this.

restaurant name: THE LOBBY at InterContinental Bangkok Sukhumvit
text:
THE LOBBY at InterContinental Bangkok Sukhumvit 🫖 InterContinental Sukhumvit 59 Lobby – Beautiful Setting  Disconnected Experience

The lobby at InterContinental Bangkok Sukhumvit 59 is beautifully designed — modern  grand  and elegant. It seemed like the perfect place to unwind with afternoon tea before heading to the airport  and so we decided to indulge in their “Winter Tale” Afternoon Tea experience.

Unfortunately  the experience fell short of expectations.

Although the space was quiet with only a few guests idling around  the service staff were surprisingly inattentive  even when we were clearly the only guests actively dining. It was a hot Bangkok afternoon  and basic attentiveness or warmth from the staff would have gone a long way.

🌨️ We were genuinely excited about the “Winter Tale” afternoon tea — the name evokes a seasonal  whimsical experience. Instead  what we received felt generic and disconnected from the theme. There was nothing “winter” about it  and the presentation lacked any storytelling or imaginative flair.

🍴 That said  some items on the menu stood out:
– The truffle brioche was wonderfully aromatic
– The prosciutto crostini (Parma ham) was a savoury highlight
– A number of the other sweet and savoury bites were decent

But then came the biggest letdown: the scone. It tasted like it came straight from the freezer and was reheated in an air fryer — dry  dense  and unfortunately  more like a rock than a proper scone. It really diminished the overall experience.

⸻

✅ What we liked:
•	Elegant and spacious lobby
•	A few standout savoury bites

⚠️ What needs work:
•	Service attentiveness and warmth
•	Thematic execution of the “Winter Tale” concept
•	Scone quality – a major miss for an afternoon tea

⸻

Final thoughts: The setting may be 5-star  but the service and execution didn’t match the ambiance. It was a missed opportunity to impress  especially given the prestige of the InterContinental brand. THE LOBBY at InterContinental Bangkok Sukhumvit The taste wasn't quite to my liking.
The afternoon tea set on the ground floor was delicious  but the desserts weren't great. The scones weren't up to par.

The atmosphere was okay. I visited in the afternoon  and the lighting and shadows were beautiful. However  the lobby on the ground floor was crowded and not very private.

Parking is convenient  no need to circle up the building. It's an automatic elevator. THE LOBBY at InterContinental Bangkok Sukhumvit Try the Afternoon Tea Set at the InterContinental Bangkok Sukhumvit.

Parking is convenient.

Price: THB 1 490++ per set for 2 people. THE LOBBY at InterContinental Bangkok Sukhumvit The Lobby is a small cafe located in front of the hotel. restaurant name: TRIBE TABLE at Tribe Bangkok Hotel
text:
TRIBE TABLE at Tribe Bangkok Hotel We stayed at Hotel Tribe in Bangkok from February 11th to 14th  and it was  without a doubt  one of the worst hotel experiences I've ever had.

The room was extremely noisy. You could hear everything—traffic  hallway noise—making sleeping nearly impossible. As if that wasn't enough  the bathroom was covered in mold. This is not only dirty  but downright unsanitary and scandalous for a hotel that takes itself seriously.

The staff was also extremely unfriendly and completely unhelpful. On my last day  I wanted to have breakfast and politely asked if I could pay partly in cash (baht) and partly by credit card  as I was traveling back to Europe and wanted to use up my cash. The lady at breakfast's response was rude and arrogant. She curtly told me it wasn't possible  without explanation  with no customer service  and with a "just figure it out for yourself" attitude.

And then the absolute worst: I had a pair of boxer shorts laundered and then received a bill for almost €80. This is absurd and completely out of proportion. The prices weren't communicated clearly in advance  and there was no consideration whatsoever for cheaper alternatives. This feels like pure deception.

In short: filthy bathroom  noisy room  arrogant service  and ridiculous extra charges.

I will never return and strongly advise everyone against staying here. Don't waste your money on this hotel. SCAMMERS! TRIBE TABLE at Tribe Bangkok Hotel This was my first time staying here and I was very impressed with the service from the staff. They were lovely  provided excellent service  and answered all my questions willingly. In the evening  when I went up to the pool to enjoy the view  I met a staff member at the bar who asked if I was taking photos and recommended the best time to sit for the beautiful light and atmosphere. The water pressure was amazing! I wish there was hot water though. The toilet was huge and satisfying. The bed was okay  not super soft but not bad either. Overall  I was very impressed with the service. It's a small  quiet hotel  perfect for someone who doesn't socialize much! TRIBE TABLE at Tribe Bangkok Hotel This review is about the food. Taisho-Tei I recently visited Ramen Taisho-Tei  and it was an enjoyable experience. The atmosphere really gives off a Japanese vibe with the warm lighting  bamboo décor  and traditional lanterns that add a cozy  authentic touch. It was quite busy when I was there  which is a good sign that the restaurant is popular  but it also means it can get a bit noisy during peak hours.

The food was satisfying and matched the pictures on the menu  which is always a good sign for me. The ramen was flavorful  the gyoza was nicely pan-fried  and the drinks were refreshing. The prices are acceptable for the portion and quality  making it a good choice for a casual meal.

One thing to note is that the restaurant uses a QR code system for ordering  so there’s limited interaction with the staff unless you need assistance. While this system is efficient  it might feel a bit impersonal if you’re looking for a more traditional dining experience.

Overall  it’s a solid spot for Japanese food lovers looking for an authentic vibe and good ramen. Worth a visit if you’re in the area! Taisho-Tei It's a good place to come on days when there are no events.
