A cool minimalist café in the heart of One Bangkok  featuring both indoor and outdoor seating  and surprising cocoa experiences in every dimension 🤎

The secret menu item we highly recommend trying is their homemade gelato. It's so delicious  you'll want to try every flavor!

With February upon us  indulge in love with their Valentine's Day menu. Every dish features chocolate enhancing the flavors  including:

​💖 Beetroot Quinoa Salad
Awaken your senses with this quinoa salad featuring pickled beetroot and mango salsa. The dish offers a perfect balance of sweet  sour  and spicy flavors  elevating the taste with balsamic dark chocolate  adding a unique and distinctive sweet and creamy dimension.

​💘 Risotto Dark Chocolate
Experience something beyond your expectations with... “Dark Chocolate Risotto” – a creative dish that redefines novelty with a touch of sophistication. The rich  deep texture and fragrant aroma of real chocolate blend perfectly with the crispy fried sweet potato and the refreshing beetroot gel  creating a complex yet harmonious flavor profile to make your meal extra special.

💕 Red And White (Beef & Fish)
A convergence of two contrasting flavors: the freshness of sea bass to the robustness of beef  served with two styles of puree – red bell pepper and wild mushroom. This dish is a testament to culinary techniques that transform contrasting elements into a captivatingly perfect combination.

Or share your love with our unique 🤎 Dubai Chocolate Chewy Ball. Because it's filled with chocolate ganache made from Tempered's own Chocolate Lab  using cocoa beans from Thailand.

Other menu items are also available. If you love All Day Brunch  I recommend 💚Avocado Toast  fragrant with butter  topped with plenty of smashed avocado and melted cheddar cheese.
Or if you're determined to have a serious meal  the Main Course  💛Chocolate Beef Bourguignon  is incredibly rich. The braised beef is infused with Thai cocoa for added richness  served with smooth mashed potatoes—a perfect combination.

For those with a sweet tooth  you shouldn't miss ❤️Scarlet  a white chocolate panna cotta refreshing with cream and raspberry compote  featuring multiple layers of intriguing flavors. Its vibrant red color is full of energy  representing good wishes for your success and abundant positive energy  just like the color Scarlet.

🧡Single Origin Chocolate Gelato that perfectly captures the character of Thai cocoa.

🤍Dirty Chocolate  made with 75% dark chocolate  poured over ice-cold fresh milk. Ten out of ten  a hundred out of a hundred!

Come and experience the unique taste of "chocolate" that you'll fall in love with at Tempered!

Today  I had the opportunity to try their special Chinese New Year + Valentine's Day set  which perfectly blends two cultures: the refined elegance of Europe with a distinct Asian influence. From the taste and presentation to the choice of ingredients  every dish truly reflects the chef's dedication.

⸻

🍝🌶️🍫 Dishes that impressed me from the first bite:

✨ Mala Stir-fried Spaghetti Noodles – A perfect combination of spaghetti and vermicelli noodles  with a delightful chewy texture.
The highlight is the mala chocolate dipping sauce; mildly spicy  fragrant  deep  and multi-dimensional  making it unlike any other. Even those who don't usually like mala will change their minds here.

🔥 Braised Duck Spring Rolls – Braised duck in spring rolls  fragrant with spices and a hint of braising sauce; incredibly enjoyable.

🥗 Quinoa  Beetroot  and Mango Chocolate Gravy – Looks like a dessert  but tastes amazing. But it's all savory dishes  refreshing and perfectly balanced in flavor.

🌟 The highlight has to be the "Chocolate Risotto."
At first glance  I thought it would be sweet  but after tasting it  the savory flavor is very prominent!
It opens with a fragrant  creamy cheese flavor  followed by a delicate chocolate aroma.
Not sweet or cloying at all  easy to eat  yet deeply fragrant and premium.

🐟 The main course  fish and vegetables with sauce — the restaurant recommended it for its outstanding flavor  and I can say it didn't disappoint. The fish was fresh  and the sauce was incredibly flavorful.

⸻

🍞🍰 What makes Tempered unique:

This place is renowned for its serious-level bread  cakes  and chocolate.
The special thing is that many savory dishes have "chocolate as an ingredient " but it doesn't overpower the flavor.
Instead  it adds a distinct dimension of aroma  depth  and luxury to the dish.

✔ European-Asian fusion  seamlessly and creatively executed.
✔ High-quality ingredients  sophisticated flavors.
✔ Chocolate is the heart of the restaurant. Don't miss any of the dishes!
✔ Great atmosphere  comfortable seating  perfect for both cafe hopping and special occasions.

The tom yum fish head soup is amazing  the cooking skills are still the same. The ingredients are fresh  the flavors are good  and the broth is rich. They also have vegetarian options  and you can order dishes without meat. I didn't tell the staff  but they were very attentive  constantly refilling my water and very polite. The air conditioning is comfortably cool. They've added a lot of new dessert menus.
Recommended dishes include: Fish head hot pot  Sizzling pan shrimp  Stir-fried water spinach  Fish maw soup  Braised pork knuckle with mantou  Crab and squid spring rolls  Stir-fried green beans with minced pork (very delicious)  and the stir-fried pork with chili paste is the best. The Bottleman Wine Bar On a lovely evening on Wireless Road  I discovered a hidden gem tucked away on the second floor of a building next to All Seasons Place — Bottleman.

The atmosphere from the moment you step inside is effortlessly luxurious. Warm lighting and a design that exudes privacy and sophistication  it's perfect for a special dinner or a night out to treat yourself.

The highlight that made me fall in love was the wine. They are truly exceptional  with fragrant aromas  a smooth texture  and easy to drink yet full of depth. It's clear the restaurant takes great care in their selection. The food was also exquisitely prepared  with well-balanced flavors. Every dish was detailed  not just beautiful but truly delicious.

Live violin music played softly throughout the meal  creating a European winter ambiance that made time seem to slow down intentionally.

Another impressive aspect was the service. The staff were attentive  polite  and offered professional wine recommendations  making the entire evening incredibly smooth.

Bottleman is a restaurant that isn't flashy. But it has a quiet  classy charm.
If you appreciate fine wine  a great atmosphere  and a touch of specialness in a big city — this is the answer. 🍷

---
Hidden on the 2nd floor beside All Seasons Place on Wireless Road  Bottleman feels like a quiet luxury escape in the city.

Beautifully curated wines (truly exceptional)  refined dishes  and soft live violin in the background create an effortlessly elegant evening.

Impeccable service  intimate atmosphere — understated  but unforgettable. 🍷✨ The Bottleman Wine Bar The restaurant is beautiful  perfect for special occasions. I will definitely come back again.

The Continent Hotel Staycation Nice location   very friendly front desk. The Continent Hotel Staycation A hotel in a perfect location with very friendly staff. The breakfast was a real surprise; it was fantastic.

The Continent Hotel Staycation Modern hotel  good area with access to BTS  nice rooms especially the panoramic ones on the top floors  rooftop pool  bar and Spa. I recommend! The Continent Hotel Staycation The Continent Hotel Sukhumvit / Asok BTS Bangkok boasts an excellent location in the Asok area  just steps from both the BTS Asok and MRT Sukhumvit stations. The surrounding area is full of restaurants  cafes  and shops  making it perfectly situated in the heart of Bangkok's vibrant city life.

The hotel features a modern and elegant design. We stayed in a Continent Room  which was well-proportioned  spacious  and comfortable.
The highlight was the large  floor-to-ceiling window offering stunning city views  including the BTS Skytrain. The amenities were complete and practical; you didn't need to bring anything extra.

Another highlight we loved was the hotel's food and bar areas  offering a variety of styles and each with its own unique and beautiful city views.

🍹 Axis & Spin (38th Floor):
A bar lounge perfect for chilling  sipping cocktails  and enjoying international fusion cuisine. Their cocktails are unique  using local herbs and spices  each named after stars in the galaxy – the idea and presentation are impressive.

🍸 Bangkok Heightz Rooftop Restaurant & Bar (39th Floor):
Serving flavorful Thai fusion cuisine  the atmosphere in the evening is fantastic  offering stunning sunset views and panoramic views of Bangkok. It was a meal that was both filling and delightful.

📸 For those who prefer a more relaxed daytime experience  there's the H2O Pool Bar on the 37th floor  where you can order refreshing drinks  relax  and enjoy unobstructed panoramic city views.

*** I have to mention the breakfast  because it truly exceeded expectations.
The food selection was incredibly diverse  including fresh vegetables  fruits  yogurt  and healthy options for health-conscious individuals.
Or  if you want a more substantial meal  there's a huge selection of hot dishes  savory options  bread  and pastries. What I loved most was that you can enjoy your meal until noon  perfect for late risers like me.

Overall  this is a centrally located hotel that has it all: a great location  comfortable rooms  excellent restaurants  and a fantastic atmosphere  especially during sunset. I highly recommend experiencing it at least once. The Continent Hotel Staycation Overall it’s fine but given the poor condition of amenities it’s overpriced.

Pros:
- great location

Cons:
- Pool and bar are depressing and in need of renovation. Didn’t use it once.
- gym is tiny and smelled very badly of fried food each time I used it.
- first room I had smelled of smoke. Then it took them 30 mins to get the next room ready. I’d been travelling for 18 hours. They don’t even offer me a drink.

restaurant name: The Courtyard At Pavilion Hotel Kuala Lumpur
text:
The Courtyard At Pavilion Hotel Kuala Lumpur With its prime location at Pavilion KL  I expected a certain standard. Unfortunately  the buffet fell far below expectations.

At over RM200 per person  the food lacked refinement  flavor  and consistency. Nothing on the spread impressed me  all taste cheap and even the desserts were underwhelming. The fruits were the only acceptable items.

For a hotel of this positioning  the quality control needs serious improvement. The Courtyard At Pavilion Hotel Kuala Lumpur  The Courtyard At Pavilion Hotel Kuala Lumpur Service was great as was the atmosphere. But food was really quite pathetic unfortunately. We arrived at 1 pm and the udang curry had only a few pieces left. But it was not replenished for the next hour. The flower crab was just salty and tasteless. No fresh prawns. Most of the cooked food looked doused in sauces which I didn't bother to try. We had the seniors' discount at 50% but even then I don't think it was worth it. The Courtyard At Pavilion Hotel Kuala Lumpur Lamb shepherd pie has very strong mutton smell  spaghetti very blend not well seasoned  shell seafood like balitung order to cook kam heong  totally fail  not fresh got sand  every balitung can't suck out the meat to eat mayb the way the shell was cut not proper  its not proper kam heong style  just kam heong soup gravy & the shell seafood soak in it. Grill seafood so so. Some chef not responsive to answer when customer ask about the food. Certain food no label. A pax RM198 not worth it  but our company have 50% discounts per pax due to hotel membership with reward points. The Courtyard At Pavilion Hotel Kuala Lumpur Sunday we went for a buffet lunch at Courtyard Pavilion Hotel Kuala Lumpur but has been told that only 15 minutes left by the receptionist. Even though that the receptionist still welcome us.

Due to only left little time so that the staff help to choose their delicious food for us. Appreciated for their services.

The food were served like salmon with the lemon sauce  bbq prawns  flower crab with butter sauce  mutton kurma  smoked duck  dessert like sweet corn soup  nyonya kuih were nice.

At last the environment of the restaurant is clean  neat and cosy.

restaurant name: The Emerald Coffee Shop  | The Emerald Hotel
text:
The Emerald Coffee Shop  | The Emerald Hotel I intended to try the bluefin tuna  but... well  the first serving wasn't very tasty; it was sour and poorly sliced. The sashimi section had beautifully sliced ​​fish  but the fish itself wasn't good.
The main dishes were delicious  almost every section was good. The seafood and ham were the least good  but I didn't try the papaya salad or spicy salad.
The desserts were good; I liked the macarons and the Thai coconut pancakes.
I tried the sashimi a second time... haha  it was covered in blood. The Emerald Coffee Shop  | The Emerald Hotel The Emerald Hotel: This Valentine's Day...Transform an Ordinary Meal
Into a Special Date at The Emerald Coffee Shop!

If you're looking for a romantic dinner with a great atmosphere to show off to your partner 
or want to celebrate your love in style 
then you must head to The Emerald Coffee Shop
on the lobby floor of The Emerald Hotel. This year  they're going all out
with a spectacular international buffet at a price that will make you say "Wow!"
Book for two for only 3 199 THB!

Start your culinary adventure with a feast of seafood. They've brought the ocean to land!
Unlimited servings of must-try items include fresh oysters 
succulent blue crab  succulent grilled river prawns  and jumbo New Zealand mussels.
Dip them in their delicious seafood sauce – it's more than worth it!

A paradise for Japanese food lovers!
Sashimi enthusiasts  get ready! They have Bluefin Tuna  the queen of the sea 
with its juicy  sweet flesh  and fresh salmon sashimi. Enjoy unlimited servings  including
Foie Gras Sushi and Wagyu Sushi  flame-seared for a fragrant  melt-in-your-mouth experience.
These are normally very expensive at other restaurants  but they're included in the buffet here.

A complete selection of both Thai and international dishes.
In addition to premium items  there are legendary Thai dishes that the hotel is famous for 
such as their signature Braised Pork Leg  a must-order for everyone 
and delicious Sea Bass Congee and Char Siu Noodles. For those who prefer Western cuisine 
there's also a pasta corner with freshly prepared dishes.

Everything is perfectly balanced – the atmosphere and the food are delicious.
Add a touch of sweetness with live violin music playing softly in the background 
along with Valentine's Day drinks  and to end your date 
a feast of desserts  cakes  and beautifully arranged fruits. The Emerald Coffee Shop  | The Emerald Hotel The food was mediocre for the price  but the worst part was the soft drinks available in the buffet. The soda was all gone (or very little was available)  replaced by awful  tasteless  sugary drinks. When I called a staff member to inform them  they said many customers had complained  but they couldn't replace the soda. There was no solution offered (they just said they were aware  but replacing it would be too complicated). So I had to endure drinking these terrible soft drinks. There were no other drinks available  nothing to substitute except for popular branded sugary drinks that didn't contain soda. The Emerald Coffee Shop  | The Emerald Hotel The restaurant is clean  and the staff constantly clears plates.
The food is fresh and delicious.
The seafood  oysters  and river prawns are very fresh. The sashimi is also fresh and the slices are large.
The foie gras sushi is delicious  but I wish they would reduce the amount of rice in the sushi. The tempura shrimp is well-fried  not just a few pieces of shrimp.
The beef noodle soup was a little too salty  but the wagyu beef was tender.
The lamb was tender with a sweet and sour European-style sauce.
The spaghetti with cream sauce was a bit watery  almost bland.
The desserts are also delicious  especially the chocolate macarons. The Emerald Coffee Shop  | The Emerald Hotel The international buffet has a huge variety of food  including seafood  spaghetti  steak  desserts  Thai food  sushi  and sashimi. Everything tasted great. There were plenty of staff providing excellent service. I ate until I was completely full! The price is a little on the expensive side. The Kinopolis The Kinopilis is a fine-dining restaurant with a relaxed atmosphere. All the food is delicious  with easy-to-understand Japanese fusion flavors. The restaurant uses high-quality ingredients  the two chefs are lovely and friendly  and importantly  it's located on the Thonburi side of the city  avoiding traffic and parking difficulties. It's very convenient. If you like this style of cuisine  I highly recommend it. The Kinopolis The Kinopolis offers a beautifully considered dining experience where Japanese ingredients meet Italian technique with precision and restraint. Each course is delicately composed  balanced in flavour  and presented with a sense of quiet confidence that reflects thoughtful craftsmanship rather than excess.

Standout dishes for me included the Caviar & Classic Blinis  the Valoute of Hokkaido Corn and Seafood  and the Crispy Madai à la Beurre Blanc — all elegant  nuanced  and harmonious. The Madai  in particular  was exceptional  highlighting the natural sweetness of the fish while remaining light and refined.

Dessert was equally impressive. The Grandma Chocolate Orange Mousse stood out for its layered textures and balanced flavours — smooth chocolate mousse  a bright orange jelly at the centre  and a crisp crumble that added contrast. The interplay of citrus acidity  gentle sweetness  and subtle bitterness made it complex yet approachable  far from overly rich or bland.

Overall  The Kinopolis delivers a refined Italian-Japanese fine dining experience with excellent ingredient sourcing  careful technique  and a calm  intimate atmosphere. A place I would gladly return to.

We had a fantastic evening with The Planet Cruise. From start to finish  the experience was well-organised and genuinely enjoyable. Cruising along the Chao Phraya at night  with iconic landmarks beautifully lit up  made for an unforgettable backdrop — from Wat Arun to ICONSIAM and the stunning bridges along the river.

The buffet spread was generous and well-presented  with plenty of variety to suit different tastes. Food stations were efficiently managed despite the crowd  and the crew kept things moving smoothly. The atmosphere onboard was lively yet comfortable  enhanced by entertaining live performances that added energy without overpowering the experience.

Yes  the queue and waiting to board the cruise can feel a little chaotic  especially during peak hours  but once you’re onboard  it’s absolutely worth the wait. The views  food  and overall experience more than make up for it.

What really stood out was the overall ambience — great lighting  vibrant colours  and panoramic views that made the whole evening feel festive and special. It’s a lovely way to experience Bangkok at night  whether you’re celebrating something or simply soaking in the city from the river.

Highly recommended for visitors and locals alike looking for a memorable night cruise with good food  great views  and a fun atmosphere. The Planet Cruise ⚓️ Real Customer Review: The Planet Cruise Sunset Cruise

The sour fish soup with grouper has large chunks of fish. The chili paste with minced pork and vegetables is flavorful and the vegetables are fresh and clean. The crispy shrimp omelet needs extra chili fish sauce and chili sauce. The shrimp salad rolls have a delicious dipping sauce.
The atmosphere is nice by the water  but if large boats are docked  there might be occasional smells of engine oil. The Siam Tea Room (Asiatique the Riverfront Branch) Disappointing Attitude from Supervisor

We visited The Siam Tea Room at Asiatique as a group of 4 while waiting for our cruise  with about one hour to spare. We politely asked the waitress if it was possible for us to order one drink to sit down for a while  as we were checking our options due to time constraints.

The waitress was polite and smiled  but when she turned to check with the supervisor  the supervisor looked at us with a very rude and judgmental expression  as if we were being “cheapo.” That reaction was extremely uncomfortable and unprofessional.

To be clear  we were completely fine to order one drink per person. Our question was simply to understand the policy  since:
•	There was no signboard or notice stating a minimum order of one drink per pax
•	No terms and conditions were explained to us beforehand

In the end  we ordered one drink per pax  which was not an issue. The issue was the attitude. If such a request is not allowed  it should be explained politely. A supervisor should never make guests feel looked down upon for asking a reasonable question.

The service attitude from the supervisor was totally unacceptable and left a very bad impression  which is disappointing for a restaurant in such a well-known tourist area. The food is delicious  flavorful  but not overly spicy.
The atmosphere is nice  cute  and has a great vibe.

My favorite dishes:
- Yellow curry: Just the right amount of spice  not too spicy.
- Squid in black ink sauce: This restaurant's version is sweet  which is nice.
- Braised pork: Delicious  I order it every time.
- Stir-fried shrimp with sataw: A must-order.

They also have desserts  and their Southern Thai tea is delicious. Thong Dang Southern Thai Cuisine The restaurant is located in Soi Nawamin 90 and has parking in front. It's a private house-style setting  offering a comfortable  simple  yet stylish atmosphere. The staff provides good service and helpful recommendations. The food and drink menu is limited  but the food comes out quickly  portions are good value for money  and the flavors are excellent  reflecting Southern Thai cuisine. While some adjustments to the flavors might be needed  it's still delicious. The green curry with beef was excellent  with tender  non-chewy beef. The shrimp dishes featured large  firm shrimp. The steamed fish curry had generous chunks of fish  and the coconut milk curry with shrimp and Gnetum leaves was flavorful and delicious. The stir-fried mixed vegetables with herbs and spices were well-balanced; while not perfectly suited to Southern Thai palates  it was still tasty and felt like a home-cooked meal. Highly recommended.

Thong Nua Kao Premium Thai BBQ Crystal Design Center  Thong Nua Kao Premium Thai BBQ Crystal Design Center If you’re craving a next-level moo krata experience with premium cuts  icy air-con  and a vibe that’s both chill and celebratory  ทองเนื้อเก้า สาขาเลียบทางด่วนรามอินทรา delivers in spades. Located inside the Crystal Design Center (CDC)  this branch is spacious  stylish  and open late—perfect for long dinners and late-night cravings.

🥩 Why It Stands Out
- Premium meats: From juicy pork belly and marbled beef to tender cuts that grill up beautifully  every bite is a reminder that this isn’t your average BBQ joint.
- Flavor-packed dipping sauces: Choose from three signature sauces that range from tangy to fiery—each one elevating the grilled goodness.
- Generous buffet spread: Beyond the meats  there’s a wide variety of Thai-style appetizers  Isaan dishes  and even desserts to round out your meal.

🪑 Atmosphere
The restaurant is well-ventilated with strong exhaust systems  so you can enjoy the smoky flavors without wearing them home. The vibe is lively but not chaotic—great for groups  families  or even a casual date night.

🌟 Final Thought
ทองเนื้อเก้า at CDC isn’t just about grilling—it’s about gathering. It’s where premium ingredients meet local soul  and where every table feels like a celebration. Come hungry  leave happy  and maybe stay a little longer than planned.

restaurant name: Timber & Toast at Le Bali Resort & Spa (Pattaya)
text:
Timber & Toast at Le Bali Resort & Spa (Pattaya) I recently stayed at La Bali Resort in Pattaya and unfortunately  my experience was not satisfactory.

First  there was no safety locker in the room  which made it inconvenient to store valuables securely. There were also no basic amenities provided such as a toothbrush or other essential toiletries.

Another issue was housekeeping. After returning from outside  I placed the sign requesting room service  but the room was not made up even after waiting four to five hours.

Regarding breakfast  there were very limited options for vegetarians  and the overall quality and taste of the food were not very good.

Additionally  the TV in the room was not a smart TV. There was no option to connect a phone  and there was no HDMI port available to connect a laptop either. So  the TV experience in the room was also disappointing.

restaurant name: Top Knot Rooftop Bar & Restaurant at Hotel Once Bangkok
text:
Top Knot Rooftop Bar & Restaurant at Hotel Once Bangkok Top Knot restaurant has a unique charm unlike typical luxury rooftop bars.
Atmosphere (Vibe & Decor)
Feeling: This place doesn't feel "stiff" like a 5-star hotel  but rather classic and cozy  like stepping into a scene from an old movie  with the spiral staircase as a key landmark. Content creators should prepare their memory cards! View: From the 9th floor  you can see the Chao Phraya River bend and Asiatique  along with the Ferris wheel lit up at night. The view here is more "warm" than "spectacular."
Food Taste & Quality
The food here is flavorful and authentically Thai  not bland to please only foreigners. This is especially true for the fusion dishes that mix Thai ingredients.
Must-Try Dishes: * Durian Massaman Curry: If this dish is on the menu  you "must order" it. The durian used has a creamy texture similar to potatoes but much more fragrant  and it pairs perfectly with the massaman curry paste.
Pasta: The noodles are perfectly cooked (Al dente).
The sauce coats the noodles evenly. Not overly sweet  Value for Money:
Price: Compared to other rooftop bars  this place offers "great value." Individual dishes start at around 200-400 baht (excluding service charge & tax).
Promotions: They often have special deals with restaurant booking apps (like Hungry Hub) with free-flow or course menus  which helps keep your budget under control.
Suitable for dates: Perfect for a first date or anniversary celebration because the atmosphere is romantic but not overwhelming.
Photo lovers: You're guaranteed to get beautiful photos from the entrance to the rooftop.
Groups of friends: Great for enjoying drinks and having long conversations after work.
If you go between 5:30 PM and 6:30 PM 
it's the Golden Hour with the most beautiful light. If you book the outdoor area  don't forget to check the weather forecast because if it rains  you'll have to move to the Glasshouse area below. Wr love the Top knot's staffs.

A peaceful rooftop setting with beautiful views of the Chao Phraya River and the Bangkok skyline made for such a relaxing evening. The service was impeccable — attentive  warm  and thoughtful throughout. A perfect spot for a quiet  romantic dinner.

text:
Treat Restaurant at Oakwood Hotel & Residence Bangkok  Treat Restaurant at Oakwood Hotel & Residence Bangkok The room smelled musty  probably because of the bathroom. When showering  the water made a mess  overflowing all over the bathroom floor. It was very wet and the bathtub wasn't properly cleaned. Treat Restaurant at Oakwood Hotel & Residence Bangkok  Treat Restaurant at Oakwood Hotel & Residence Bangkok Beware  you'll be SCAMMED! This accommodation is not pet-friendly; they're thieves!
They charged me 1500 baht for my 3kg Yorkshire Terrier  and when I complained  they told me I could leave because the booking fee wasn't refundable since the cancellation policy was strict.
That's the scam: if you don't pay for your pet  you don't get in  and if you cancel  you lose your money. Outraged by the theft  I told them again that nowhere does it say pets pay 1500 baht  and they said Booking.com has it listed  but it's not visible. I asked if they were messing with me  and they stopped responding. They told me that if I wanted to leave  the door was right there.

The fact is  when you book  nobody tells you that pets have an exorbitant surcharge. Then  when you arrive at check-in  after they've asked for both passports and wasted at least 15 minutes  and after they finally have your room key  they hit you with the pet fee. You have no way out; they're thieves and scammers!

And the room they gave us was the smallest in the hotel  about 4 or 5 square meters  and that was after we complained. Booking.com  as always  washes its hands of the matter and continues to hide information  making them complicit in these scams. Nothing is ever clear on Booking.com  unlike on other platforms that are superior because they don't allow guests to be ripped off. And this hotel isn't even close to being a 4-star hotel; they even lie about that. I don't know if it's even 2 stars  being generous. There are hotels like the IBIS that are excellent and accept pets for free. There was a foul smell in the room  probably from the drain  and the neck of the hair dryer was broken  making it difficult to use  but other than that it was comfortable.

Twelve Flavours We enjoyed our meal in this very talented chef’s home. Interesting flavours and the duck which was the star dish was impeccably done. He is very young but I’m sure his repertoire and skills will undoubtedly get even better. One suggestion maybe would be to serve the starter bites together? Twelve Flavours Down to earth  no frills  no pretense  he is who he is  a local guy with self taught skills  same education system  just a father 2 mths ago  with 4 generations living under 1 roof. The community he has  the journey he has been through. I took away more than Twelve Flavours. I took away love  community  kampung spirit  passion  courage and most of all  the agility of the everchanging industry. Thank you Derek Twelve Flavours An Unforgettable Culinary Journey: A 7-Month Wait Worth Every Moment

After counting down the days for seven months  the moment finally arrived for our highly anticipated private dining experience with Chef Derek. Joined by five close friends  we embarked on a gastronomic adventure that surpassed every expectation.

The anticipation was palpable  and as each dish was presented  it was clear that the wait had been a part of the experience  allowing us to savor every moment fully. Each course was a masterpiece of flavor  crafted with an artistry that only Derek could achieve. The way he combined ingredients was nothing short of extraordinary  creating a symphony of taste that left us in awe.

From the first bite to the last  our taste buds were taken on an expedition of unparalleled delights. Every dish was a conversation starter  not only because of its stunning presentation but also because of the complex flavors that danced on our palates. It was evident that each plate was the result of meticulous thought  creativity  and passion.

Dining with Derek is not merely a meal; it's an immersive experience that captivates all the senses. The environment was intimate  adding a personal touch that elevated the evening into something truly special. Sharing this experience with friends made it even more memorable  as we each discovered our own favorite dishes and marveled at Derek's remarkable talent.

When asked if the seven-month wait was worth it  the answer is a resounding yes. It's not just about the food  but the entire journey that makes Derek's private dining a once-in-a-lifetime experience. We left the evening with our hearts and bellies full  already contemplating the next reservation  knowing it will be worth every moment of the wait. Twelve Flavours From the moment the starters were served in this private dining experience  it was clear that this was going to be something truly special.

The food served was the entire star of the night. Each dish was exquisitely presented and masterfully balanced  showcasing simple ingredients with impeccable technique. Flavours were so refined yet bold  with thoughtful progression from course to course. It was clear that every element—from seasoning to plating—had been meticulously thought of.

My family and I absolutely love this whole dining experience  including my two year old nephew who is extremely picky with his food - but can’t seem to have enough of Chef Derek’s food.

Highly recommend booking this. I even booked my next available slot in March for his next menu dining experience. Twelve Flavours Had a birthday dinner here. Delicious on the palette  visual treat on the eyes. Private dining helmed by Chef Derek  winner of the Master Chef Singapore 2021. We love it. Thanks Chef  for the food  and for opening your home.

restaurant name: Up & Above Restaurant and Bar (Rooftop) at The Okura Prestige Bangkok
text:
Up & Above Restaurant and Bar (Rooftop) at The Okura Prestige Bangkok Sometimes  simply sitting quietly  gazing at a high vantage point and the endless skyline  letting conversation flow slowly while sipping warm tea  is a wonderful way to reset the soul. 🤍

Today  we visited Up & Above and enjoyed their “HANABI” themed Afternoon Tea set  which perfectly complemented this experience. Sitting and admiring the view while sipping Marige Frères tea was incredibly reconnecting  like a complete self-reset.

“HANABI” means fireworks  and this set was as beautiful as fireworks blooming in the sky. The colors were soft and delicate  meticulously arranged like a work of art. Each piece  both savory and sweet  was designed to shine  both on the shelf and in our feelings. Paired with Fuji Yama and Marco Polo The Noir teas  it was simply wonderful.

The afternoon light illuminated the teacups  the small pastries beautifully arranged  and the conversation unhurried. Time seems to stand still right there. #Recommended ✨

📌 Up & Above Restaurant and Bar  24th Floor - The Okura Pristige Bangkok
🕒 2 PM – 5 PM daily
💸 2 200++ THB/ 2 people

Vana Nava Water Jungle (Hua Hin) สวนน้ำ วานา นาวา วอเตอร์ จังเกิ้ล หัวหิน (Vana Nava Water Jungle Hua Hin)
วานา นาวา เป็นสวนน้ำยอดนิยมระดับโลกในหัวหินที่มาในคอนเซปต์ "Water Jungle" แห่งแรกของเอเชีย ผสมผสานระหว่างสวนน้ำและป่าเมืองร้อน มีเครื่องเล่นมาตรฐานระดับโลกมากมาย เหมาะสำหรับทั้งกลุ่มเพื่อน คู่รัก และครอบครัว
ข้อมูลสำคัญที่คุณควรรู้ก่อนไปเที่ยวมีดังนี้ครับ:
1. ราคาบัตรเข้าชม (โดยประมาณ)
ราคาอาจมีการเปลี่ยนแปลงตามโปรโมชั่นหรือแพลตฟอร์มที่จอง (เช่น Klook  Traveloka หรือหน้างาน)
ผู้ใหญ่: ประมาณ 1 200 - 1 500 บาท (ส่วนสูง 122 ซม. ขึ้นไป)
เด็ก: ประมาณ 800 บาท (ส่วนสูง 111 - 122 ซม.)
ผู้สูงอายุ: ประมาณ 800 บาท (อายุ 60 ปีขึ้นไป)
เด็กเล็ก: เข้าฟรี (ส่วนสูงต่ำกว่า 111 ซม.)
หมายเหตุ: บัตรมักจะรวมผ้าเช็ดตัวและล็อกเกอร์แล้ว แต่ควรตรวจสอบเงื่อนไขตอนซื้ออีกครั้ง
2. เวลาเปิด-ปิด
เปิดบริการทุกวัน: ปกติเวลา 11:00 - 17:00 น.
ช่วงวันหยุดยาวหรือเทศกาลอาจมีการขยายเวลา ควรเช็คหน้าเพจ Facebook ของสวนน้ำก่อนเดินทาง
3. ไฮไลท์และเครื่องเล่นแนะนำ
The Abyss (อะบิส): สไลเดอร์ยักษ์รูปทรงกรวยขนาดใหญ่ที่สุดในโลก (ที่เป็นสัญลักษณ์ของที่นี่) พาคุณดิ่งลงและเหวี่ยงขึ้นสูง สนุกและเสียวมาก
Boomerango (บูมเมอแรงโก้): สไลเดอร์ที่ยาวที่สุดในประเทศไทย
AquaLoop: สไลเดอร์ตีลังกา 360 องศา ท้าทายแรงโน้มถ่วง
Vana Nava Falls: น้ำตกจำลองที่สูงที่สุดในเอเชีย
โซนสำหรับเด็ก: มีเครื่องเล่นและสระน้ำที่ปลอดภัยสำหรับเด็กเล็ก
4. เคล็ดลับ (Tips) เพื่อความคุ้มค่า
พักที่ Holiday Inn Vana Nava: หากคุณพักที่โรงแรม Holiday Inn Resort Vana Nava Hua Hin (ซึ่งอยู่ติดกัน) คุณจะได้รับสิทธิ์ เข้าสวนน้ำฟรีแบบไม่จำกัดจำนวนครั้ง ตลอดการเข้าพัก ซึ่งมักจะคุ้มกว่าการซื้อบัตรแยกถ้าไปกันหลายคน
การแต่งกาย: ต้องใส่ชุดว่ายน้ำ หรือเสื้อยืด/กางเกงที่ไม่มีกระดุมและซิปโลหะเท่านั้น (เพื่อความปลอดภัยและป้องกันเครื่องเล่นเสียหาย)
อาหารและเครื่องดื่ม: ไม่อนุญาตให้นำอาหารและน้ำดื่มจากภายนอกเข้าไป (ด้านในมี Food Court และบาร์ให้บริการ ใช้ระบบเติมเงินในสายรัดข้อมือ RFID)
มาเช้าดีกว่า: แนะนำให้มาตั้งแต่ช่วงเปิด (11:00 น.) เพื่อจองที่นั่งเปลชายหาดในทำเลดีๆ และคิวเครื่องเล่นยังไม่ยาวมาก
5. การเดินทาง
ตั้งอยู่ที่ตำบลหนองแก ติดถนนเพชรเกษม เยื้องกับซอยหัวหิน 112 (ทางไปเขาตะเกียบ) สามารถสังเกตเห็นสไลเดอร์และตึกโรงแรมสูงๆ ได้ชัดเจน Vana Nava Water Jungle (Hua Hin) Small portions 3 tiny shrimps Vana Nava Water Jungle (Hua Hin)  Vana Nava Water Jungle (Hua Hin)  Vana Nava Water Jungle (Hua Hin)

The ambiance was elegant and inviting  creating a wonderful setting for a special occasion. Food service was prompt when the restaurant was less crowded; however  as more guests arrived  the waiting time for dishes became slightly longer.
Although there were only a few staff members on duty  they were courteous  attentive  and accommodating throughout our visit. Reordering dishes was very convenient and comfortable  allowing us to fully enjoy the buffet experience.
The quality of the food was generally good. Some dishes stood out in flavor  though those prepared with red wine sauce tasted quite similar to one another.
Overall  this was an enjoyable dining experience. I would recommend this buffet to others and would certainly return to dine here again in the future. Vantage Point One Bangkok  Vantage Point One Bangkok Overall  I'd give it an 8/10. The food was amazing! We went for the buffet and paid in advance  and the food was truly excellent  I'd even give it a 9/10. It was totally worth it! Meat lovers should definitely try it. However  I have to deduct points for the service. It was terrible  even awful. The service didn't justify the price. I understand they might not have many staff  but we paid over 1000-2000 baht per person  so for four people it was almost 10 000 baht. We expected better service  but the staff seemed uninterested. They served the wrong items; we told them we didn't order them  and they just checked to make sure we hadn't ordered them. They didn't apologize at all. We went to celebrate a birthday  but there was nothing special about it. LOL. Towards the end of our ordering time  we were confused because other tables got birthday cakes  like what you'd expect in a regular restaurant. We wondered if we should get one too. We asked the staff again  "No birthday cake?" They just said  "Oh  do you want a birthday cake as well?" We were like  "Why did everyone else get one  but we didn't?" The staff seemed busy  just serving and going. We don't know if they forgot about our birthday cake or not. When they finally served it  they didn't apologize or anything  just walked away. Honestly  considering the service and the price  there's urgent room for improvement. The restaurant's ambiance and the service are completely mismatched. However  if you focus solely on the food  it's genuinely delicious  high-quality  and there's a wide variety of dishes on the menu. Vantage Point QSNCC Diverse menu  high-quality ingredients  very delicious.
More Information
Vantage Point Restaurant (Queen Sirikit National Convention Center)
Currently  when Thais talk about Vantage Point  they usually mean the trendy European buffet restaurant:
Founder: Managed by Bistro Asia Co.  Ltd.  a subsidiary of ThaiBev.
Concept and Naming: The word "Vantage Point" means "the point with the best view." The restaurant is named after its location  considered the most beautiful spot in the new Queen Sirikit National Convention Center (QSNCC)  offering a clear panoramic view of Benjakitti Park and the lake in the heart of the city.
Launch History: Launched in early 2023 after the major renovation of the Queen Sirikit National Convention Center  it was intended to be a "European Food Court" bringing together various European cuisines in one place to cater to event attendees and tourists. Vegas Cafe & Gallery The food was delicious  the restaurant atmosphere was great  spacious  with plenty of seating  giving a chill vibe perfect for chatting and listening to music. I loved it! Vegas Cafe & Gallery Vegas’s Cafe & Gallery is not your typical coffee shop — it’s a charming blend of art  nostalgia  and caffeine  perfect for anyone who appreciates a warm flat white and a good dose of Japanese pop culture.

The first thing you’ll notice is the impressive collection of Japanese figurines — from anime icons to detailed collectibles  the space feels like a mini-gallery curated by a passionate collector. Every corner invites a closer look  and it adds a playful  almost museum-like charm to the experience.

Coffee-wise  the flat white is smooth and well-balanced  with a nice depth from quality beans and skilled extraction. It’s clear the barista knows their craft. Pair that with their cinnamon roll — soft  slightly gooey  with just the right hit of spice — and you’ve got the perfect afternoon pairing. Vegas Cafe & Gallery

Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Phet Kasem An amazing experience from start to finish. Excellent service  delicious food  and a wonderful atmosphere ~> Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Phet Kasem Very delicious  good price. Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Phet Kasem A super cozy  homey restaurant with a great variety of drinks. The pasta is delicious  and the steak is surprisingly good as well. They also play nice jazz music  which makes the atmosphere even better. Prices are very reasonable for the quality. Highly recommend! Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Phet Kasem Opening the menu  it seemed reasonable and reasonably priced. We ordered four dishes.
The cheese platter offered a variety of side dishes  including three cheeses  but the flavor was mild and not sharp. I liked the dried strawberries.
The smoked Iberico pork was fragrant and easy to eat  but a bit greasy. It got a bit greasy when the food started to cool.
The sirloin steak was a large piece  but unexpectedly tough. I've been to many restaurants and never encountered this. It seemed like they used a low heat to avoid undercooking. The inside was a deep red  blue-rare  but the outside was cooked crispy and tough. The edges of the meat had membranes  adding a tough texture. Normally  this should be trimmed before cooking. There was a piece of foie gras  but it wasn't charred  so I asked for a flamethrower.
The grilled lamb chops were inexpensive  four pieces for 799 baht  but they weren't cooked to perfection. They were overcooked and had a lot of marinade  but they still had a strong odor. They placed the trimmed edges on the plate  which was quite confusing. They were probably worried it wouldn't be worth it. Some people might like it  but I thought it was too fatty and unappetizing. I just piled it all on the plate.

The mint sauce served with the lamb was very sweet. The steak sauce (probably similar to black pepper BBQ) was overly sweet. The garnishes were almost identical for every dish: pickled cucumber  granulated pink salt (I wonder if they weren't worried about the saltiness!)  pre-ground black pepper  pickled wasabi stalks  and boiled vegetables  but it seemed like they forgot to season it. It was very bland.
I'd like to see improvements. I tried this restaurant because the reviews were good and it's close to my house. Perhaps they've changed chefs or are lacking in monitoring? I tried to inform the staff  but they didn't seem interested in asking any further questions.
The service was polite and up to standard. The restaurant's wine recommendations were interesting. The live music was beautiful and jazzy. Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Phet Kasem A meat restaurant for meat lovers. They also have wine and beer. I recommend you come and try it.

Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Rattanathibet  Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Rattanathibet This area has a bunch of shops all together. It has a fun atmosphere  the food is surprisingly good for the area  and the wine is cheap and you can buy some to take home. Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Rattanathibet  Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Rattanathibet Vela offers a cozy and inviting atmosphere  perfect for a relaxed meal with family or friends. The picanha steak is a standout — tender  juicy  and grilled just right  showcasing quality meat and great flavor. The mushroom soup is also excellent  rich and creamy with a comforting homemade taste.

The spaghetti bolognese leans a bit on the sweet side  which might not suit everyone’s taste  but it’s still well-prepared and enjoyable overall. The combination of good food  pleasant setting  and friendly service makes Vela a solid choice for anyone craving Western-style comfort dishes in the Rattanathibet area. Vela - Low&Slow Cooked Rattanathibet We ordered many dishes and all tasted okay  but the highlight dish  the grilled Black Angus sirloin steak topped with foie gras and caramelized onion  was a huge letdown. Everything on the plate was well-prepared  with a perfectly seared crust and a medium-rare interior  as ordered. Juicy and fragrant with buttery herbs like rosemary  thyme  and garlic. However  the sauce that came with the meat completely killed the beef. It was served as barbecue sauce. Instead of enhancing the flavor and aroma of the meat  it ruined it by using the barbecue aroma and sweet and sour flavors to mask the flavor and fat of the meat. If the sauce were changed to Jus or Gravy  it would not have ruined the flavor of the meat. We waited for this dish for almost 40 minutes  but the barbecue sauce completely ruined the entire dish. It must be eaten without the sauce. If it was with the provided sauce  it would have been a complete disaster.

Real tastes of Italy are offered on the menu.. The truffle pizza is thin and delectable. Enjoy with some savory Italian sausage  refreshing rocket salad and the very delicious ravioli with creamy pink sauce.

Life is short  so don't forget to order dessert. There's Italian tiramisu and a melt-in-your-mouth chocolate lave cake with ice cream.

The food was delicious  our Create your own charcuterie board was great. Stuffed mushrooms were a little bit dry  as were the crostinis. W Omakase Pradit Manutham  W Omakase Pradit Manutham Hold on a minute! 2999+ baht  but the experience was awful. The chef was terrible; their attire was...unfriendly  no smiles. The food was mediocre  there was no presentation of ingredients to make it appealing. The table was dirty and unattended  with ants everywhere. They tried to create a luxury atmosphere  but the materials were substandard. The ambiance was bad  and the glasses used to serve the sea urchin were shocking. Overall  a complete disappointment and a non-returnable experience. They should study other restaurants' standards. W Omakase Pradit Manutham I've been going since the restaurant first opened  but it feels like the portions are getting smaller and smaller. I always pay over 3000 baht per person. The last time  the portions were extremely small  and there were even ants on the desserts! W Omakase Pradit Manutham Overall  the experience felt utterly chaotic. From the moment I stepped through the door until I sat down  I was assaulted by four distinct artificial scents that clashed horribly. The decor was mismatched and incoherent—I’m not even sure if the Christian Dior tableware was authentic. The atmosphere was loud and overwhelming  making it feel like I was eating a Japanese bento box inside a public restroom. Halfway through the meal  someone resembling an assistant chef appeared wearing a Disney character Shirley May headpiece  spewing profanities left and right. It was a complete disaster—truly baffling  as if no one knew what they were doing. The entire dining experience was a mess. W Omakase Pradit Manutham My first omakase experience. It was delicious  especially the cheesecake. The restaurant's decor was lovely  but when I booked for a private group  the last time was 10:00 PM. I asked several times on the official Line account.
But at 9:00 PM  the staff told me the restaurant was closed  as if they were rushing home. I had to ask to stay and take a photo. I was like  "Please!!" We were allowed to stay until around 9:30 PM  but all the staff acted like they were rushing home. This wasn't omakase-quality service. The chef didn't give any explanations or anything  as if they were working from home  even though my friends and I chatted a lot.
But isn't this normal for private rooms?
Is the restaurant underpaying its employees or not paying overtime? If so  the restaurant should have said the closing time was 9:00 PM if they were going to let the employees go home at 10:00 PM. Otherwise  they should have hired them to go home at 11:00 PM  allowing time to clean up.

WINESTONE Mercure on Steven SG Booked the place for a Valentine’s Day celebration with my partner. The service was a mess  there isn’t a clear system on serving. Got the 4 meal Valentine’s Day set. Got served starters twice  corrected them and took them another half an hour to serve the next dish despite reminding them multiple times. The table next to us experienced the same thing too. There is no proper system for service  people who came way later then us are eating their 3 course while we’re still at our first. Don get me wrong  service staffs are great and attended to our request but could clearly tell it’s beyond their control.

Food wise we order the short ribs and the salmon. The salmon was horrible  clearly frozen and wasn’t defrosted properly before cooking. Short Ribs was okay but all the food was quite cold although it took so long to be served. Clearly  the food was already done for a long time before serving.

Really disappointed with the experience  it’s beyond what my words could express. Saw the past reviews were great  perhaps things are better during their normal hours. WYWS Cocktail Bar Good cocktail bar with journey-led stories for each drinks  quiet  cozy  and friendly WYWS Cocktail Bar Hidden away behind an unmarked door in the heart of Bangkok  this speakeasy is a true gem for cocktail lovers. The atmosphere strikes the perfect balance between mystery and sophistication  with dim lighting  smooth jazz  and an intimate vibe that instantly pulls you in.

What truly sets this place apart  though  are the bartenders. They’re not just drink makers — they’re artisans. Every cocktail is crafted with precision  passion  and a deep understanding of flavor balance. Whether it’s a classic Negroni or one of their inventive house creations  each drink feels like a work of art. You can even share your mood or favorite spirit  and they’ll whip up something tailor-made that always hits the spot.

If you’re searching for an unforgettable night in Bangkok  where each sip tells a story  this speakeasy is a must-visit. WYWS Cocktail Bar Amazing vibes and great cocktails. Hidden gem WYWS Cocktail Bar I wasn't disappointed at all! I believe introverts will love it because the atmosphere is truly amazing. It's cozy  comfortable  and warm. All the bartenders are very friendly and nice. I was very impressed :)

P.S. The food is 100% delicious (highly recommended!). WYWS Cocktail Bar If you're looking for a genuine  friendly bar with wine and cocktails  and also some light snacks (I recommend Kyoto Tower; beautiful people eat there 😂)  then this is the place.

Wagyu Volcano Kyoto🇯🇵 Full-bodied Wagyu and Seafood Donburi

Perhaps due to the lack of Chinese tourists  there was no queue.

The most popular dish was probably the A5 Wagyu beef slices covering the entire rice bowl. The slices were so large that multiple plates were needed to hold them all. Slightly grilled  it cost ¥2090.

Each topping cost extra  so the average price per bowl was ¥5000-6000.

We tried salmon roe  sea urchin  and Hokkaido scallops.

The first three items were a popular combination  incredibly fresh and sweet! Each seafood portion was generous.

Personally  I think this full-bodied donburi isn't too expensive at around HK$300.

However  the taste wasn't particularly amazing. We felt the Wagyu beef slices were a bit too raw. They could be cooked in a broth to bring out the aroma of the Wagyu beef. This was a slight drawback.

Highly recommended for photos. Go straight for Valentine's Day  no waiting! Wagyu Volcano 祇園四条エリアの落ち着いた和モダン空間で、肉と米に徹底的にこだわったお店です。

名物の和牛火山は、A4〜A5ランク黒毛和牛を贅沢に使用した看板メニューで、目の前に運ばれてきた瞬間のインパクトがすごい。薄く重ねられた和牛は直火で炙ることで香ばしさが際立ち、噛むほどに甘みのある脂が広がります。そこに雲丹と炙り鰻を重ねると、濃厚さと旨みの層がさらに深まり、中央の卵黄を崩した瞬間に全体がまろやかにまとまる完成度の高い一杯でした。

ご飯は一晩寝かせてから高温で炊き上げる羽釜炊き。粒立ちがよく甘みもしっかり感じられ、肉の旨みを受け止める土台としても優秀。
さらに途中からは薬味で味変、そしてお出汁をかけてお茶漬け風にして楽しめるのも魅力。濃厚な和牛の旨みが出汁と合わさり、最後はさっぱりと締められるので、ボリュームがありながらも最後まで美味しくいただけました！

もう一品の黒毛和牛ステーキ重も、柔らかな肉質と絶妙な焼き加減で満足度が高く、黒毛和牛の旨みが素晴らしかったです。北海道ホタテの甘みもアクセントになり、シンプルながら完成度の高さを感じる一杯でした。

また、グルテンフリー対応やヴィーガンメニュー、ムスリムフレンドリー対応メニューも用意されており、食のスタイルや宗教的な制限がある方でも利用しやすい配慮がされている点も魅力。観光客の多い祇園らしい柔軟な対応力も感じられます。

見た目の華やかさだけでなく、素材・火入れ・ご飯まで抜かりない完成度。祇園でしっかり満足感のある肉ランチ・ディナーを探している人におすすめしたい一軒です。 Wagyu Volcano Wagyu beef waterfall rice bowl!!

restaurant name: Waterfall Lounge at Furama Riverfront Hotel
text:
Waterfall Lounge at Furama Riverfront Hotel The absolute worst eating experience in Singapore. We decided to eat dinner in the hotel. We ordered pasta  soup and salad. The pasta was disgusting  soaking in tomato sauce. The pumpkin soup was just cream with no taste at all. Half of it was spilled on the table by the waiter  which didn’t even notice. Next time it would better to get something from the 7–11 next door. Waterfall Lounge at Furama Riverfront Hotel Very relaxing  great food and just what we wanted and felt like. The lighting is superb and perfect for photos.

I visited for dessert and drinks. Cocktails were reasonably priced  though the quality was questionable. Experienced a white Kir Royale for the first time  likely white wine swapped in for red  which wasn’t what I was expecting. The margarita and whisky sour leaned very spirit-forward  which I enjoyed  but it might not suit everyone’s taste.

On the dessert side  the tiramisu was underwhelming and I have had better at Saizeriya years ago. Coffee and mocktails were decent  though not as well-priced as the cocktails.

Staff gives you space and rarely check in  which paired with the abundance of tables and chairs  creates a truly relaxed atmosphere.

The overall vibe makes up for most of the shortcomings. Waterfall Lounge at Furama Riverfront Hotel  Waterfall Lounge at Furama Riverfront Hotel I (painfully) ordered two cocktails at the bar because the QR code ordering system for non-Club guests wasn't working properly. The first cocktail arrived  but the second didn't... 15 minutes later  I went to the bar to tell them the second cocktail was missing. The same person I ordered from simply replied  "Oh  okay " and started preparing the second  without a word of apology...
This place isn't really a bar (it's the only one in the hotel)  in fact: it's the space for Club guests and various events  but regular customers aren't welcome there  apparently. Shoutout to the Cheshire Cat Diamond for a spectacular performance with great audience work.

I believe the actor for the pocket watch room did not show up (based on the fact that every room should have had an actor and seeing one present in past reviews); while kudos to the other actors who tried to salvage the situation with some improv  the overall performance was not well received.

Storyline was also inconsistent with some actors leaning heavily into the plot while others felt irrelevant and unprepared  with some unnecessary comments on “Alice’s” height and weight.

Will not recommend to go. Wonderland Upside Down Awesome experience Wonderland Upside Down I went with 2 kids for Sunday brunch on 1 Feb.

Food portion was very generous and tasted good for mass produced portions. Think wedding banquet style.

I think the immersion experience could be improved with:
1. Variation of the characters personalities - 4 out of 6 were sassy & sarcastic  the other 2 nice and normal. I chose Une Tranche  a red wine from Beaujolais  France. It offers fresh fruit flavors and aromas like strawberry  cherry  and cranberry. It's a gorgeous wine with a rich depth of flavor. I think it pairs perfectly with the food.

The portions were generous. The penne was delicious. Every dish was exquisite  pairing perfectly with the wine. Paired with a mandarin orange salad  it was especially delicious.

The real surprise here was the dessert menu. The restaurant has a great reputation  so I was looking forward to it  and it truly lived up to my expectations. The brownies were amazing! They were also perfect to pair with the wine.

text:

This hotel is great for couples and families. It's located in a quiet neighborhood  away from the entertainment district. The small pool and small gym are a bit disappointing. Here are the pros and cons I felt during my five-day stay: The room was spacious and clean  with good soundproofing. The shower pressure was decent  but the floor drain was slow to drain. If you let it drain  the water would pool quickly. There were a lot of group package guests  but a lot of them were Korean  so you could hear Korean constantly. Sometimes  it was confusing whether you were even in Korea. There are quite a few Korean restaurants behind the hotel  so if you like Korean food  this hotel will be a good choice. Personally  I was generally satisfied with the price (around 50 000 won). Prices vary depending on the day  so I recommend researching before making a decision.

I had a great time. XSO Kitchen at Best Western Plus Nexen (Pattaya) A reasonably priced hotel. Located in a quiet area. The ladies at reception are very helpful and friendly. Xiao Fuwang Hotpot (Central Westville) Let me preface this by saying this review is solely for the buffet.

I was very impressed with this brand on my first visit.

All the mala dishes were fragrant and delicious. My wife tried the beef oil mala  which is spicier than the clear broth mala. The clear broth has Sichuan peppercorns (green  similar to green peas) floating in it; just sucking on them makes your mouth tingle  and you can easily remove them. The tomato soup 🍅 was fragrant and delicious  and got even better the longer it simmered.

The dipping sauce area had a huge selection  was clean  and well-organized.
They also had delicious pre-made sauces.

The meats – beef neck  beef brisket  beef tongue – were all excellent. The duck intestines  duck blood (highly recommended)  and the homemade pork meatballs were also tasty.

What really stood out were the udon and the homemade noodles. I highly recommend saving room for these two; they're definitely good.

As for dessert  the chocolate was barely sweet at all  like they forgot to add sugar 😂. The hazelnut dessert was ridiculously sweet.
The grass jelly was a forgettable option 🥹

Overall  it's still a highly-rated place. I encourage you to try it. Xiao Fuwang Hotpot (Central Westville) They now offer a buffet  but the variety and value for money aren't quite impressive  especially when compared to other restaurants in the market.

The food is average in taste; tasty but nothing special.

restaurant name: YOK Chinese Restaurant | The Emerald Hotel
text: YOK Chinese Restaurant | The Emerald Hotel  YOK Chinese Restaurant | The Emerald Hotel The food was delicious and the service was excellent. We ate in a private room  which was very private and soundproofed. The Peking duck was as delicious as always. The dim sum was in large baskets with big pieces; it's best to share a variety of dishes if you're coming with a group. The fried taro was also tasty. We tried the Peking duck grilled over a charcoal fire with a spicy dipping sauce – it was unique and the duck meat was tender  although the broth was a bit too thick; the chef might have sliced ​​it incorrectly. Overall  it was a good experience. The restrooms were clean. YOK Chinese Restaurant | The Emerald Hotel The crab fried rice is highly recommended  even if I am full i still can finish by myself YOK Chinese Restaurant | The Emerald Hotel Upon entering the hotel  the Chinese restaurant is located on the mezzanine floor to the right. The food  in general  was decent. We had hairy crab (it was the end of the season  so the crabs were a bit small). YOK Chinese Restaurant | The Emerald Hotel Delicious! The Peking duck was crispy and the pancakes were thin. The shrimp dim sum was excellent. text:

The food is beautifully presented  made with fresh  high-quality ingredients  and reflects authentic Japanese flavors. Yang Gao Gorn  Yang Gao Gorn The curry dishes were too bland.
The chili paste had very few vegetables.
The rice was tasty but clumpy  probably because too much sticky rice was mixed in.
The prices were quite high compared to the portion sizes and the size of the restaurant.
Parking was difficult. Yang Gao Gorn  Yang Gao Gorn Thai food with a well-balanced flavor. I took the adults there and they all liked it. Yang Gao Gorn This is one of the best restaurants I've been in Thailand. The crab and sea blight curry is amazing  as is the crab and coconut dip. Wonderful service as well - so kind. We ate here 3 times on our last trip.

Yanghin Seuton Geulil Rama 4 very delicious Yanghin Seuton Geulil Rama 4 Although Google said they closed at midnight  I arrived at 10:25 PM. The staff informed me that they weren't accepting any more orders because the last order was at 10:30 PM. However  they said I could stay until midnight if I wanted. I thought it would be okay to order extra  as I'd have enough time. When I walked in  there were 2-3 tables that hadn't been cleaned yet; the staff had just left them like that. They only cleaned them after I chose a table. By the time they finished wiping the table and brought the menu  it was already 10:29 PM.

I chose the highest rate set at 598 baht  which included soft drinks. They charged an extra 100 baht for using the stove if you're dining alone (I think this is the only place I've seen charge like this).

Many items on the menu were sold out. If you wanted seaweed  you had to order it separately A la Carte. They're very petty.

The food was served quickly  probably because I was the only one ordering. The food tasted good. I liked the marinated shrimp and marinated salmon; the dipping sauces were good  and the seafood was delicious. The meat was just okay  quite chewy. I liked the spicy squid; you should definitely order that. The steamed egg with shrimp and cheese was excellent. Actually  almost all the cooked dishes were good. Was it worth it? It's more worthwhile than ordering A la Carte if you finish everything. Yanghin Seuton Geulil Rama 4  Yanghin Seuton Geulil Rama 4 The restaurant's exhaust system is well-designed  but the heat distribution through the stones is uneven and doesn't distribute the heat evenly. The 598 baht buffet price was considered poor quality. The only advantage is that it includes soft drinks. The service was decent. It's okay if you're coming for the novelty and to take photos  but if you're looking to enjoy the food  don't expect too much to avoid disappointment. Yanghin Seuton Geulil Rama 4 It was delicious on the first try. Now I'm full.

restaurant name: Ye Lai Xiang 夜来香 Rca
text:
Ye Lai Xiang 夜来香 Rca  Ye Lai Xiang 夜来香 Rca  Ye Lai Xiang 夜来香 Rca They have good  fresh food. Cleanliness is excellent. Recommended. Ye Lai Xiang 夜来香 Rca 🇹🇭Discovered an Incredibly Spicy Chinese Flavor at RCA in Bangkok! The "Night Fragrance" restaurant was amazing! After several days of indulging in Thai food in Bangkok  my "spicy soul" was itching for a bite.

A friend recommended "Night Fragrance (RCA branch) " and it completely raised my expectations for overseas Chinese restaurants. The ambiance was incredibly trendy  and the food was so delicious I wanted to lick my fingers.

😄The industrial style mixed with neon lights makes every corner a perfect spot for a hot photoshoot. The trendy Chinese graffiti on the walls is super cool!

🦆A special mention goes to the braised platter. The three duck wings were incredibly satisfying  the meat firm and soaked in the savory braising sauce. The duck gizzards were bursting with juice  the three duck chins were increasingly flavorful with every chew  and the chicken feet were so tender they practically fell off the bone—perfectly braised with just the right amount of spiciness!

🦐The braised mantis shrimp ordered separately is a delightful surprise! The shells are crispy  the meat is fresh and juicy  and the aroma of the braising sauce permeates every bite – you'll be hooked from the first bite!

🦞The Changsha-style Thai crayfish is incredibly spicy  with a fresh spiciness and a hint of garlic. The shrimp meat is bouncy and plump.

🥟Hunan snacks: Changsha stinky tofu  orchid stems  sugar-coated rice cakes – absolutely amazing!

🐚Garlic vermicelli grilled scallops: The vermicelli soaks up the broth  and the scallop meat is huge.

🐷Liuyang steamed sausage is oily  savory  and fragrant  paired with perfectly cooked fried rice.

🌶️The chili-fried pork is a rice-eating machine; the perfect balance of lean and fatty pork flavor mixed with the aroma of chili instantly brings back memories of home! If you're craving authentic spicy Chinese food in Bangkok  this is the place to go – the ambiance is photogenic  and the food is so delicious you'll be stuffed! I heard that a new branch of a restaurant is opening in Bangkok in November! Looking forward to it! #BangkokNightFragrance #BangkokChineseFood #BangkokHunanCuisine #BangkokFoodRecommendations #HunanCuisineLandmark #HometownTasteThatHunanPeopleCareAbsolutelyDelight Ye Lai Xiang 夜来香 Rca The bullfrogs and chicken didn't seem very fresh.

I asked the owner  and he said they were all fresh ingredients.

If you're blatantly lying  then I have nothing more to say. Yenney Stomach-ache foods Yenney Too much MSG Yenney Visited for the first time and very pleased with the food quality! If you have an Oddle pass  can login to claim your first welcome drink. I ordered homemade lemongrass tea :) - not very sweet! 👍🏻

I really loved the dry noodles and the spring roll is super crispy! Pork is nicely cooked  not too hard and just right.

Loved the service  I came over at 1.50pm and their lady staff saw me and seated me quickly 😀 Loved her enthusiasm and she did not forget to ask me to order a beverage to go with my meal.

I will definitely come here again! Yenney Ordered the beef pho and pork chop rice. The pho was flavourful  but I felt somewhat thirsty after finishing it. The pork chop was slightly on the dry side for my liking. The fried spring roll was decent. Overall  an okay meal. Yenney YenneY Restaurant is really a hidden gem that truly elevates Vietnamese cuisine. We have meals with our guests yesterday dinner. Their French-Vietnamese beef steak is outstanding — beautifully tender  perfectly cooked  and rich with depth  blending classic French technique with authentic Vietnamese flavours in the most refined way.
What truly stole the show is their homemade chilli. It’s incredibly fragrant  well-balanced  and addictive — not just spicy  but layered with flavour and warmth that enhances every bite. You can tell it’s crafted with care and a deep understanding of Vietnamese taste.
Will be back again soon and highly recommended

Yokkrok Zabzone I went to Chiang Mai during the New Year festival in 2025 (Thai year 68) and stayed in the city center  which made it very difficult to find restaurants; everything was fully booked. The receptionist at KM Hotel recommended a restaurant there  saying the food was delicious  the staff were very polite and attentive  and the atmosphere was great and private. I ate dinner there for two nights. Yokkrok Zabzone The food is very delicious. The restaurant is not far from the airport. Stopped by to eat before boarding the flight back home. The restaurant has a good atmosphere. Yokkrok Zabzone The food is delicious. Yokkrok Zabzone The atmosphere is wonderful  the air conditioning is cool and comfortable  and all the food is delicious  but the Korat papaya salad and fried chicken are the best.
The food didn't arrive at the same time  but the wait wasn't too long.

I'm deducting one star because I ordered Korat papaya salad  but the waiter brought me a fruit papaya salad. I told him I didn't order fruit papaya salad. The waiter brought it back and said there are two types of Korat papaya salad: fruit papaya salad and papaya salad with shredded papaya. If there are two types  why didn't they mention it when ordering? I didn't order fruit papaya salad  but I ordered Korat papaya salad.

I also ordered two soft drinks: iced tea and iced espresso. The iced tea had no aroma  completely overpowering the milky flavor  and it felt a bit greasy  with black tea particles mixed into the glass.

Overall  I'd definitely go again if I had the chance. Yokkrok Zabzone

restaurant name: You & I Restaurant and Bar at We Hotel Riverfront
text: You & I Restaurant and Bar at We Hotel Riverfront The atmosphere just fits my kind of vibe. Especially with trees  flowers  and a slow breeze across the river. You & I Restaurant and Bar at We Hotel Riverfront  You & I Restaurant and Bar at We Hotel Riverfront The staff are well-trained and very friendly. The food is delicious and offers good value for money. You & I Restaurant and Bar at We Hotel Riverfront Terrible experience. I ordered food over 40 minutes ago and haven't received anything  plus they didn't even come to tell me what was wrong. When I asked about my order the first time  they said there were a lot of customers  both upstairs and inside. I looked up and saw only 2-3 tables upstairs and 2 tables downstairs  and they were small tables  and they'd already gotten their food. I waited and waited  and when I went to ask  they said they hadn't even made a single dish yet. If you're not ready  don't open a restaurant! It was a waste of time and very frustrating. There were very few customers; don't use "lots of customers" as an excuse. Including our table  there were less than 10 tables. This is my first time here. Serious improvements are needed. Yunnan (Chon buri) Best dinner in Chiang Mai  Best price and great taste Yunnan (Chon buri) Yunnan Restaurant Chiang Mai (ภัตตาคารยูนนาน)
Located on Sridonchai Road near the Chang Khlan intersection  this restaurant is a fascinating culinary crossroads where Yunnanese Chinese traditions meet Burmese (specifically Shan and Kachin) influences. It is widely considered a "hidden gem" for those seeking authentic border-region flavors rather than standard Thai-Chinese fare.
The Burmese-Yunnan Influence
The menu reflects the history of the "Chinese Diaspora" in Northern Thailand and Eastern Myanmar. You’ll notice the heavy use of pickled greens  dried chilies  and specific spices that are staples in both Yunnan and the Shan State of Myanmar.
A unique highlight is the availability of Kachin Rice Wine  a traditional beverage that links the cultural heritage of the Kachin people living across the Yunnan-Myanmar border.
Signature Dishes & What to Order
• Yunnanese Ham & Braised Pork Leg: Often served with pillowy-soft Mantou (steamed buns). The pork is slow-cooked until it’s melt-in-your-mouth tender.
• Mala Stir-fried Potatoes: A simple but addictive dish. The potatoes are thinly sliced and wok-fried with numbing Sichuan peppercorns and dried chilies.
• Stir-fried Fish with Yunnan Chili/Salt: High marks for freshness; the fish or shrimp are typically flash-fried with aromatic herbs and "shrimp head oil" for deep flavor.
• Handmade Dumplings (Jiaozi): These are rustic and thick-skinned  exactly how they are made in home kitchens along the border.
• Pickled Green Stir-fries: A signature flavor profile of the region—tangy  salty  and spicy.
Atmosphere & Service
• Vibe: It is a no-frills  family-run establishment. The decor is simple  clean  and functional—focusing entirely on the food.
• Pricing: Very reasonable (budget-friendly). Portions are generous  making it an excellent choice for groups or families.
• Staff: The owners are known for being very welcoming and can often explain the origins of their more unique ingredients. Yunnan (Chon buri) Authentic and delicious Chinese home-style flavor

While there are many excellent restaurants in Thailand  this is the first time I've given this restaurant a one-star rating.

The food wasn't terrible  but for that price  I felt like I should expect food of at least this standard.

Also  as the name suggests  this is a Japanese-style restaurant  but the appetizers included kimchi  garlic  and wakame seaweed  and even a hot pot sauce mixed with gochujang  which made me wonder if this is a Korean-owned Japanese restaurant. While this can be confusing for customers  it's not particularly bad.

What was most disappointing was the service. No matter how many times I ordered  the food and tableware never arrived  and it took two hours for a meal that could have been eaten in 45 minutes. Having to pay a 10% service charge for this is like paying for oxygen.

That said  it's possible that operations were chaotic due to the high number of customers at the end of the year  or that the order machine just happened to be broken. It took a while for my order to be processed  but I think the staff were doing their job honestly. Please take this as just one opinion. I’m really excited every time I come back to stay and I come back six times a year and I always enjoy the zest bar and the staff in particular Zest Bar & Terrace @ The Westin Grande Sukhumvit I recently visited Zest Bar & Terrace at The Westin Grande Sukhumvit for their seasonal “6 Shades of Mangoes” Afternoon Tea  and it was an absolute delight. The set features a vibrant mix of mango-themed sweet and savory treats—from mango pavlova to mango choux—all beautifully presented on a three-tier stand.

The ambiance is elegant yet relaxed  with a modern lounge setting that makes it perfect for a quiet afternoon escape. I paired my set with lemongrass tea  which added a refreshing balance to the tropical flavors.

The price is THB 1 648 net for two persons  including tea or coffee  and is available daily from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM between April and June 2025.

Whether you’re a mango lover or simply looking for a unique afternoon tea experience in Bangkok  this seasonal offering is well worth a visit.

Highly recommended for both locals and travelers! Zheng Huang Qi Dumpling King  Zheng Huang Qi Dumpling King The most frustrating thing about this restaurant is its extremely inconsistent quality control  especially its signature Guo Bao Rou (sweet and sour pork). Sometimes it's crispy and authentically Northeastern  and other times it's mushy like pork cooked in sweet and sour sauce  resulting in a huge difference in experience and severely impacting repeat business. The barbecue  sizzling beef  and tendon dishes are relatively reliable and have received numerous positive reviews. The portions are generous  and the prices are affordable; you can eat your fill for 30-50 yuan per person  making it good value. A colleague from Northeast China confirmed it has a "hometown flavor " but details like the sauerkraut and cold noodles aren't quite authentic  and the chicken stew with mushrooms has been overly salty on occasion. Service is prone to dropping when busy  with instances of dishes being changed haphazardly  no one offering takeout  and having to order at the counter. The overall atmosphere is down-to-earth  but parking is a bit difficult. It's suitable for those craving something delicious  but if the quality control and service don't improve  it's unlikely to become a consistent choice. It's recommended to avoid peak hours  order carefully  and don't have high expectations for the Guo Bao Rou. Zheng Huang Qi Dumpling King There were many options near the hotel  but none of them appealed to us. This restaurant was packed  had a huge selection  and everything we ordered (skewers  dumplings  and a meat-filled tortilla) was delicious.
They accept credit cards. Great service. I highly recommend it. Zheng Huang Qi Dumpling King Northeast Dumpling King is a Chinese restaurant in Singapore where you can find truly authentic Northeastern Chinese cuisine. The overall experience is excellent  with each bite feeling incredibly genuine. The dumplings are a must-order; the skin is chewy and the filling is generously filled  with a rich and juicy flavor that's incredibly satisfying. The scallion pancakes are crispy on the outside and soft on the inside  bursting with scallion flavor that intensifies with each chew.

The chive boxes are generously portioned  with a non-greasy outer layer and a fragrant chive filling that offers a layered texture. The Northeastern-style cold noodles are also highly recommended—tangy  flavorful  and perfectly seasoned  a quintessential Northeastern home-style dish. The overall flavor profile leans towards authentic  original tastes rather than modern modifications  showcasing the chef's skill.

The prices are affordable  and the portions are generous  making it perfect for gatherings with friends or family. Sharing several dishes is especially satisfying. I ordered the beef burger — an impressive  beefy  thick-cut burger. If you’re into juicy  hearty burgers that feel like a proper meal  this one’s for you. On the other hand  if you’re more into thin  crispy-edged smash burgers  you might want to skip this and look elsewhere on the menu.

I added cheddar cheese for an extra 89 baht  but honestly  it wasn’t worth it. The burger was so thick and flavorful that the cheese got completely lost — I couldn’t really taste it or feel its presence in the texture. It didn’t add much to the overall experience.

The wedge-cut fries were a pleasant surprise — nicely seasoned and perfectly crisp on the outside  soft on the inside.

The service was excellent. The staff went above and beyond — friendly  attentive  and clearly cared about the customer experience.

The only downside was the atmosphere during the time I visited. The rooftop setting definitely has potential  but on a cloudy  hot afternoon  it wasn’t at its best. I imagine the ambiance would be much better after sunset when the lights come on and the temperature drops.
